2:154: And do not say about those who are killed in the way of God that they are (dead. Rather, they are alive, their souls are in the crops of green birds that roam in Paradise wherever they wish, according to a hadith about that but you do not perceive you know what they are in.
2:155: And We will surely test you with something of fear of the enemy and hunger drought and a loss of wealth through destruction and lives through killing, death, and diseases and fruits through plagues, that is, We will test you and see whether you will be patient or not and give good tidings to the patient in the face of affliction with Paradise.
2:156: They are those who, when a calamity befalls them, say, Indeed we belong to God a king and servants, who does with us what He wills and indeed to Him we will return in the Hereafter, so He will reward us. And in the hadith, Whoever seeks refuge in God when a calamity befalls him, God will reward him for it and replace it with something better. And in it, the lamp of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was extinguished, so he sought refuge in God, and Aisha said, This is only a lamp. He said, Everything that a believer does wrong is a calamity. Narrated by Abu Dawud in his Marasil.
2:157: On them are blessings forgiveness from their Lord and mercy grace and it is those who are the [rightly] guided to the truth.
2:158: Indeed, As-Safa and Al-Marwah are two mountains in Mecca are among the symbols of God the signs of His religion, the plural of Sha'irah so whoever makes Hajj to the House or performs 'Umrah that is, undertakes Hajj or 'Umrah, and their origin is intention and visitation there is no blame upon him sin upon him for walking between them in which the ta' in the origin is assimilated into the ta' by walking between them by running between them seven times. It was revealed because the Muslims disliked that because the people of ignorance used to walk between them and they had two idols that they would touch. And from Ibn Abbas that running is not obligatory because it indicates that the sin is removed from the choice. Al-Shafi'i and others said that it is a pillar, and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, explained his obligation by saying: Indeed, God has prescribed running for you narrated by Al-Bayhaqi and others. He said: Begin with what God began with meaning As-Safa. Narrated by Muslim And whoever volunteers and in a reading with a dot and a shaddah on the ta’, in the jussive mood, and in it is the assimilation of the ta’ in it {voluntarily} good meaning with good, meaning he does what is not obligatory upon him of circumambulation and other things then indeed God is Appreciative for his work with reward for it Knowing of it.
2:159: And it was revealed about the Jews: Indeed, those who conceal from the people what We have sent down of clear proofs and guidance such as the verse of stoning and the description of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace after We made it clear to the people in the Scripture the Torah those are cursed by God He distances them from His mercy and cursed by the cursers the angels and the believers or everything by calling upon them to be cursed.
2:160: Except for those who repent return from that and amend their actions and make clear what they concealed for those I will turn to I will accept their repentance and I am the Accepter of Repentance, the Most Merciful to the believers.
2:161: Indeed, those who disbelieved and died while they were disbelievers is a state upon them is the curse of God and of the angels and of mankind, all together meaning they deserve it in this world and the hereafter. And it was said that people are general. And it was said that they are the believers.
2:162: They will abide therein meaning the curse and the fire indicated by it The punishment will not be lightened for them for the blink of an eye nor will they be reprieved they will be given respite for repentance or an excuse
2:163: And when they said, Describe your Lord to us, it was revealed: And your God, the One who deserves to be worshipped by you, is one God, who has no equal in His essence or in His attributes. There is no god but Him, He is the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. And they asked for a sign for that, so it was revealed:
2:164: Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth and what is in them of wonders and the alternation of the night and the day with their coming and going, increase and decrease and the ships the ships that sail upon the sea and do not settle, being heavy with that which benefits people of trade and transportation and what God sends down from the sky of water rain and revives thereby the earth with vegetation after its lifelessness its dryness and disperses separates and spreads thereby therein every kind of moving creature because they grow due to the fertility that comes from it and the directing of the winds their turning south and north, hot and cold and the clouds the clouds controlled subdued by the command of God the Most High, moving to wherever God wills between the heaven and the earth without any connection are signs indicating His Oneness the Most High for a people who reason who reflect.
2:165: And among mankind are those who take for themselves other than God that is, other than Him rivals idols who love them with glorification and submission as they love God that is, as they love Him but those who believe are stronger in love for God than their love for rivals because they do not deviate from Him in any way, and the disbelievers deviate in severity towards God. And if you could but see O Muhammad, you could see those who did wrong by taking rivals when they see in the active and passive form they see the punishment you would see a great matter. And when means if that that is because the power ability and dominance is entirely God's a state and that God is severe in punishment and in the reading {sees} the subject is the pronoun of the listener, and it was said that those who did wrong, so it means knows and that and what follows it filled the place of the two objects and the answer to if is omitted and the meaning is if they knew in this world the severity of God's punishment and that the power belongs to God alone at the time they witness Him, which is on the Day of Resurrection, they would not have taken rivals besides Him.
2:166: When instead of the before it those who were followed disavowed that is, the leaders from those who followed that is, they denied their misguidance and and they had seen the punishment and the causes the connections that were between them in this world of kinship and affection were severed) in apposition to disavowed them from them
2:167: And those who followed said, If only we had another chance to return to this world (so we could disown them that is, those who were followed as they disowned us today. If only is for wishing, and we disown is its answer thus that is, just as He showed them the severity of His punishment and some of them disowned others God will show them their deeds the bad ones as regrets a state of remorse over them, and they will not emerge from the Fire after entering it.
2:168: And it was revealed about those who forbade the free-range animals and the like: O mankind, eat from whatever is on the earth [that is] lawful a state and good an emphatic description, meaning enjoyable and do not follow the footsteps of Satan’s] paths meaning his adornment for indeed, he is to you a clear enemy between enmity
2:169: He only orders you to do evil sin and immorality that which is ugly according to Islamic law and that you say about God what you do not know such as forbidding what He has not forbidden and other things.
2:170: And when it is said to them meaning the disbelievers Follow what God has revealed of monotheism and the permissibility of good things they say No rather we will follow what we found we found our fathers doing of worshipping idols and forbidding the sa'i' and the lakes. God the Almighty said: A They follow them even though their fathers did not understand anything of the matter of religion nor were they guided to the truth and the hamza is for denial
2:171: And the example of those who disbelieved and of those who call them to guidance is like the example of one who calls makes a sound with what he hears only a call and a cry that is, a sound and does not understand its meaning, that is, in hearing the sermon and not contemplating it, like the animals that hear the voice of their shepherd but do not understand it, they are deaf, dumb, and blind, so they do not understand the sermon
2:172: O you who have believed, eat from the good things lawful which We have provided for you and be grateful to God for what He has made lawful for you (if it is Him you worship.
2:173: He has only forbidden you dead animals meaning eating them, since the discussion is about them, and so is what comes after them, which is what is not mentioned in the Shari'ah. The Sunnah included with them what is clear from living things, and fish and locusts were singled out and blood meaning poured out as in livestock and the flesh of swine He singled out meat because it is most of what is intended and other things are subordinate to it and that which is dedicated to other than God meaning slaughtered in the name of someone other than Him. And the call to prayer is raising the voice, and they used to raise it when slaughtering for their gods but whoever is forced meaning necessity forces him to eat something of what was mentioned, so he eats it not desiring going against the Muslims nor transgressing against them by cutting off the road (there is no sin upon him) for eating it Indeed, God is Forgiving to His friends and Merciful to the people of His obedience, as He has made it easy for them to do so. And the transgressor and the transgressor are excluded, and they include every sinner who travels, such as the runaway and the tax collector, so it is not permissible for them to eat anything of that unless they repent, and Al-Shafi'i agrees with this.
2:174: Indeed, those who conceal what God has revealed of the Book which contains the description of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they are the Jews and exchange it for a small price of this world, which they take in exchange from their lowest people, so they do not reveal it for fear of missing out on it they eat nothing into their bellies except the Fire because it is their destination and God will not speak to them on the Day of Resurrection in anger with them nor will He purify them He will cleanse them from the filth of sins and they will have a painful punishment painful, which is the Fire.
2:175: Those are the ones who have purchased error in exchange for guidance they took it instead in this world and punishment in exchange for forgiveness prepared for them in the Hereafter if they had not concealed it so how patient they are with the Fire meaning how intense their patience is, and this is amazement to the believers from committing their obligations without caring, otherwise what patience do they have?
2:176: That which was mentioned about those who were eaten by the Fire and what follows it because because God sent down the Book with the truth is related to sent down, so they differed about it as they believed in some of it and disbelieved in some of it by concealing it and indeed those who differed about the Book by that and they are the Jews and it was said the polytheists in the Qur’an as some of them said poetry and some of them magic and some of them divination are in dissension disagreement far from the truth
2:177: Righteousness is not that you turn your faces in prayer towards the east or the west revealed in response to the Jews and Christians when they claimed that but righteousness meaning the righteous person and it was read with the opening of the ba’ meaning the righteous is he who believes in God and the Last Day and the angels and the Book meaning the Books and the prophets and gives wealth with his love for it to relatives relatives and orphans and the needy and the traveler the traveler and those who ask the seekers and for the freeing of slaves those who are freed and the captives and establishes prayer and gives zakah the obligatory and what preceded it in the voluntary and those who fulfill their covenant when they have made a covenant with God or the people and the patient accusative for praise in poverty severe poverty and hardship sickness and at the time of battle at the time of severe fighting in the way of God those described by what was mentioned are those who are true in their faith or claim of righteousness and those are the righteous of God
2:178: O you who have believed, retaliation has been prescribed for you as an obligation for those killed in description and action the free is killed for a free and is not killed for a slave and the slave for the slave and the female for the female and the Sunnah has made clear that the male is killed for it and that similarity in religion is considered so a Muslim is not killed even if he is a slave for an infidel even if he is free then whoever pardons of the two killers of the blood of his brother who was killed anything by leaving retaliation from him, and the indefiniteness of something indicates that retaliation is dropped by pardoning some of it and some of the heirs, and in mentioning his brother there is a connection calling for pardon and an indication that killing does not sever brotherhood in faith, and from is a conditional or relative subject and the predicate then follow meaning that the one who pardons must follow the killer in a good manner by demanding blood money from him without violence, and arranging following after pardon indicates that one of them is obligatory and it is one of the two opinions of Al-Shafi’i and the second is obligatory retaliation and blood money as a substitute for it, so if he pardons and does not name it then there is nothing and it is preferred and on the killer to pay The blood money to him meaning the pardoner who is the heir with kindness without delay or impurity that the mentioned ruling of the permissibility of retaliation and pardoning him for the blood money is a mitigation facilitation from your Lord upon you and a mercy for you since He made that easy and did not make one of them obligatory as He made retaliation obligatory upon the Jews and blood money upon the Christians but whoever transgresses wrongs the killer by killing him after that meaning the pardon will have a painful punishment painful in the Hereafter by fire or in this world by killing
2:179: And there is life for you in retaliation meaning great permanence O you who possess understanding those with intellects, because if the murderer knows that he will be killed, he will be deterred and revive himself, and whoever he wants to kill, it is prescribed that you may fear killing out of fear of retaliation.
2:180: It is written an obligation upon you, when death approaches any of you that is, its causes if he leaves behind wealth money the will is raised by it is written and is related to if if it is circumstantial and indicates its answer if it is conditional and the answer to if that is, then let him bequeath to parents and relatives according to what is reasonable with justice, not exceeding a third and not giving preference to the rich a right a source confirming the content of the sentence before it upon those who fear God God. This was abrogated by the verse on inheritance and the hadith: There is no will for an heir narrated by al-Tirmidhi
2:181: So whoever changes it meaning the will from a witness or a testator after he has heard it knowing it then his sin meaning the changed will is only upon those who change it in it the apparent is used in place of the implicit Indeed, God is Hearing of the statement of the testator Knowing of the action of the testator, so it is a punishment for him.
2:182: But whoever fears from the testator light and heavy inclination deviation from the truth by mistake or sin by intentionally doing so by increasing the third or singling out a rich person, for example and brings about reconciliation between them between the testator and the one to whom the testator was given by commanding justice there is no sin upon him in that indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful
2:183: O you who have believed, decreed upon you is an obligation as it was decreed upon those before you of the nations that you may become righteous of sins, for it breaks the desire that is its beginning.
2:184: Days is an accusative of fasting or they fast for a specified period numbered i.e. few or fixed in a known number, which is Ramadan as will come, and He shortened it to make it easier for those who are obligated So whoever among you when he witnesses it is ill or on a journey i.e. a traveler traveling on a shortened journey and fasting exhausts him in both cases, so he breaks his fast then the number he must fast the number of days he broke of other days he fasts them in his place he fasts them in his place And for those who cannot it due to old age or an illness from which there is no hope of recovery a ransom which is the feeding of a poor person i.e. the amount he eats in his day, which is a mudd of the country’s main food for each day, and in a reading with the addition of a ransom, which is for clarification, and it was said that it is not specified, and they were given the choice in the early days of Islam between fasting and the ransom, then it was abrogated by specifying the fasting with His saying whoever among you witnesses the month, let him fast it, Ibn Abbas said: Except for the pregnant and breastfeeding woman if they break their fast for fear for the child, then it remains without abrogation in their case So whoever volunteers good by increasing the amount mentioned in the ransom it is that is, volunteering (it is better for him. And that you fast) is the subject and its predicate it is better for you than breaking the fast and the ransom if you should know that it is better for you, then do it.
2:185: Those days the month of Ramadan in which the Qur'an was sent down from the Preserved Tablet to the lowest heaven on the Night of Decree, from it is guidance a state that guides from misguidance for mankind and clear proofs clear verses of guidance of what guides to the truth of the rulings and of the Criterion of what distinguishes between truth and falsehood so whoever witnesses is present among you the month, let him fast it, and whoever is ill or on a journey - then an equal number of other days something similar has come before and has been repeated so that it is not thought that it was abrogated by generalizing whoever witnesses God intends for you ease and does not intend for you hardship and therefore He permitted you to break the fast in sickness and travel and because that is also in the sense of a reason for the command to fast, it is connected to it and that you may complete with the lightening and emphasis the number meaning the number of fasts of Ramadan and that you may glorify God upon completing it for that He has guided you He guided you to the landmarks of His religion and perhaps you will be grateful to God for that
2:186: And a group asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: Is our Lord near so that we may commune with Him, or is He far so that we may call upon Him? So He revealed: And when My servants ask you concerning Me - indeed I am near to them with My knowledge, so He informed them of that I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me by granting Him what he asked So let them respond to Me My invocation with obedience and believe persist in believing in Me that they may be guided be guided.
2:187: Lawful to you on the night of the fast is sexual intercourse meaning to have intercourse with your wives through sexual intercourse. It was revealed to abrogate what was forbidden in the early days of Islam, and to prohibit eating and drinking after dinner. They are your garments and you are their garments a metaphor for their embracing or each of them needing the other. God knows that you were betraying betraying yourselves by having sexual intercourse on the night of the fast. This happened to Umar and others, and they apologized to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. So He turned to you before your repentance (and pardoned you. So now) since it has been made lawful for you have intercourse with them have sexual intercourse with them. And seek seek what God has decreed for you that is, what He has permitted of sexual intercourse or what He has decreed of children and eat and drink all night until the white thread of dawn appears that is, the true one, an explanation of the white thread and an explanation of the black thread is omitted, that is, from the night. He likened what appears of whiteness and what extends with it of dimness to two threads, white and black in extension then complete the fast from dawn until the night that is, until it begins with sunset and do not have sexual relations with them that is, your women while you are in retreat residing with the intention of retreat in the mosques related to those who are in retreat, a prohibition for whoever goes out while in retreat and has sexual relations with his wife and then returns those the rulings mentioned are the limits of God His limits for His servants so that they may stop at them so do not approach them more eloquently than do not transgress them expressed in another verse thus as He explained to you what He mentioned God makes clear His verses to the people that they may fear His prohibitions
2:188: And do not consume one another's wealth that is, some of you consume the wealth of others in falsehood that which is forbidden by Islamic law, such as theft and usurpation. And do not and do not send receive with it that is, with its governance or with the money as a bribe to the rulers in order to consume through arbitration a portion a group of the wealth of the people while you know) that you are wrongdoers.
2:189: They ask you O Muhammad about the new moons plural of hilal, it does not appear for a minute then increases until it is filled with light then returns as it appeared and is not in one state like the sun Say to them They are times plural of miqat for the people they know by them the times of their planting and their trade and the number of their wives and their fasting and breaking their fast and the Hajj in apposition to the people, meaning that its time is known by them, if it remained in one state it would not be known And righteousness is not that you enter houses from their backs in ihram by digging a hole in them through which you enter and exit and leave the door open, and they used to do that and claim it was righteousness But righteousness meaning righteousness is he who fears God by not disobeying Him and enter houses from their doors in ihram and fear God that you may succeed you will succeed
2:190: And when the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, was prevented from the House in the year of Al-Hudaybiyyah, and he made peace with the infidels on the condition that he would return the following year and leave Mecca for him for three days and prepare for the Umrah of Compensation, and they feared that the Quraysh would not fulfill their obligation and would fight them, and the Muslims hated fighting them in the Sacred Sanctuary, in the state of Ihram, and in the sacred month, the verse was revealed: And fight in the way of God those who fight you the infidels and do not transgress against them by initiating the fighting for God does not like transgressors those who exceed what is set for them, and this was abrogated by the verse of Bara’ah or by His saying:
2:191: And kill them wherever you find them you find them and expel them from where they expelled you that is, from Mecca, and this was done to them in the year of the conquest and persecution their polytheism is more severe greater than killing them in the sanctuary or the state of ihram which you considered great and do not fight them at the Sacred Mosque that is, in the sanctuary (until they fight you there. But if they fight you) there then kill them there, and in a reading without an alif in the three verbs such is killing and expulsion the recompense of the disbelievers
2:192: If they desist from disbelief and submit, then indeed, God is Forgiving to them, Merciful to them.
2:193: And fight them until there is no more fitnah polytheism and religion worship is for God alone, and no one else is worshipped. But if they desist from polytheism, then do not transgress against them. This is indicated by there is no aggression aggression by killing or otherwise except against the wrongdoers. And whoever desists is not a wrongdoer, so there is no aggression against him.
2:194: The sacred month Muharram is the opposite of the sacred month so just as they fought you therein, kill them in the same time, a response to the Muslims’ greatness in that and the sacred things the plural of sacredness is what must be respected retaliation meaning that the like of it is taken away if it is violated so whoever transgresses against you by fighting in the sacred area or in the state of ihram or the sacred month then transgress against him in the same way that he transgressed against you He called his counterpart transgression because of its resemblance to the counterpart in form and fear God in victory and refraining from transgression and know that God is with the righteous with aid and victory
2:195: And spend in the cause of God obeying Him through jihad and other things and do not throw yourselves with your own hands meaning yourselves, and the ba’ is redundant into destruction destruction by withholding spending in jihad or abandoning it because it strengthens the enemy against you and do good through spending and other things indeed, God loves the doers of good meaning He rewards them.
2:196: And complete the Hajj and Umrah for God perform them according to their rights but if you are prevented prevented from completing them by an enemy then whatever is easy is easy of the sacrificial animal for you, which is a sheep and do not shave your heads that is, do not become released until the sacrificial animal mentioned reaches its place where it is permissible to slaughter it, which is the place of confinement according to Al-Shafi’i, so it is slaughtered there with the intention of becoming released and it is distributed to its poor people and he is shaved and thus becomes released but whoever among you is ill or has an ailment of the head such as lice or a headache and shaves while in the state of ihram then he must offer a ransom of fasting) for three days or charity of three sa’ of the most common food of the country for six poor people or a sacrifice that is, slaughtering a sheep and the or is for choice and he included with him whoever shaved without an excuse because he is more deserving of expiation, and likewise whoever enjoys something other than creation such as perfume, clothing, or oil due to an excuse or otherwise but when you are safe from the enemy, whether he has gone or not then whoever (Enjoy enjoy Umrah i.e. because he has finished it with the prohibitions of ihram to Hajj i.e. to enter ihram for it by entering ihram for it during its months then whatever is easy is easy of the sacrificial animal for him, which is a sheep that he slaughters after entering ihram for it, and the best is the day of sacrifice and whoever does not find the sacrificial animal because he has lost it or lost its price then he must fast i.e. he must fast three days during Hajj i.e. while entering ihram for it, so he must then enter ihram before the seventh of Dhul-Hijjah, and the best is before the sixth because fasting on the Day of Arafah is disliked, and it is not permissible to fast them during the days of Tashreeq according to the most correct of the two opinions of al-Shafi’i and seven when you return to your homeland, Mecca or elsewhere, and it was said when you have finished the rituals of Hajj, and in this there is a shift from absence that is ten complete a sentence emphasizing what came before it. That the mentioned ruling of the obligation of the sacrifice or fasting on the one who enjoys for the one whose family is not present at the Sacred Mosque that is, they are not less than two stages from the Haram according to Al-Shafi’i. If they are, then there is no blood sacrifice or fasting on him even if he enjoys. And in mentioning the family, there is an indication of the condition of settlement, so if he resided before the months of Hajj and did not settle or enjoy himself, then he must do that, and this is one of two views according to Al-Shafi’i, and the second is no, and the family is a metaphor for the self, and the one who enjoys is included in what is mentioned in the Sunnah as the Qarin, which is the one who enters into the state of ihram for Umrah and Hajj together, or the Hajj is included in it before the Tawaf and fear God in what He commands you and forbids you and know that God is severe in penalty for whoever disobeys Him.
2:197: Hajj its time are well-known months Shawwal and Dhul-Qi'dah and ten nights of Dhul-Hijjah and it was said all of it so whoever has made Hajj obligatory upon himself therein by entering into ihram for it there is no sexual relations sexual intercourse therein nor wickedness sins nor disputing quarreling during Hajj and in a reading with the opening of the first two, and what is meant in the three is the prohibition and whatever good you do such as charity God knows it and He will reward you for it, and it was revealed about the people of Yemen and they used to perform Hajj without provisions and so they were a burden on the al-Nas 114:And take provisions what will sustain you for your journey but indeed, the best provision is fear of God what one uses to avoid asking people and other things and fear God, O you of understanding those with intellects
2:198: There is no blame upon you for seek ask for bounty provision from your Lord through trade during the Hajj. This was revealed in response to their dislike of that. So when you depart leave from Arafat after standing there then remember God after spending the night in Muzdalifah by uttering the Talbiyah, Tahlil and supplication at the sacred monument which is a mountain at the end of Muzdalifah called Quzah. In the hadith, it is stated that the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) stood there remembering God and supplicating until it became very light. Narrated by Muslim. And remember Him as He has guided you to the landmarks of His religion and the rituals of His Hajj. The kaf is for explanation. And if lightened you were before him before His guidance among those astray.
2:199: Then proceed O Quraysh from where the people proceed that is, from Arafat, so that you stand there with them, and they used to stand at Muzdalifah, out of disdain for standing with them, and then for the order in the remembrance and seek forgiveness of God for your sins Indeed, God is Forgiving to the believers and Merciful to them.
2:200: So when you have completed performed your rites the rituals of your Hajj by throwing pebbles at the Jamarat al-Aqaba and circumambulating and settling in Mina then remember God with glorification and praise as you remember your fathers as you used to remember them when you finished your Hajj with boasting or with a more intense remembrance than your remembrance of them, and the accusative of more intense is in the state of mentioning the accusative of remembering because if it had been delayed from it, it would have been a description of it for among the people is he who says, Our Lord, give us a share in this world and he is given it and he will have no share in the Hereafter a share
2:201: (And among them are those who say, Our Lord, give us in this world that which is good [i.e., a blessing] [and in the Hereafter that which is good] [i.e., Paradise] [and protect us from the punishment of the Fire] [i.e., from not entering it]. This is a statement of what the polytheists were like and the state of the believers, and the purpose of it is to urge people to seek the best of both worlds, just as He promised reward for that in His saying:
2:202: Those will have a share reward for what they have earned) the work they have done of Hajj and supplication and God is swift in account He will hold all creation to account in the space of half a day of the days of this world, according to a hadith to that effect.
2:203: And remember God by glorifying God when throwing the pebbles during the appointed days i.e. the three days of Tashreeq and whoever hastens i.e. hastens to leave Mina in two days i.e. on the second day of Tashreeq after throwing his pebbles there is no sin upon him for hastening and whoever delays until he spends the night of the third and throws his pebbles there is no sin upon him for that i.e. they have a choice in that and the sin is negated for him who fears God in his pilgrimage because he is the pilgrim in reality and fear God and know that to Him you will be gathered in the Hereafter and He will reward you for your deeds
2:204: And among the people is he whose speech pleases you in worldly life but does not please you in the Hereafter because it contradicts his belief and he calls God to witness as to what is in his heart that it agrees with what he says but he is the most hostile of opponents fiercely hostile to you and your followers because of his enmity towards you. He is Al-Akhns bin Shuraik. He was a hypocrite who spoke sweetly to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace. He would swear that he was a believer and loved him, so he would come near to him, but God denied him in that. He passed by some crops and donkeys belonging to some Muslims and burned them and hamstrung them at night, as God Almighty said:
2:205: And when he turns away turns away from you he strives walks through the land to cause corruption therein and destroy crops and livestock as part of the corruption and God does not like corruption meaning He is not pleased with it.
2:206: And when it is said to him, Fear God in your action pride seizes him arrogance and zeal drive him to commit the sin which he was commanded to avoid So sufficient for him is enough for him Hell, and wretched is the resting place the bed is
2:207: And among the people is he who buys sells his soul that is, he spends it in obedience to God seeks seeking the pleasure of God His satisfaction, and he is Suhaib, when the polytheists harmed him, he migrated to Medina and left them his money and God is Kind to His servants as he guided them to what pleases Him.
2:208: And it was revealed about Abdullah bin Salam and his companions when they glorified the Sabbath and hated camels after Islam (O you who have believed, enter into peace) with the opening of the seen and its breaking, Islam all of it a state of peace, meaning in all of its laws and do not follow the footsteps of the paths of Satan meaning his adornment of division for indeed, he is to you a clear enemy between enmity
2:209: But if you slip turn away from entering into all of it after the clear proofs have come to you the clear arguments that it is true then know that God is Exalted in Might nothing is beyond His power to take revenge on you and Wise in His creation.
2:210: Are what they are waiting for the ones who are leaving it waiting to enter it unless God comes to them that is, His command, as He says, or the command of your Lord comes, that is, His punishment in the shades plural of shade of clouds clouds and the angels, and the matter is decided the matter of their destruction has been completed and to God all matters are returned in the passive and active construction in the Hereafter, so He rewards each one according to his work
2:211: Ask O Muhammad the Children of Israel as a rebuke how many We have given them how much is an interrogative suspended. Ask about the second object, and it is the second object of We have given and what distinguishes it of a clear sign apparent like the splitting of the sea and the sending down of manna and quails, but they exchanged it for disbelief and whoever exchanges the favor of God that is, what He has bestowed upon him of signs because they are the cause of guidance after it has come to him for disbelief then indeed, God is severe in penalty for him.
2:212: The life of this world has been made attractive to those who disbelieve from the people of Mecca by deception, so they loved it (and they mock those who believe because of their poverty, like Bilal, Ammar, and Suhaib, meaning they mock them and are arrogant towards them with money and those who feared God polytheism, and they are these people (are above them on the Day of Resurrection. And God provides for whom He wills without account) meaning abundant provision in the Hereafter or in this world, so that the mocked one among them owns the money and necks of the mockers.
2:213: Mankind was one community in faith, then they differed in that some believed and some disbelieved so God sent the prophets to them as bearers of good tidings to those who believed in Paradise and as warners to those who disbelieved in Hell and sent down with them the Scripture meaning the books with the truth related to sent down to judge with it between the people concerning that in which they differed of the religion and no one differed therein meaning the religion except those who were given it meaning the Scripture, so some believed and some disbelieved after the clear proofs had come to them the apparent arguments for monotheism, and min is related to differed and it and what follows it is presented before the exception in meaning out of envy on the part of the disbelievers (between themselves. So God guided those who believed to that in which they differed) of the truth by His permission by His will and God guides whom He wills His guidance to a straight path the path of truth
2:214: And it was revealed in a hardship that befell the Muslims Or rather Do you think that you will enter Paradise while (there has not come to you the like) of what came to those who passed on before you of the believers of trials, so be patient as they were patient They were afflicted a renewed clause explaining what came before it with hardship severe poverty and distress illness and they were shaken they were disturbed by various types of affliction until the Messenger and those who believed with him said in the accusative and nominative, meaning he said When will come the victory of God which we were promised and they were answered by God Unquestionably, the victory of God is near its coming
2:215: They ask you O Muhammad what they should spend meaning what they should spend, and the questioner was Amr ibn al-Jamuh, who was an old man with wealth, so he asked the Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) about what he should spend and on whom he should spend it Say to them Whatever you spend of good a statement of what includes little and much, and in it is a statement of the spender, which is one part of the question, and he answered about the expenditure, which is the other part, by saying: For parents and relatives and orphans and the needy and the wayfarer meaning they are more deserving of it And whatever you do of good spending or otherwise then indeed, God is Knowing of it so he rewarded him for it
2:216: He wrote He imposed on you fighting for the infidels and it is disliked disliked for you of course because of its hardship and perhaps you dislike a thing and it is good for you and perhaps you like a thing and it is bad for you because of the soul’s inclination towards the desires that require it and its aversion to the obligations that require its happiness, so perhaps there is good for you in fighting, even if you dislike it, because in it is either victory and spoils or martyrdom and reward, and in abandoning it, even if you like it, there is evil because in it is humiliation and poverty and deprivation of reward and God knows what is good for you and you do not know that, so hasten to what He commands you to do.
2:217: The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent his first expedition, led by Abdullah bin Jahsh, and they fought the polytheists on the last day of Jumada al-Akhira. Rajab was confusing to them, so the infidels reproached them for making it permissible, so this verse was revealed: They ask you about the sacred month the sacred month fighting therein a substitute for inclusion Say to them fighting therein is a great sin a great sin, subject and predicate and averting subject preventing people from the way of God His religion and disbelief in Him God and averting from the Sacred Mosque meaning Mecca and the expulsion of its people therefrom they are the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the believers and the predicate of the subject is greater greater sin in the sight of God than fighting therein and persecution polytheism from you is greater than killing you in it and they will not cease meaning the infidels fighting you O believers until so that they turn you back from your religion to disbelief (if they are able. And whoever among you turns back from His religion, so he dies while he is a disbeliever. So those, their good deeds have become worthless in this world and the Hereafter. They are not to be taken into account and there is no reward for them. The restriction of dying upon him indicates that if he returns to Islam, his deeds will not be nullified and he will be rewarded for them and he will not repeat them, like the Hajj, for example. Al-Shafi’i agreed with this. And those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.
2:218: And when the secret police thought that if they were saved from sin they would not receive any reward, it was revealed Indeed, those who believed and those who emigrated left their homelands and fought in the cause of God to exalt His religion they are the ones who hope for the mercy of God His reward and God is Forgiving to the believers and Merciful to them.
2:219: They ask you about wine and gambling gambling, what is the ruling on them? Say to them: In them is a great sin great, and in a reading with the triangle {much} because of what happens because of them of quarreling and insulting and saying obscene things and some benefit for people through pleasure and joy in wine and gaining money without toil in gambling and their sin meaning what results from them of corruption is greater greater than their benefit and when it was revealed some people drank it and others abstained from it until the verse of the Table forbade it and they ask you what they should spend meaning what He has estimated say spend that which is beyond need meaning what is excess and do not spend what you need and waste yourselves, and in a reading with the nominative with the estimation of it thus meaning as He explained to you what He mentioned God makes clear to you the verses that you may reflect
2:220: In the matter of this world and the Hereafter, so you take what is best for you in both. And they ask you about orphans and the hardship they encounter in their affair. If they eat with them, they sin, and if they set aside their wealth and prepare food for them alone, then there is hardship. Say, "Improvement for them" in their wealth by increasing it and your intervening (is better than leaving that. And if you mix with them that is, mix your spending with their wealth they are your brothers that is, they are your brothers in religion, and it is the nature of a brother to mix with his brother, that is, you have that. And God knows the corrupter of their wealth by mixing with him from the reformer with it, so He will punish both of them. And if God had willed, He could have made you suffer He would have made it difficult for you by forbidding mixing. Indeed, God is Exalted in Might Prevailing over His affair Wise in His creation.
2:221: And do not marry O Muslims, polytheistic women meaning disbelieving women (until they believe. And a believing slave woman is better than a polytheist) a free woman because the reason for its revelation is the fault of the one who marries a slave woman and encourages him to marry a free polytheist even if she pleases you for her beauty and wealth. This is specific to non-scriptural women in the verse {And chaste women from among those who were given the Scripture} And do not marry marry polytheistic men meaning disbelieving believing women (until they believe. And a believing slave is better than a polytheist, even if he pleases you) for his wealth and beauty. Those meaning the polytheists invite to the Fire by their invitation to the actions that necessitate it, so it is not appropriate to marry them. And God invites through the tongues of His messengers to Paradise and forgiveness meaning the actions that necessitate them by His permission by His will, so it is obligatory to respond to Him by marrying His saints and He makes His verses clear to the people that perhaps they will be reminded to be admonished.
2:222: And they ask you about menstruation meaning menstruation or its place, what is done to women during it Say, "It is a harmful thing" filth or its place so keep away from women refrain from intercourse with them during menstruation meaning its time or place and do not approach them with intercourse until they are purified with a sukoon on the taa and a shaddah on it and the haa and in it the assimilation of the taa in the original into the taa meaning they wash themselves after it stops then when they have purified themselves, then come to them with intercourse from where God has commanded you to avoid it during menstruation which is the vagina and do not go beyond it to anything else Indeed, God loves rewards and honors those who repent from sins and He loves those who purify themselves from impurities.
2:223: Your women are a place of cultivation for you meaning the place where you sow your child so come to your place of cultivation meaning its place, which is the vagina however however you wish standing, sitting, lying down, facing forward and backward. It was revealed in response to the Jews’ saying: Whoever comes to his wife in her vagina, meaning from her anus, the child will be born cross-eyed and put forth for yourselves righteous deeds such as saying Bismillah before intercourse and fear God in His commands and prohibitions and know that you will meet Him at the resurrection, so He will reward you for your deeds and give good tidings to the believers those who fear Him of Paradise.
2:224: And do not make God i.e. swearing by Him an object a reason preventing your oaths i.e. an objection to them by swearing by Him a lot that you do not be righteous and fear God so swearing on that basis is disliked and breaking it is recommended and expiation is required, unlike doing righteousness and the like, so it is obedience and reconcile between people meaning do not refrain from doing what was mentioned of righteousness and the like if you swear on it, rather do it and expiate because the reason for its revelation was refraining from that and God is Hearing of your words Knowing of your conditions.
2:225: God will not hold you accountable for what is unintentional in your oaths, which is what the tongue utters without intending to swear, such as, By God, and Yes, by God. There is no sin or expiation upon him, but He will hold you accountable for what your hearts have earned, meaning what you intended of oath if you broke your oath. And God is Forgiving for what was unintentional and Forbearing in delaying punishment for those who deserve it.
2:226: For those who swear not to have sexual intercourse with their wives that is, they swear not to have sexual intercourse with them (a waiting period of four months. But if they return) they return to the oath or after that to sexual intercourse then indeed, God is Forgiving of them for what they have done of harm to the woman by swearing and Merciful to them.
2:227: And if they decide on divorce meaning on him if they do not return, then let them carry it out then indeed, God is Hearing of their words and Knowing of their decision, meaning that they have no waiting after what was mentioned except return or divorce.
2:228: And divorced women shall wait that is, they shall wait concerning themselves from marriage three menstrual periods that pass from the time of divorce, the plural of qrṭr with the opening of the qaf, which is purity or menstruation, there are two opinions, and this is for those who have been consummated with, but for others there is no waiting period for them, according to His statement {So you have no waiting period for them} and for those other than those who have despaired and the young girl, their waiting period is three months, and for pregnant women their waiting period is until they give birth, as in Surat At-Talaq and for the slave girls, their waiting period is two menstrual periods according to the Sunnah and it is not lawful for them to conceal what God has created in their wombs of children and menstruation (if they believe in God and the Last Day. And their husbands) their husbands have more right to take them back by returning to them even if they refuse in that that is, during the waiting period if they desire reconciliation between them, not to harm the woman, and it is an incitement to intend it, not a condition for the permissibility of returning, and this is in divorce and more deserving, there is no preference in it, since others have no right to marry them during the waiting period And they have over their husbands the same as they have over them of rights according to what is reasonable according to the Shari’ah, such as good treatment, refraining from causing harm, and the like. And men have a degree over them a virtue in the right of the obligation of their obedience to them for what they have given of the dowry and spending. And God is Exalted in Might in His kingdom, Wise in what He has planned for His creation.
2:229: Divorce i.e. the divorce after which one may return twice i.e. two then either keep them i.e. you must keep them after it by returning to them with kindness without causing harm or release them i.e. send them (with kindness. And it is not lawful for you) oh husbands to take back from what you have given them of dowries anything if you divorce them unless they both fear i.e. the spouses that they will not be able to keep within the limits of God i.e. that they will not fulfill what He has set for them of rights. In the reading of {they fear} in the passive form, then not keeping is a substitute for inclusion of the pronoun in it, and it was read with the supra-verbal in both verbs but if you fear that they will not be able to keep within the limits of God, then there is no blame upon them (for what they ransom themselves with) of money in order to divorce her i.e. there is no blame upon the husband for taking it nor upon the wife for giving it those the rulings mentioned (are the limits of God, so do not transgress them. And whoever transgresses the limits of God Those are the wrongdoers.
2:230: And if he divorces her the husband after the second then she is not lawful to him thereafter that is, after the third divorce until she marries marries another husband and he has intercourse with her as in the hadith narrated by the two sheikhs And if he divorces her that is, the second husband then there is no blame upon them that is, the wife and the first husband if they return to marriage after the expiration of the waiting period (if they think that they can keep within the limits of God. And those) the aforementioned limits of God He makes clear for a people who know who reflect.
2:231: And when you divorce women and they have fulfilled their term their waiting period is about to expire then keep them by taking them back according to kindness without harm or release them with kindness leave them until their waiting period ends and do not keep them by taking them back to cause harm an object for which to transgress against them by forcing them to ransom or divorce or prolong their imprisonment and whoever does that has wronged himself by exposing himself to the punishment of God and do not take the verses of God in jest mocked by opposing them and remember the favor of God upon you by Islam and what He has sent down to you of the Book the Qur’an and wisdom what is in it of rulings by which He instructs you by thanking them by acting upon it and fear God and know that God is Knowing of all things and nothing is hidden from Him
2:232: And when you divorce women and they have fulfilled their term their waiting period has expired do not prevent them addressing the guardians, meaning prevent them from marrying their husbands those who have divorced them, because the reason for its revelation is that the sister of Muqal ibn Yasar was divorced by her husband, so he wanted to take her back, but Muqal ibn Yasar prevented her, as narrated by Al-Hakim if they agree meaning the husbands and women between themselves in kindness according to the Shari’ah that the prohibition of preventing preventing is admonished by whoever among you believes in God and the Last Day because he is the one who benefits from it that meaning abandoning preventing preventing is purer better for you and cleaner for you and for them because of what is feared for the spouses from suspicion because of the relationship between them and God knows what is in the interest and you do not know that, so follow His commands
2:233: And mothers shall suckle that is, they shall suckle their children for two complete years two years for him who desires to complete the nursing and there is no more for him and upon the father of the child that is, the father is their provision feeding the mothers and clothing for the nursing if they are divorced according to what is acceptable according to his ability no soul shall be charged except [with that within] its capacity (no mother shall be harmed by her child that is, because of it by being forced to nurse him if she refuses nor (a father of the child shall be harmed by his child) that is, because of it by being charged beyond his capacity. The addition of the child to each of them in both places is to show compassion and upon the heir that is, the father’s heir, which is the boy, that is, upon his guardian in his wealth the same as upon the father for the mother of the provision and clothing but if they both desire that is, the parents weaning weaning him before the two years, proceeding by mutual consent agreement from them and consultation between them to show the boy’s interest in it there is no blame upon them in That and if you wish addressing the fathers to have your children nursed by wet nurses other than the mothers there is no blame upon you in that if you hand over to them what you have given that is, if you wish to give it to them as wages according to what is acceptable in a beautiful manner, such as with a good heart and fear God and know that God is Seeing of what you do nothing of it is hidden from Him.
2:234: (And those of you who die and leave wives behind them, they shall wait for four months and ten nights after marriage. This is for those who are not pregnant. As for pregnant women, their waiting period is until they give birth, according to the verse of divorce, and the female slave is entitled to half of that in a year. (And when they have reached their term) their waiting period has ended, there is no blame upon you, O guardians, for what they do with themselves of adorning themselves and exposing themselves to speech in a fair manner according to the Shari'ah. And God is Acquainted with what you do. He knows its inner meaning as well as its outer meaning.
2:235: And there is no blame upon you for what you hint at hint at of proposing marriage to women whose husbands have died during their waiting period, such as when a person says, for example: You are truly beautiful, and who can find someone like you? And there is a Lord who desires you or conceal it conceal within yourselves of the intention of marrying them God knows that you will mention them by proposing marriage and will not be patient without them, so He permitted you to hint but do not make a secret promise to them that is, to marry unless but you say a proper word that is, what is known in the Shariah of hinting, so you may do that and do not resolve on the marriage contract that is, on concluding it until the written that is, the written of the waiting period its term is over and know that God knows what is in your souls of resolve and other things so beware of Him lest He punish you if you resolve and know that God is Forgiving to those who warn Him and Forbearing in delaying punishment from those who deserve it
2:236: There is no blame upon you if you divorce women before you have touched them and in another reading {touch them} meaning have intercourse with them or have not assigned for them a dowry and ma is a circumstantial source meaning there is no responsibility upon you - in divorce at a time of no touching and imposition - for sin or dowry, so divorce them and provide for them give them what they enjoy on the wealthy the rich among you according to his ability and on the poor the one with limited means according to his ability indicating that there is no consideration for the value of the wife provision provision according to what is acceptable according to the law, an attribute of provision rightly a second attribute or an emphatic source on the doers of good the obedient
2:237: And if you divorce them before you have touched them and you have already specified for them a dowry, then half of what you specified is due to them and half returns to you unless but that they forgive meaning the wives, so they leave him or the one in whose hand is the marriage contract forgives meaning the husband, so he leaves her everything, and from Ibn Abbas: the guardian if she is incapacitated, then there is no blame in that and that you forgive is a subject whose predicate is (is closer to righteousness. And do not forget graciousness between you) meaning that some of you should be generous to others Indeed, God is Seeing of what you do so He will reward you for it.
2:238: Maintain the prayers the five prayers by performing them at their appointed times and the middle prayer is the afternoon, dawn, noon, or other sayings, and it was mentioned separately for its virtue and stand before God in prayer devoutly it was said obediently, based on the saying of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace: Every devoutness in the Qur’an is obedience narrated by Ahmad and others, and it was said silently, based on the hadith of Zayd ibn Arqam: We used to talk during prayer until it was revealed, so we were commanded to be silent and forbidden from talking, narrated by the two sheikhs
2:239: But if you fear an enemy, a flood, or a wild animal on foot the plural of rajil, meaning pedestrians, pray or riding the plural of rakib, meaning however possible, facing the qiblah or otherwise, and he gestures for bowing and prostrating But when you are secure from fear then remember God meaning pray as He has taught you that which you did not know before teaching Him of its obligations and rights. The kaf means like, and ma is a source or relative.
2:240: And those of you who die and leave wives behind let them make a bequest for their wives and give them provisions what they enjoy of maintenance and clothing until the completion of a year from their death, which is what they are required to wait other than turning them out in the present, meaning not being taken out of their homes but if they go out by themselves there is no blame upon you O guardians of the deceased for what they do with themselves of good according to the Shariah, such as adorning themselves, abandoning mourning, and cutting off maintenance for them and God is Exalted in Might in His kingdom and Wise in His creation, and the aforementioned bequest was abrogated by the verse of inheritance and the waiting of a year by the verse of four months and ten, which preceded and was later in revelation, and the residence is established for them according to Al-Shafi’i, may God have mercy on him.
2:241: And for divorced women is provision they give it according to what is acceptable as much as possible a right in the accusative case with its implied verb upon the righteous God Almighty repeated it to include the possessed as well, since the previous verse is in another one.
2:242: Likewise as He explains to you what He mentioned God makes clear to you His signs that you may understand ponder.
2:243: Have you not seen an interrogative of astonishment and suspense to hear what comes after it, i.e. your knowledge ends at those who left their homes, and they were thousands four or eight or ten or thirty or forty or seventy thousand for fear of death an object for which they were a people from the Children of Israel in whose country the plague struck, so they fled so God said to them, Die so they died then He revived them after eight days or more through the supplication of their prophet Ezekiel with a broken silent letter and the qaf and a silent zay [and he is the prophet of God Dhul-Kifl] so they lived for a long time with the traces of death on them, they did not wear a garment except that it became like a shroud and it continued among their tribes Indeed, God is full of bounty to mankind and from this is the revival of these but most of the people and they are the disbelievers are not grateful and the purpose of mentioning the report of these is to encourage the believers to fight and therefore He conjoined it with it
2:244: And fight in the cause of God that is, to exalt His religion and know that God is Hearing of your words and Knowing of your conditions, so He will reward you.
2:245: Who is it that would lend to God by spending his money in the way of God a goodly loan by spending it for God the Almighty with a good heart so He may multiply it and in another reading {and multiply it} with emphasis for him many times over from ten to more than seven hundred as will come and God withholds withholds provision from whomever He wills as a test and expands He expands it for whomever He wills as a test and to Him you will be returned in the Hereafter by resurrection and He will reward you for your deeds
2:246: Have you not considered the assembly the group of the Children of Israel after the death of Moses that is, their story and report when they said to a prophet of theirs he is Samuel Send establish for us a king, that we may fight with him in the way of God through whom our word will be organized and to whom we will return He said the prophet to them Will you perhaps with the fat-ha and the kasra if fighting is prescribed for you, will you not fight the predicate of perhaps and the interrogative is to confirm the signing with it (They said: And what is the matter with us) n that we should not fight in the way of God while we have been expelled from our homes and our children because of them and killed them and the people of Goliath did that to them, that is, there is no objection to it despite the existence of its requirement, the Most High said: But when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned away from it and were cowardly except a few of them and they are those who crossed the river with Talut as will come and God is Knowing of the wrongdoers so He will reward them and the prophet asked to send a king and he answered him to send Talut
2:247: And their prophet said to them, Indeed, God has sent you Saul as a king. They said, How can he have kingship over us when we are more deserving of kingship than he? because he was not from the tribe of the kingdom or the prophethood and was a tanner or a shepherd and he has not been given an abundance of wealth to help him establish the kingdom the prophet said to them Indeed, God has chosen him selected him for kingship over you and has increased him abundantly in knowledge and body) and he was the most knowledgeable of the Children of Israel at that time and the most beautiful and perfect of them in character and God gives His kingdom to whom He wills giving it is not objectionable and God is Bountiful His grace and Knowing of who is worthy of it
2:248: And their prophet said to them when they asked him for a sign of his kingdom Indeed, the sign of his kingship is that the Ark will come to you the chest in which were pictures of the prophets, which God sent down to Adam and continued to them, but the giants overcame them and took it and they used it to gain victory over their enemy and advance it in battle and find peace with it as God Almighty said in it is tranquility reassurance for your hearts from your Lord and a remnant of what the family of Moses and the family of Aaron left behind which were Moses’ sandals, his staff, Aaron’s turban, a handful of manna that was sent down to them and fragments of tablets the angels carry it a state of the subject of comes to you Indeed in that is a sign for you of his kingship if you are believers so the angels carried it between heaven and earth while they were looking at it until they placed it with Talut, so they acknowledged his kingship and hastened to jihad, so he chose seventy thousand of their young men
2:249: So when he departed Talut went out with the soldiers from Jerusalem and the heat was intense and they asked him for water (he said: Verily, God will test you) test you with a river to show who among you is obedient and who is disobedient, and it is between the Jordan and Palestine So whoever drinks from it that is, from its water is not of me that is, of my followers And whoever does not taste it tastes it is of me, except for he who takes a handful with his hand) and is satisfied with it and does not add anything more, for he is of me So they drank from it and when they met him in large numbers Except for a few of them so they limited themselves to the handful, it was narrated that it was sufficient for their drinking and their animals and they were three hundred and some men So when he and those who believed with him crossed it and they were those who limited themselves to the handful They said that is, those who drank We have no strength power today against Goliath and his soldiers that is, to fight them and they were cowardly and did not cross it Those who are certain said are certain They will meet God at the resurrection, and they are those who passed it how many a piece of information meaning many of a small group a group who overcame a large group by God’s permission by His will and God is with the patient with help and victory.
2:250: And when they came forward to face Goliath and his soldiers that is, they appeared to fight them and lined up they said, Our Lord, pour out upon us patience and plant firmly our feet by strengthening our hearts for jihad (and give us victory over the disbelieving people
2:251: So they defeated them they broke them by God's permission by His will and killed David who was in the army of Talut Goliath and God gave him that is, David the kingdom over the Children of Israel and wisdom the prophecy after the death of Samuel and Talut and they had not been combined for anyone before him and taught him of whatever He willed such as the making of armor and the language of birds and had God not repelled some people He replaced some people by others, the earth would have been corrupted by the dominance of the polytheists and the killing of the Muslims and the destruction of the mosques but God is full of bounty to the worlds so He repelled some of them by others
2:252: These these verses are the verses of God which We recite We relate to you O Muhammad in truth in honesty and indeed, you are among the messengers the emphasis with in and others is in response to the infidels’ saying to him, You are not a messenger.
2:253: Those are the subject of the messengers an adjective or predicate We preferred some of them over others by singling them out with a virtue that no one else has Among them are those to whom God spoke like Moses And He raised some of them meaning Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace in degrees over others by the universality of the call and the seal of prophethood and the preference of his nation over all other nations and the many miracles and numerous characteristics And We gave Jesus, son of Mary, clear proofs and supported him strengthened him with the Holy Spirit Gabriel who walks with him wherever he goes And if God had willed He would have guided all people Those after them after the messengers, meaning their nations would not have fought each other after the clear proofs had come to them because of their differences and the misleading of one another But they differed because of His will And among them are those who believed who remained steadfast in their faith And among them are those who disbelieved like the Christians after Christ And if God had willed, they would not have fought each other an emphasis But God does what He wills of guiding whomever He wills and failing whomever He wills
2:254: O you who have believed, spend from that which We have provided for you its zakat before there comes a Day in which there will be no trade ransom nor friendship a friendship that will benefit nor intercession without His permission, and that is the Day of Resurrection. And in a reading with the three raised and the disbelievers in God or in what was imposed upon them they are the wrongdoers for placing God’s command in the wrong place.
2:255: God, there is no god that is, there is no true deity in existence except Him, the Ever-Living the Eternal, the Self-Sustaining) the Sustainer of all things, the utmost in managing His creation He is not overtaken by drowsiness drowsiness (nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth) as dominion, creation, and servants Who is it that that is, no one can intercede with Him except by His permission for Him in it He knows what is before them that is, creation and what is behind them that is, of the affairs of this world and the Hereafter and they do not encompass anything of His knowledge that is, they do not know anything of His information except what He wills to teach them of it by informing the messengers His Kursi encompasses the heavens and the earth it was said that His knowledge encompasses them, and it was said that the Kursi itself includes them due to its greatness, according to the hadith: The seven heavens in comparison to the Kursi are nothing but seven dirhams thrown into a shield. And He is not burdened by preserving them (i.e. the heavens and the earth) and He is the Most High above His creation by force the Great the Grand
2:256: There is no compulsion in religion to enter it The right path has become distinct from error meaning it has become clear through clear verses that faith is right path and disbelief is error. It was revealed about those who had children from the Ansar and wanted to force them to convert to Islam So whoever disbelieves in Taghut Satan or idols, and it is used for both singular and plural and believes in God has grasped has grasped the firmest handhold the firm contract with no break interruption (in it. And God is Hearing) of what is said Knowing of what is done.
2:257: God is the protector of those who believe. He brings them out from darknesses) disbelief into the light faith. (And those who disbelieve, their protectors are false deities. They bring them out from the light into darknesses) The mention of bringing them out is either in contrast to His statement {He brings them out from darknesses} or in every one of the Jews who believed in the Prophet before his mission and then disbelieved in him. Those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally
2:258: Have you not seen the one who argued argued with Abraham about his Lord because God had given him the kingdom that is, his arrogance caused him to do so because of God’s blessing, and he was Nimrod when instead of argued Abraham said when he said to him, Who is your Lord to whom you are calling us? My Lord is He who gives life and causes death that is, He creates life and death in bodies He said he I give life and cause death by killing and pardoning him, and he called for two men and killed one of them and left the other, and when he saw him dumb Abraham said moving to a clearer argument than it Then God brings the sun from the east, so bring it you from the west. So the disbeliever was dumbfounded) he was confused and astonished and God does not guide the wrongdoing people by disbelief to the path of argument.
2:259: or I saw like the one the kaf is redundant passing by a village which is Jerusalem riding on a donkey and with him a basket of figs and a cup of juice which is Ezra and it was empty fallen on its thrones its fall when Bukhtunasr destroyed it he said how how God will revive this after its death in awe of His Almighty power so God caused him to die and made him remain a hundred years and then resurrected him He revived him to show him how that would be he said the Almighty to him how long did you stay did you stay here he said I stayed a day or part of a day because he slept at the beginning of the day then he was taken and revived at sunset so he thought it was the day of sleep he said rather you stayed a hundred years so look at your food the figs and your drink the juice it has not become old it has not changed with the passage of time, and the ha is said to be the origin of sanaht and it is said for the silence from sanayt and in a reading with its deletion and look at your donkey how it is so he saw it Dead and his bones white and shining! We did this so that you may learn and to make you a sign of the resurrection (for mankind. And look at the bones) of your donkey how We resurrect them We revive them with a damma on the noon and it is read with a fatha from anshaz and nashaz, two languages. And in the reading {ننشوها} with the ra’ we move it and raise it and then We cover it with flesh so he looked at it and it was assembled and covered with flesh and the soul was breathed into it and it brayed and when it became clear to him by witnessing he said, I know he knew by witnessing that God is over all things competent and in the reading {أ’lam} a command from God to him
2:260: And remember (when Abraham said, My Lord, show me how You give life to the dead. He said, Do you not believe? in My ability to give life? He asked Him, knowing that he believed in that, so that He would answer him with what He answered, so that the listeners would know His purpose. (He said, Yes. I believe, but I asked You, so that my heart may be reassured by witnessing the evidence. (He said, Then take four birds and bring them to you, with a kasra on the sad and a damma on it, incline them to you and cut them up and mix their meat and feathers. (Then place on every mountain) of the mountains of your land a portion of them, then call them to you they will come to you running. And know that God is Exalted in Might and Wise in His creation. So He took a peacock, an eagle, a crow, and a rooster and did with them what was mentioned, and held their heads close to him and called them, so the parts flew to each other until they were complete, then they came to their heads.
2:261: The example of the expenditures of those who spend their wealth in the way of God i.e. in His obedience is like the example of a grain which grows seven ears, in each ear a hundred grains so their expenditures are multiplied seven hundred times and God multiplies more than that for whom He wills, and God is all-Encompassing in His grace and Knowing of who deserves multiplication.
2:262: Those who spend their wealth in the way of God and do not follow up what they have spent with a sign from Us to the one on whom the money was spent by saying, for example: I have done good to him and improved his condition nor harm to him by mentioning that to someone who does not like to be informed about it and the like they will have their reward the reward for their spending with their Lord, and there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve in the Hereafter.
2:263: A well-known saying A good word and a beautiful response to the questioner and forgiveness for his insistence is better than charity followed by injury by a favor and reproaching him for asking and God is free from the charity of the servants and Forbearing in delaying the punishment for the one who favors and the one who causes harm
2:264: O you who have believed, do not invalidate your charities that is, their rewards with reminders or injury invalidation like one that is, like invalidating the spending of one who spends his wealth to be seen by people showing off to them and does not believe in God and the Last Day he is the hypocrite his example is like that of a smooth rock a smooth stone upon which is dust, then a downpour heavy rain leaves it hard hard and smooth, with nothing on it they are unable a resumption to explain the example of the hypocrite who spends to be seen by people and the pronoun is plural in consideration of the meaning of who for anything of what they have earned what they have done, that is, they will not find any reward for it in the Hereafter, just as there is nothing on the smooth rock from the dust that was on it because the rain has washed it away and God does not guide the disbelieving people
2:265: And the example of the expenditures of those who spend their wealth seeking seeking the pleasure of God and to establish themselves that is, to achieve the reward for it, unlike the hypocrites who do not hope for it because of their denial of it. And from the beginning is like a garden an orchard on a hill with a damma on the ra’ or a fatha on it, a high, level place which is struck by a downpour [heavy rain] and it produces its fruit twice twice what others produce but if no downpour strikes it, then a light drizzle a light rain that strikes it and is sufficient for it because of its height, the meaning: it produces fruit and grows whether the rain is abundant or little, so the expenditures of those mentioned grow with God whether it is abundant or little and God is Seeing of what you do so He will reward you for it.
2:266: Would any of you like to have a garden an orchard of palm trees and grapevines with rivers flowing beneath it, for him therein fruits of all kinds of fruits, and old age has overtaken him) so he has become too weak from old age to earn and he has weak offspring young children who are unable to support him and a hurricane a strong wind with fire in it struck it and it was burned so he lost it when he needed it most and he and his children were left helpless and confused, without a means to help them. This is a metaphor for the expense of the hypocrite and the one who shows off in its disappearance and its lack of benefit when he needs it most in the Hereafter. The question is in the sense of negation. According to Ibn Abbas, it is the man who did acts of obedience, then Satan was sent to him, so he did acts of disobedience until he burned up his deeds Thus as he explained what was mentioned God makes clear to you the verses that you may reflect so you may take heed.
