2:267: O you who have believed, spend that is, give zakat from the good things of what you have earned and from the good things (which We have produced for you from the earth of grains and fruits and do not aim intend the bad the poor thereof that is mentioned spending it in zakat, a state of the pronoun of aim and you would not take it that is, the bad if you gave it as part of your rights unless you close your eyes to it by being lenient and lowering your gaze, so how can you fulfill the right of God from it and know that God is free from your expenditures Praiseworthy praised in every situation.
2:268: Satan threatens you with poverty frightens you with it if you give charity and then hold back and orders you to immorality miserliness and withholding zakat while God promises you for spending forgiveness from Him for your sins and bounty provision in replacement from Him and God is All-Encompassing His bounty and Knowing of the spender.
2:269: He gives wisdom meaning beneficial knowledge that leads to action (to whom He wills. And he who has been given wisdom has certainly been given much good) because he is destined for eternal happiness. And none will be reminded in it is the assimilation of the ta’ in the original into the dhal, so he learns a lesson except those of understanding the people of intellects.
2:270: And whatever you spend whether you pay zakat or charity or vow and fulfill it then indeed God knows it and He will reward you for it and the wrongdoers by withholding zakat and vows or by spending in the wrong place of disobedience to God will have no helpers to protect them from His punishment.
2:271: If you disclose disclose your charitable expenditures, i.e. voluntary expenditures, they are good, i.e. a good thing to disclose them, but if you conceal them conceal them and give them to the poor, it is better for you than disclosing them and giving them to the rich. As for the obligatory charity, it is better to disclose it so that one may follow an example and so that one is not accused, and giving it to the poor is obligatory, and He will expiate with the letter ya and the letter nun, in the jussive mood, in apposition to the place of fawwa and in the nominative case as a resumption, from you some of your misdeeds, and God is Acquainted with what you do, knowing its inner meaning as its outer meaning, nothing of it is hidden from Him.
2:272: And when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was forbidden from giving charity to the polytheists so that they would convert to Islam, it was revealed: You are not responsible for their guidance meaning the people to enter Islam, you are only responsible for conveying the message but God guides whom He wills guiding him to enter it and whatever you spend of good money it is for yourselves because its reward is for it and you do not spend except seeking the countenance of God meaning His reward and nothing else from the worldly matters, a report with the meaning of prohibition and whatever you spend of good it will be fully repaid to you its reward and you will not be wronged you will not be deducted from it in any way, and the two sentences are an emphasis on the first
2:273: For the poor is the predicate of a deleted subject, meaning the alms who are restricted in the cause of God meaning they confined themselves to jihad, it was revealed about the people of the Suffa, who were four hundred of the emigrants who were assigned to learn the Qur’an and go out with the raiding parties who are unable to travel travel in the land for trade and livelihood because they were busy with jihad the ignorant one thinks them in their condition rich because of their self-restraint meaning because of their self-restraint from asking and leaving it you will recognize them O addressee by their mark their sign of humility and the effect of effort they do not ask people anything, so they are persistent persistently meaning they do not ask at all, so they do not persist, which is insistence and whatever you spend of good - indeed, God is Knowing of it so it is a reward for it
2:274: Those who spend their wealth by night and by day, secretly and publicly will have their reward with their Lord, and no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.
2:275: Those who consume interest that is, take it, which is the increase in transactions with money and food in quantity or term will not rise from their graves except rising as one rises who is being beaten overcome by Satan madness, related to they rise that that which has befallen them because because they said, Trade is just like usury in permissibility, and this is from the opposite of simile, exaggeration, so God Almighty said in response to them (and God has permitted trade and forbidden usury. So whoever comes to him) conveys to him an admonition an admonition from his Lord and desists from consuming it will have what is past before the prohibition, that is, it will not be taken back from him and his command in forgiving him (is up to God. But whoever returns) to consuming it, likening it to selling in permissibility they are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally
2:276: God destroys usury He reduces it and takes away its blessing and gives increase for charities He increases them, makes them grow, and multiplies their reward and God does not like any disbeliever by permitting usury and sinful by consuming it, i.e. He punishes him.
2:277: (Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds and established prayer and given zakah will have their reward with their Lord, and no fear will there be concerning them, nor will they grieve.)
2:278: O you who have believed, fear God and give up leave what remains of interest if you should be believers sincere in your faith, for it is the nature of the believer to obey the command of God Almighty. It was revealed when some of the companions demanded interest that they had previously after the prohibition.
2:279: But if you do not do what you have been commanded then be permitted know a war from God and His Messenger for you in it is a severe threat to them and when it was revealed they said we have no hand in waging war against him and if you repent turn away from it then you shall have the principal the roots of your wealth - you shall not wrong with an addition nor shall you be wronged with a reduction
2:280: And if a debtor is in hardship, then grant him respite that is, you must delay it until it is easy with the opening of the seen and its closing, that is, a time of ease and that you give in charity with emphasis on the assimilation of the ta’ in the original into the sad and with the lightening of its deletion, that is, give charity to the one in difficulty by releasing him that is better for you if you only knew that it is better, so do it. And in the hadith: Whoever grants respite to one in hardship or forgives him, God will shade him in His shade on the day when there is no shade but His shade. Narrated by Muslim
2:281: And fear a Day when you will be returned in the passive form you will be returned and in the active form you will be proceeded to God it is the Day of Resurrection then every soul will be compensated in it for what it earned of good and evil and they will not be wronged by a reduction in a good deed or an increase in a bad deed.
2:282: O you who have believed, when you contract a debt such as a loan or a loan for a specified term known then write it down as a security and to prevent dispute and let a scribe write the debt book between you in justice with the truth in writing it, neither increasing nor decreasing the money or the term and let no scribe refuse refrain from writing when he is called upon to do so as God has taught him that is, He has favored him with writing so he does not withhold it, and the kaf is related to ib so let him write to emphasize and let the scribe dictate dictate to the one upon whom the debt is owed the debt because he is the one against whom the witness is testified, so he acknowledges so that he knows what he owes and let him fear God, his Lord in dictating and not withhold decrease from it that is the right (anything. But if the one upon whom the debt is owed is foolish) spendthrift or weak from dictating due to young or old or is unable to dictate himself due to muteness or ignorance of the language or something like that then let his guardian dictate the one in charge of his affairs From a father, guardian, trustee, and interpreter with justice and bring to witness bear witness to the religion two witnesses witnesses from among your men that is, adult, free Muslims and if there are not two that is, the two witnesses then a man and two women who bear witness from among those whom you accept as witnesses for his religion and justice and the multiplicity of women so that lest one of them should go astray forget the testimony due to the deficiency of their intellect and control then remind one of them) the memory of the other the forgetful, and the sentence of reminding is the place of the cause, that is, to remind if she goes astray and enters into error because it is its cause. And in a reading with a kasra {in} conditional and raising the remembrance is a resumption of its answer and let not the witnesses refuse when redundant they are called to bear witness and give it and do not tire get tired of writing it down that is, what you have witnessed of the truth due to the frequency of that whether it is small it was or great little or much until its term the time of its occurrence is a state of the ha in you write it down that that is, the writings The most just the most equitable with God and the most upright for testimony that is, the most helpful in establishing it because He mentions it and the closest to A N do not doubt doubt the amount of the right and the term unless it is it occurs a present trade and in a reading it is in the accusative, so it is incomplete and its name is the pronoun of trade which you conduct among yourselves that is, you receive it and there is no term in it then there is no blame upon you in A N do not write it down and what is meant by it is the trade in it and bring witnesses when you make a transaction on it because it prevents disagreement. This and what preceded it are a command of encouragement and neither the scribe nor the witness the owner of the right and the one against him shall be harmed by distortion or refusal to testify or write, and the owner of the right shall not harm them by charging them with what is not appropriate in writing and testimony and if you do what you have been forbidden then it is disobedience departure from obedience, not right (to you. And fear God) in His command and prohibition and God will teach you the interests of your affairs, a state that is estimated or renewed and God is Knowing of all things
2:283: And if you are on a journey that is, traveling and you have borrowed and cannot find a scribe, then a pledge and in the reading {Frihan} the plural of Rahn received you secure it, and the Sunnah has shown the permissibility of a pledge in the city and the presence of a scribe, so adherence to what was mentioned is because the security in it is more severe, and his saying received indicates the condition of possession in the pledge and sufficing with it from the mortgagee and his agent But if one of you trusts another that is, the creditor the debtor regarding his right and does not take a pledge then let him who is trusted that is, the debtor deliver his trust his debt and let him fear God his Lord in fulfilling it and do not conceal testimony if you are called upon to give it and whoever conceals it, then indeed his heart is sinful He was specifically mentioned because it is the place of testimony and because if he sins, someone else will follow him and he will be punished with the punishment of sinners and God is Knowing of what you do nothing of it is hidden from Him
2:284: (To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Whether you disclose) show what is in your souls of evil and the intention to do it or conceal it keep it secret He will bring you to account inform you of it on the Day of Resurrection and forgive whom He wills forgiveness for him and punish whom He wills his punishment and the two verbs in the jussive mood are in apposition to the answer to the condition and the nominative, meaning He is and God is over all things competent and from it is your accountability and your reward
2:285: He believed he believed the Messenger Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace in what was revealed to him from his Lord of the Qur’an and the believers the tanween is attached to it each its tanween is a substitute for the complement to it believes in God and His angels and His books in the plural and singular and His messengers they say we make no distinction between any of His messengers so we believe in some and disbelieve in others as the Jews and Christians did and they say, we hear that is, what we were commanded to hear and accept and we obey we ask You for Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the destination the reference is to resurrection, and when the verse before it was revealed, the believers complained about the whispers and it was difficult for them to be held accountable for them, so this was revealed:
2:286: (God does not charge a soul except [with that within] its capacity. It shall have [the reward of] what it has earned, and it shall bear [the burden of] what it has acquired, of evil, and no one shall be punished for the sin of another or for what he has not earned, which his soul has whispered to him. Say, Our Lord, do not punish us with punishment if we have forgotten or erred if we have left what is right, not intentionally, as those before us were punished for it. God has lifted that from this nation, as mentioned in the hadith. So his asking is an acknowledgment of God's blessing. Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden something that is too heavy for us to bear as You laid upon those before us meaning the Children of Israel, such as killing a soul in repentance, giving a quarter of one's wealth in zakat, and lending a place of impurity. Our Lord, and lay not upon us that which we have no ability to bear of duties and trials and pardon us erase our sins and forgive us and have mercy upon us in mercy in addition to forgiveness You are our Master our Master and the One who takes charge of our affairs. (So grant us victory over the disbelieving people) by establishing proof and victory in fighting them, because it is the nature of a master to grant victory to his masters over the enemies. In the hadith, “When this verse was revealed and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited it, it was said to him after every word, ‘You have done it.’”
3:1: Alif, Lam, Meem God knows best what He meant by that.
3:2: God, there is no god but He, the Ever-Living, the Self-Sustaining
3:3: He has sent down to you O Muhammad the Book the Qur’an, mixed with the truth with honesty in its report confirming what was before it before it of the books and He sent down the Torah and the Gospel
3:4: Before meaning before its revelation guidance is a state meaning two guides from misguidance for the people who followed them, and He expressed them with the word revealed and in the Qur’an with the word revealed which requires repetition because they were revealed all at once, unlike it and He revealed the Criterion meaning the books that distinguish between truth and falsehood, and He mentioned it after mentioning the three to include everything else Indeed, those who disbelieve in the verses of God the Qur’an and other than them will have a severe punishment, and God is Exalted in Might dominant over His affair, so nothing prevents Him from fulfilling His promise and His threat possessor of retribution severe punishment for those who disobey Him, the like of which no one is capable of.
3:5: Indeed, nothing is hidden from God that exists on the earth or in the heavens because of His knowledge of what occurs in the world of the general and the particular, and He singled them out for mention because the senses do not go beyond them.
3:6: It is He who shapes you in the wombs however He wills of maleness and femaleness, whiteness and blackness, etc. There is no god but He, the Exalted in Might in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
3:7: 7 - It is He who has sent down to you the Book, in it are verses that are precise clear in meaning they are the foundation of the Book its origin that is relied upon in rulings and others that are ambiguous whose meanings are not understood like the beginnings of the surahs and He made it all precise in His statement {His verses are precise} meaning that there is no defect in it, and ambiguous in His statement {a similar Book} meaning that some of it resembles others in goodness and truth But as for those in whose hearts is deviation deviation from the truth they follow that which is ambiguous of it, seeking seeking trial for their ignorance by falling into doubts and confusion and seeking its interpretation its explanation and none knows its interpretation its explanation except God alone and those firmly rooted the steadfast and established in knowledge subject and predicate they say, We believe in it meaning in the ambiguous that it is from God and we do not know its meaning all of the precise and ambiguous are from our Lord and that which is reminded by assimilating the ta’ in The root of the word dhal is to be admonished except those of understanding those with intellects. They also say when they see someone following him:
3:8: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate do not make them deviate from the truth by seeking an interpretation of it that is not befitting for us, as You have deviated the hearts of those after You have guided us directed us to it and grant us from Yourself from You mercy as confirmation for You are the Bestower
3:9: O Our Lord, You will surely gather mankind You will gather them for a Day that is, on a Day about which there is no doubt there is no doubt and You will reward them for their deeds as You promised (Indeed, God does not fail in His promise His promise of resurrection in it is a shift from the address and it is possible that it is from His words, the Most High, and the purpose of supplicating with that is to show that their concern is the matter of the Hereafter and therefore they asked for steadfastness in guidance so that they may attain its reward. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of Aisha, may God be pleased with her, she said: “The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, recited this verse: {It is He who has sent down to you, [O Muhammad], the Book, in it are verses that are precise} until the end of it, and he said: So when you see those who follow that which is ambiguous in it, then those are the ones whom God has named, so beware of them.” At-Tabarani narrated in Al-Kabeer on the authority of Abu Musa Al-Ash’ari that he heard the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, say: “I do not fear for my nation except three things, and he mentioned among them that the Book will be opened for them and the believer will take it seeking its interpretation, and none knows its interpretation except God, and those firmly rooted in knowledge will say, ‘We believe.’” All of it is from our Lord and is not mentioned to those of understanding.” Hadith
3:10: Indeed, those who disbelieve - neither their wealth nor their children will avail them at all against God i.e. His punishment and those are the fuel of the Fire with the opening of the waw, what is kindled with it
3:11: Their habit like the habit like the habit of the people of Pharaoh and those before them of the nations like Aad and Thamud denied Our signs, so God seized them destroyed them for their sins and the sentence explains what came before it and God is severe in penalty and it was revealed when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, ordered the Jews to convert to Islam after his return from Badr, so they said to him, do not be deceived by the fact that you killed a group of Quraysh who were ignorant and did not know how to fight:
3:12: Say O Muhammad to those who disbelieve from the Jews You will be overcome with the ta’ and the ya’ in this world by killing, captivity, and the imposition of the jizya tax, and that has already happened and you will be gathered with both faces in the Hereafter to Hell so you will enter it and wretched is the resting place the bed is it.
3:13: There has already been for you a sign a lesson, and the verb is mentioned for the purpose of separation in two companies two groups that met on the day of Badr to fight one company fighting in the way of God that is, obeying Him, and they were the Prophet and his companions, and they were three hundred and thirteen men with them horsemen, six shields, and eight swords, and most of them were men and another disbelieving company who saw them that is, the disbelievers twice as many as they were that is, the Muslims, that is, more than them, and they were about a thousand with the eye that is, a clear, direct vision, and God aided them despite their small number and God supports strengthens (with His aid whom He wills. Indeed in that) that is mentioned is a lesson for those of insight those with insight, so will you not take heed of that and believe?
3:14: Beautified for people is the love of desires what the soul desires and calls for, God or Satan has made it beautiful of women and children and heaps of wealth heaped up of gold and silver and branded horses) of beautiful livestock) meaning camels, cows and sheep and tillage crops that mentioned is the enjoyment of worldly life which is enjoyed in it and then perishes and God has with Him the best destination the reference, which is Paradise, so it should be desired over anything else
3:15: Say O Muhammad, to your people Shall I inform you tell you of something better than that mentioned of desires, a question of confirmation for those who fear polytheism with their Lord predicate of its subject Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein that is, destined for eternity and purified spouses from menstruation and other things that are disgusting and approval with the first letter broken and the first letter closed, two languages, that is, abundant approval from God? And God is Seeing Knowing of the servants so He rewards each of them for his deeds.
3:16: Those are an adjective or substitute for those before it who say O Our Lord, indeed we have believed we have believed in You and in Your Messenger so forgive us our sins and protect us from the punishment of the Fire
3:17: The patient ones in obedience and against disobedience is described and the truthful ones in faith and the obedient ones those who obey God and the spenders those who give charity and those who seek forgiveness from God by saying, O God, forgive us in the early hours of the night the last part of the night is specifically mentioned because it is the time of heedlessness and the pleasure of sleep.
3:18: God bears witness He made clear to His creation with evidence and signs that there is no god that is, there is no true god in existence except Him and the angels bear witness to that and those of knowledge from the prophets and the believers with belief and the wording establishing in managing His creations and its accusative is in the state and the agent in it has the meaning of that is, He is unique in justice in fairness there is no god except Him He repeated it for emphasis the Almighty in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
3:19: The religion that is pleasing to God is Islam meaning the law that the messengers were sent with, based on monotheism. In a reading with the opening of {an} instead of that, etc. instead of including And those who were given the Scripture the Jews and Christians did not differ in religion, with some believing in monotheism and some disbelieving except after knowledge had come to them of monotheism out of envy on the part of the disbelievers (among themselves. And whoever disbelieves in the verses of God - then indeed, God is swift in account) meaning in punishing him.
3:20: So if they dispute with you the infidels dispute with you, O Muhammad, about religion say to them I have submitted my face to God I have submitted to Him and whoever follows me and the face was mentioned specifically for its honor, so others are more deserving and say to those who were given the Scripture the Jews and Christians and the unlettered the polytheists of the Arabs Have you submitted that is, have you submitted? If they have submitted, then they have been guided from misguidance but if they turn away from Islam then upon you is only the conveyance conveying the message and God is Seeing of the servants so He will reward them for their deeds, and this was before the command to fight.
3:21: Indeed, those who disbelieve in the verses of God and kill and in another reading {fight} the prophets without right and kill those who enjoin justice with fairness among the people and they are the Jews. It was narrated that they killed forty-three prophets, so one hundred and seventy of their servants forbade them, so they killed them that day then give them tidings inform them of a painful punishment painful, and mentioning the tidings is to mock them, and the fa’ entered into the report of inna due to the similarity of their name connected by the condition.
3:22: Those are the ones whose deeds have become worthless their good deeds such as charity and maintaining family ties in this world and the Hereafter are not to be taken into account because they are not a condition and they will have no helpers to prevent the punishment.
3:23: Have you not seen you look at those who were given a portion a share of the Scripture the Torah they call a state to the Book of God to judge between them, then a party of them turns away while they are averse from accepting His judgment. It was revealed about the Jews. Two of them committed adultery, so they brought their case to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he ruled that they be stoned, but they refused. Then the Torah was brought and found in it, so they were stoned, and they became angry.
3:24: That turning away and turning away because they said that is, because of their saying The Fire will not touch us except for a few days forty days, the period during which their fathers worshipped the calf, then it will be removed from them and their deception in their religion is related to his saying what they used to invent from their saying that
3:25: How will they be (when We gather them for a Day) that is, on a Day about which there is no doubt that is, the Day of Resurrection and every soul from the People of the Book and others will be fully compensated for what it earned) it did of good and evil and they that is, the people will not be wronged by a reduction in good or an increase in bad.
3:26: And it was revealed when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, promised his nation the kingdom of Persia and Rome, and the hypocrites said, It is impossible. Say, O God, Owner of the Kingdom, You give the kingdom to whomever You will of Your creation, (and You take the kingdom away from whomever You will, and You exalt whomever You will by giving it and You abase whomever You will by taking it away from him. In Your hand by Your power is good that is, evil. Indeed, You are over all things competent.
3:27: You bring the night into the day and You bring the day into the night, each of them increases by what the other lacks and You bring forth the living from the dead like the human being and the bird from the sperm and the egg and You bring forth the dead like the sperm and the egg from the living and You provide for whom You will without account meaning abundant provision.
3:28: Let not the believers take the disbelievers as allies to befriend them instead of that is, other than (the believers. And whoever does that) that is, befriends them then there is no religion of God in anything except that you fear them with fear the source of taqiyyah, that is, you fear fear, so you may befriend them with the tongue rather than the heart. This was before the glory of Islam and it applies to someone who is in a country in which he is not strong. And God warns you frightens you of Himself that He will be angry with you if you befriend them. And to God is the final destination the reference, so He will reward you.
3:29: Say to them if you conceal what is in your hearts your hearts of their allegiance or reveal it show it God knows it and He knows what is in the heavens and what is on the earth, and God is over all things competent and from this is the punishment of those who allegiance to them
3:30: Remember the Day when every soul will find present what it has done of good and what it has done of evil subject and predicate it will wish that between it and that was a great distance ultimate limit that cannot be reached and God warns you of Himself repeated for emphasis and God is Kind to His servants
3:31: And it was revealed when they said, We worship idols only out of love for God, so that they may bring us closer to Him. Say to them, O Muhammad, If you love God, then follow me, and God will love you, meaning that He will reward you, (and forgive you your sins. And God is Forgiving and Merciful) to those who follow me, for what has come before that.
3:32: Say to them Obey God and the Messenger in what he commands you to do regarding monotheism But if they turn away turn away from obedience then God does not like the disbelievers in it is the use of the apparent in place of the implicit, meaning He does not like them in the sense that He will punish them.
3:33: Indeed, God chose He selected Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of Imran meaning themselves over the worlds by making the prophets from their descendants.
3:34: Some of their descendants are from some of their offspring and God is All-Hearing, All-Knowing
3:35: Remember when the wife of Imran said Hannah when she had grown old and longed for a child, so she called upon God and felt pregnant, O my Lord, I have vowed to make for You what is in my womb, dedicated free from the concerns of this world, to serve Your Holy House so accept it from me, for You are the Hearer of supplication the Knower of intentions, and Imran died while she was pregnant.
3:36: And when she gave birth to her a girl gave birth to her and she was hoping that it would be a boy since only boys were freed she said apologetically O my Lord, indeed I have given birth to a female, and God knows best meaning He knows what she has given birth to an interjection from His words, the Most High. In a reading with a damma on the ta’ and the male that she requested is not like the female that she was given because it is intended for service and she is not suitable due to her weakness and nakedness and what befalls her from menstruation and the like and indeed I have named her Mary, and I seek refuge for her and her offspring her children from Satan the accursed expelled in the hadith No child is born except that Satan touches him when he is born and he cries out screaming, except Mary and her son narrated by the two sheikhs
3:37: So her Lord accepted her meaning He accepted Mary from her mother with good acceptance and made her grow in a good manner He created her with good character so she grew in a day as a newborn grows in a year and her mother brought her to the rabbis, the guardians of the Holy House and said: Here is this vowed one so they competed over her because she is the daughter of their leader. Zachariah said: I have more right to her because her maternal aunt is with me. They said: No, not until we draw lots. So they set out and they were twenty-nine to the Jordan River and cast their pens on the condition that whoever’s pen remained in the water and ascended would be more deserving of her. So Zachariah’s pen remained, so he took her and built a room for her in the mosque with a ladder that no one else could ascend to. He would bring her food, drink and oil and would find summer fruits in the winter and winter fruits in the summer as God Almighty said And Zachariah took charge of her he joined her to him and in a reading with emphasis and Zachariah’s accusative is extended and shortened and the subject is God Whenever Zachariah entered upon her the sanctuary the room which is the most honorable of gatherings (he found with her Provision. He said, O Mary, from where did you get this? She said, while she was young, It is from God. He brings it to me from Paradise. God provides for whom He wills without account. Ample provision without any obligation.
3:38: There meaning when Zachariah saw that and knew that the One who is able to bring about something at the wrong time is able to bring about a child in old age and his family had become extinct Zachariah called upon his Lord when he entered the sanctuary to pray in the middle of the night (He said: My Lord, grant me from Yourself) from You a good offspring a righteous child You are indeed the Hearer the Answerer of supplication
3:39: Then the angels called to him i.e. Gabriel while he was standing praying in the sanctuary i.e. the mosque that i.e. with that and in a reading with the kasra with the estimation of the saying God gives you good tidings heavy and light of John, confirming a word that is from God i.e. Jesus, that he is the spirit of God and he was called a word because he was created with the word Be and a master followed and chaste forbidden from women and a prophet from among the righteous it was narrated that he did not commit a sin and did not intend to do so
3:40: (He said: My Lord, how can I have a boy while I have reached old age and my wife is barren?) She has reached ninety-eight years. (He said: Thus it is.) Whoever created a boy from you two, God does what He wills. Nothing is beyond His power. To demonstrate this great power, He inspired him to ask in order to be answered. And when his soul yearned for the haste of the one who was to be given glad tidings,
3:41: He said, My Lord, make for me a sign meaning a sign of my wife's pregnancy. He said, Your sign for him is that he will not speak to the people meaning refrain from speaking to them, other than mentioning God Almighty for three days meaning their nights except by gesture allusion and remember your Lord much and glorify pray in the evening and the morning late in the day and early in the morning.
3:42: And remember when the angels said meaning Gabriel O Mary, indeed God has chosen you selected you and purified you from the touch of men and chosen you above the women of the worlds meaning the people of your time.
3:43: O Mary, be devout toward your Lord Obey Him and prostrate and bow with those who bow i.e. pray with those who pray.
3:44: That mentioned about the matter of Zachariah and Mary is from the report of the unseen report of what is hidden from you We reveal it to you O Muhammad and you were not with them when they cast their pens into the water, casting lots to show them which of them would be responsible raise Mary. Nor were you with them when they disputed) about her responsibility, so you knew that and informed about it. You only knew it through the direction of revelation.
3:45: When the angels said meaning Gabriel O Mary, indeed God gives you good tidings of a word from Him meaning a son whose name is the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary He addressed her by attributing him to her, indicating that she would give birth to him without a father, since it is the custom of men to attribute themselves to their fathers honorable of status in this world through prophethood and in the Hereafter through intercession and high ranks and among those brought near to God.
3:46: And he will speak to the people in the cradle that is, as a child before the time of speaking and as an adult and among the righteous
3:47: (She said, My Lord, how can I have a son while no man has touched me? He said, Thus, who created a child from you without a father? God creates what He wills when He decrees a matter. He only says to it, Be, and it is.) That is, it is.
3:48: And We will teach him with the letter noon and the letter ya the Book the line and wisdom and the Torah and the Gospel
3:49: And We made him a messenger to the Children of Israel in youth or after puberty, so Gabriel blew into the pocket of her garment and she became pregnant, and what happened to her was what was mentioned in Surah Maryam. So when God sent him to the Children of Israel, he said to them: I am the Messenger of God to you that I that is, that I have come to you with a sign a sign of my truthfulness from your Lord which is that I and in a reading with the kasra as a resumption create I form for you from clay like the form of a bird so the kaf is a passive participle then I breathe into it the pronoun is for the kaf and it becomes a bird and in a reading a bird by God’s permission by His will, so He created for them the bat because He perfected the bird in creation, so it would fly while they watched it, and when it disappeared from their sight, it would fall dead so that the action of the created would be distinguished from the action of the Creator, who is God Almighty, and so that it would be known that perfection belongs to God and I heal I cure the blind who was born blind and the leper and they were specifically mentioned because they are a disease of exhaustion and He sent him in the time of medicine, so he healed fifty thousand people in one day by supplication on the condition of faith and I bring the dead to life by God’s permission he repeated it to negate the illusion of divinity in him, so he brought back to life Lazarus, a friend of his, the son of the old woman, and the daughter of the tenth, so they lived and had children, and Sam, the son of Noah, who died immediately and I inform you of what you eat and what you store you hide in your houses of what I did not see, so he would inform the person of what he ate and what he would eat after there is in that mentioned a sign for you if you are believers
3:50: And I have come to you confirming what came before me before me of the Torah and to make lawful for you some of what was forbidden to you in it, so He made lawful for them from the fish and birds what has no sect. It was said that He made all of them lawful, so some means all. And I have come to you with a sign from your Lord He repeated it for emphasis and to build upon it So fear God and obey me in what I command you of monotheism of God and obedience to Him.
3:51: (Indeed, God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This) that I command you is a straight path but they denied him and did not believe in him.
3:52: And when Jesus perceived he knew their disbelief and they wanted to kill him (he said: Who are my supporters) my supporters going to God to support His religion (the disciples said: We are the supporters of God) the supporters of His religion and they are the chosen ones of Jesus, the first to believe in him and they were twelve men of the houris which is pure whiteness and it was said that they were shorts who whitened the clothes we believe we believe in God and bear witness O Jesus that we are Muslims
3:53: Our Lord, we have believed in what You revealed of the Gospel and we have followed the Messenger Jesus so register us among the witnesses to Your Oneness and to Your Messenger’s truthfulness.
3:54: God Almighty said: And they plotted meaning the infidels of the Children of Israel against Jesus when they assigned someone to kill him by treachery and God plotted against them by casting the likeness of Jesus on the one who intended to kill him, so they killed him and Jesus was raised to heaven and God is the best of plotters He knows him best.
3:55: Remember (when God said, O Jesus, indeed I will take you and raise you to Me from this world without death and purify you from those who disbelieve and make those who follow you, those who believe in your prophethood from among the Muslims and Christians, superior to those who disbelieve, until the Day of Resurrection. Then to Me is your return, and I will judge between you concerning that over which you used to differ.") of the matter of religion.
3:56: As for those who disbelieve, I will punish them with a severe punishment in this world by killing, captivity, and the jizya and in the Hereafter by fire and they will have no helpers to protect them from it.
3:57: And as for those who believe and do righteous deeds, He will pay them their rewards in full with the letter Ya and the letter Noon and God does not love the wrongdoers meaning He will punish them. It was narrated that God Almighty sent a cloud to him and lifted him up, and his mother clung to him and cried, so he said to her, The Resurrection will gather us together. That was on the Night of Decree in Jerusalem, and he was thirty-three years old, and his mother lived six years after him. The two sheikhs narrated the hadith, He will descend near the Hour and rule according to the law of our Prophet, and kill the Antichrist and the pig, and break the cross, and abolish the jizyah. In a hadith of Muslim, it is stated that he will remain for seven years, and in a hadith from Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi, forty years, and he will die and the funeral prayer will be performed over him. It is possible that what is meant is the totality of his stay on earth before and after being lifted up.
3:58: That mentioned about the matter of Jesus We recite it We relate it to you O Muhammad of the verses a state of the ha’ in we recite it and its factor is what is in that of the meaning of indication and the wise remembrance the decisive, meaning the Qur’an
3:59: The likeness of Jesus his strange affair in the sight of God is like the likeness of Adam as his affair in his creation without a mother or a father, and this is from likening the strange to the strangest in order to be more decisive for the opponent and more effective in the soul He created him from dust, then said to him, Be, a human being and he was, that is, and he was. And likewise Jesus, He said to him, Be, without a father, and he was.
3:60: The truth is from your Lord is the predicate of a deleted subject, i.e. the command of Jesus so do not be among those who doubt those who doubt it.
3:61: So whoever disputes with you argues with you from the Christians about it after what has come to you of knowledge about its matter say to them Come, let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, ourselves and yourselves so we gather them then let us supplicate we implore in supplication and invoke the curse of God upon the liars by saying: O God, curse the liar regarding the matter of Jesus. And the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, called the delegation of Najran for that when they argued with him about it and they said: Until we look into our matter then we will come to you. So the one with their opinion said: You have known his prophethood and that no people have challenged a prophet except that they perished. So they made peace with the man and left and came to the Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he had come out with al-Hasan, al-Husayn, Fatimah and Ali and he said to them: When you call, then say Amen. So they refused to curse and made peace with him on the jizyah. Narrated by Abu Nu`aym, and on the authority of Ibn Abbas, he said: If those who challenged the jizyah had gone out, they would have returned without finding money or Hello, and it was narrated: If they had gone out, they would have been burned.
3:62: Indeed, this mentioned is the true story the report about which there is no doubt (and there is no god except God, and indeed, God is the Exalted in Might) in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
3:63: But if they turn away turn away from faith then indeed, God is Knowing of the corrupters and He will punish them. In this, the apparent is placed in place of the implicit.
3:64: Say, O People of the Scripture Jews and Christians Come to a word that is common between us and you which is A N We do not worship except God and do not associate anything with Him and do not take one another as lords besides God as you took the rabbis and monks But if they turn away turn away from monotheism then say you to them Bear witness that we are Muslims monotheists
3:65: And it was revealed when the Jews said: Abraham is a Jew and we are of his religion, and the Christians said likewise: O People of the Scripture, why do you argue quarrel about Abraham on the grounds that he is of your religion and the Torah and the Gospel were not revealed until after him a long time after their revelation, and after Judaism and Christianity occurred Do you not understand the invalidity of your statement
3:66: Ha for emphasis you is the subject, O these and the predicate you have argued about that of which you have knowledge of the matter of Moses and Jesus and your claim that you are of their religion so why do you argue about that of which you have no knowledge of the matter of Abraham and God knows his matter and you do not know God Almighty said, exonerating Abraham:
3:67: Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian, but he was one inclining toward truth inclined away from all religions to the true religion a Muslim a monotheist and he was not of the polytheists
3:68: The people who have the most right to Abraham are those who followed him in his time and this Prophet Muhammad because he agreed with him in most of his law and those who believed from his nation, so they are the ones who should say we are on his religion, not you and God is the protector of the believers their supporter and protector.
3:69: And it was revealed when the Jews called Muadh, Hudhayfah, and Ammar to their religion: A party of the People of the Scripture wish they could mislead you, but they mislead only themselves because the sin of misleading them is upon them and the believers do not obey them in it and they do not perceive that.
3:70: O People of the Scripture, why do you disbelieve in the verses of God the Qur’an that includes the description of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace while you bear witness you know that it is the truth
3:71: O People of the Scripture, why do you mix truth with falsehood) through distortion and forgery and conceal the truth that is, the description of the Prophet while you know that it is the truth?
3:72: And a party of the People of the Scripture said the Jews to one another Believe in what was revealed to those who believe that is, the Qur’an at the beginning of the day its beginning and disbelieve in it at its end that perhaps they that is, the believers will return from their religion, since they say that these people did not return from it after entering it, and they are people of knowledge, except because they knew its falsehood.
3:73: And they also said and do not believe give charity except to one the lam is redundant follows agrees with your religion God Almighty said: Say to them, O Muhammad that guidance is the guidance of God which is Islam and everything else is misguidance, and the sentence is an interjection that meaning that that someone is given the like of what you were given of the Book, wisdom and virtues, and that is the object of believe, and the exception from it is someone preceded by the exception meaning: Do not acknowledge that anyone is given that except to one who follows your religion or that they argue with you meaning the believers will defeat you before your Lord on the Day of Resurrection because you are more correct in religion, and in the reading: that with the hamza of rebuke meaning that giving someone the like of it you acknowledge God Almighty said: (Say: Indeed, the bounty is in God’s hand. He gives it to whom He wills) so how do you know that no one is given the like of what you were given and God is Bountiful abundant in bounty Knowing of who is worthy of it
3:74: He chooses for His mercy whom He wills, and God is the possessor of great bounty.
3:75: And among the People of the Scripture is he who, if you entrust him with a great amount of money, will return it to you for his trustworthiness, like Abdullah ibn Salam, to whom a man entrusted one thousand two hundred ounces of gold, and he returned it to him. And among them is he who, if you entrust him with a dinar, will not return it to you for his betrayal unless you stand over him and do not leave him, and when you leave him, he denies it. Like Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, to whom a Qurayshite entrusted a dinar, and he denied it. That that is, he did not return it because they said because of their saying There is no way for us concerning the unlettered ones that is, the Arabs to do that is, sin, because they consider permissible the oppression of those who oppose their religion and attribute it to God Almighty. God Almighty said And they tell a lie about God in attributing that to Him while they know.
3:76: Yes there is a way for them in it whoever fulfills his covenant that which he made a covenant with or God’s covenant with him of fulfilling the trust and other things and fears God by abandoning sins and performing acts of obedience then indeed, God loves the righteous in it He puts the apparent in place of the implicit, meaning He loves them in the sense that He rewards them
3:77: And it was revealed about the Jews when they changed the description of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God’s covenant with them in the Torah, and about those who swore falsely in a claim or in the sale of goods: Indeed, those who purchase they exchange God’s covenant to them in belief in the Prophet and fulfilling the trust and their oaths their false oaths by Him, the Most High for a small price of this world they will have no portion share in the Hereafter, and God will not speak to them in anger nor look at them have mercy on them on the Day of Resurrection, nor will He purify them cleanse them and they will have a painful punishment painful.
3:78: And among them meaning the People of the Book is a party a group like Ka`b ibn al-Ashraf who twist their tongues with the Book meaning they bend it in reading it from the revealed to what they distorted of the description of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and the like so that you may think it meaning the distorted is from the Book that God revealed and it is not from the Book and they say, It is from God and it is not from God and they tell a lie about God while they know that they are lying.
3:79: And it was revealed when the Christians of Najran said that Jesus had commanded them to take him as a lord, and when some of the Muslims asked to prostrate to him, may God bless him and grant him peace. It is not befitting for a human being that God should give him the Scripture and wisdom meaning understanding of the Sharia and prophethood and then say to the people, Be worshippers of me instead of God. Rather he says be rabbis working scholars attributed to the Lord by adding an alif and a nun for emphasis because you used to know with emphasis and emphasis the Scripture and because you used to study meaning because of that, its benefit is that you work.
3:80: And He does not command you in the nominative case as a resumption, meaning God, and the accusative case as an apposition to He says, meaning mankind that you take the angels and the prophets as lords as the Sabians took the angels, the Jews took Ezra, and the Christians took Jesus Does He command you to disbelieve after you have been Muslims this is not appropriate for Him.
3:81: And mention when when God took the covenant of the prophets their pledge when with the opening of the lam for the beginning and to emphasize the meaning of the oath that is in taking the covenant and its breaking is related to taking and what is connected in both ways, meaning to what I gave you it, and in a reading {We gave you} of the Book and wisdom, then there came to you a Messenger confirming what is with you of the Book and wisdom, and he is Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace that you must believe in him and support him the answer to the oath if you reach him and their nations follow them in that He said the Most High to them Have you acknowledged that and taken accepted upon that My covenant My covenant they said we have acknowledged. He said then bear witness) against yourselves and your followers of that and I am with you among the witnesses against you and against them
3:82: So whoever turns away rejects after that the covenant then those are the wicked
3:83: Do they seek other than the religion of God with the letter ya and the letter ta, meaning the followers while to Him has submitted obeyed whoever is in the heavens and the earth, willingly without refusal and by compulsion with the sword and witnessing what he resorts to and to Him you will be returned with the letter ta and the letter ya and the hamza at the beginning of the verse for denial
3:84: Say to them, O Muhammad, We believe in God and what was revealed to us and what was revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes his sons (and what was given to Moses and Jesus and the prophets from their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them) in belief or denial and we are Muslims [in submission] to Him sincere in worship. And it was revealed about those who apostatized and joined the infidels:
3:85: And whoever desires other than Islam as religion - never will it be accepted from him, and he, in the Hereafter, will be among the losers because he will be destined for the Fire that is being supported against him.
3:86: How that is, does not God guide a people who disbelieved after their belief and bore witness that is, their testimony that the Messenger is true and that clear proofs have come to them the apparent arguments for the truthfulness of the Prophet and God does not guide the wrongdoing people that is, the disbelievers.
3:87: Their recompense is that upon them is the curse of God and the angels and the people, all together.
3:88: They will abide therein meaning the curse or fire indicated by it the punishment will not be lightened for them, nor will they be reprieved they will be given respite
3:89: Except for those who repent after that and amend their deeds then indeed, God is Forgiving to them and Merciful to them.
3:90: And it was revealed about the Jews Indeed, those who disbelieved in Jesus after their belief in Moses and then increased in disbelief in Muhammad their repentance will not be accepted if they gurgle or die as disbelievers and it is those who are astray
3:91: Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers - never will it be accepted from one of them an earth full of gold, even if he were to ransom himself with it. The letter fa was inserted in the predicate of inna to resemble those who are with the condition and to indicate that the cause of the non-acceptance of death in disbelief is (those will have a painful punishment. And they will have no helpers.) to prevent it.
3:92: You will not attain righteousness meaning its reward, which is Paradise until you spend give charity out of what you love of your money and whatever you spend, indeed, God is Knowing of it so He will reward for it.
3:93: And it was revealed when the Jews said that you claim that you follow the religion of Abraham and he did not eat camel meat or milk: All food was lawful permissible for the Children of Israel except what Israel Jacob forbade himself which is camels when he had sciatica with the fat-ha and the qasr, so he vowed that if he recovered he would not eat them, so they were forbidden to him before the Torah was revealed and that was after Abraham and it was not forbidden during his time as they claimed Say to them Then bring the Torah and recite it so that the truth of your statement if you are truthful in it becomes clear, so they were dumbfounded and did not bring it. God Almighty said:
3:94: So whoever invents a lie against God after that meaning the emergence of the proof that the prohibition was only on the part of Jacob and not during the time of Abraham then those are the wrongdoers who transgress the truth towards falsehood.
3:95: (Say: God has spoken the truth) in this as in all that He has informed us of So follow the religion of Abraham which I am upon as a monotheist inclined away from every religion to Islam and he was not of the polytheists
3:96: And when they said, Our qiblah is before your qiblah, it was revealed: Indeed, the first House established as a place of worship for mankind on earth was that at Bakkah with the letter ba in the language of Mecca. It was named thus because it crushes the necks of tyrants, i.e., it crushes them. The angels built it before the creation of Adam, and after it Al-Aqsa was established, and between them there were forty years, as in the hadith of the two Sahihs and in the hadith: The first thing that appeared on the surface of the water when the heavens and the earth were created was a white foam, and the earth spread out from beneath it. Blessed is a state of who, i.e., possessing blessing and a guidance for the worlds because it is their qiblah.
3:97: In it are clear signs including the station of Abraham meaning the stone on which he stood when building the House, so the trace of his feet in it has remained until now despite the passage of time and the circulation of hands on it, and including the doubling of good deeds in it and that birds do not climb it and whoever enters it is safe not exposed to killing or injustice or anything else and Hajj to the House is a duty that mankind owes to God obligatory with the kasra and fatha of the ha’, two languages in the source of Hajj intended and it is replaced by people whoever can find a way to it a path which the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, interpreted as provisions and a mount, narrated by Al-Hakim and others and whoever disbelieves in God or in what He has imposed of Hajj then indeed, God is independent of the worlds mankind, the jinn, and the angels and their worship
3:98: Say, O People of the Scripture, why do you disbelieve in the verses of God the Qur’an and God is Witness over what you do so He will reward you for it
3:99: (Say, O People of the Scripture, why do you turn away from the way of God, that is, His religion, those who believe, by denying the Prophet and concealing His favor, seeking it crooked, while you are witnesses, knowing that the acceptable and upright religion is the religion of Islam as in your book? And God is not unaware of what you do, of disbelief and denial. Rather, He delays you until your time so that He may reward you.)
3:100: And it was revealed when some Jews passed by the Aws and Khazraj and their friendship was angered by them, so they reminded them of the strife that had occurred between them in the pre-Islamic era, so they quarrelled and almost fought: O you who have believed, if you obey a party of those who were given the Scripture, they will turn you back, after your belief, to disbelief.
3:101: And how can you disbelieve An interrogative of astonishment and rebuke (while the verses of God are recited to you and among you is His Messenger. And whoever holds fast) clings to God has certainly been guided to a straight path
3:102: O you who have believed, fear God as He should be feared by obeying and not disobeying, thanking and not ungrateful, and remembering and not forgetting. They said, O Messenger of God, who has the strength to do this? So it was abrogated by the Almighty’s saying {So fear God as much as you are able} and do not die except as Muslims monotheists.
3:103: And hold fast cling to the rope of God that is, His religion all together and do not become divided after Islam and remember the favor of God His favor upon you O people of Aws and Khazraj when you were before Islam enemies and He brought your hearts together through Islam and you became so you became by His favor brothers in religion and allegiance and you were on the brink edge of a pit of fire there was nothing between you and falling into it except that you die as disbelievers and He saved you from it through faith thus as He explained to you what He mentioned God makes clear to you His verses that you may be guided
3:104: And let there arise from you a community inviting to good Islam (and enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong. And those) the callers, the commanders, the forbidders are the successful ones the victorious ones. Min is for partialization because what was mentioned is a communal obligation that is not incumbent upon the entire community and is not appropriate for everyone, such as the ignorant. It was said that it is redundant, meaning that you will be a community.
3:105: And do not be like those who became divided from their religion and differed about it after the clear proofs had come to them and they are the Jews and Christians and those will have a great punishment
3:106: On the Day when some faces will turn white and some faces will turn black meaning the Day of Resurrection As for those whose faces will turn black meaning the disbelievers, they will be thrown into the Fire and it will be said to them as a rebuke Did you disbelieve after your belief on the Day the covenant was taken Then taste the punishment for what you used to disbelieve
3:107: And as for those whose faces will be white and they are the believers they will be in the mercy of God that is, His Paradise therein they will abide eternally
3:108: These meaning these verses are the verses of God which We recite to you O Muhammad (in truth. And God does not intend injustice to the worlds) by taking them without a crime.
3:109: And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth as dominion, creation, and servants and to God are returned (matters) return.
3:110: You were O nation of Muhammad in the knowledge of God Almighty the best nation produced made manifest (for mankind. You enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and believe in God. And if the People of the Scripture had believed, it would have been better for them. Among them are the believers) like Abdullah ibn Salam, may God be pleased with him, and his companions but most of them are wicked the disbelievers.
3:111: They will not harm you meaning the Jews, O Muslims, with anything except for some harm with the tongue, such as insults and threats and if they fight you, they will turn their backs in defeat and then they will not be victorious over you, but rather you will be victorious over them.
3:112: They are inflicted with humiliation wherever they are found wherever they are found, so they have no honor or protection except being with a rope from God and a rope from the people the believers, which is their covenant with them of safety on the basis of paying the jizyah, meaning they have no protection other than that and they incurred they returned (with wrath from God, and poverty has been inflicted upon them. That is because) that is because (they disbelieved in the signs of God and killed the prophets without right. That) is an emphasis because they disobeyed the command of God and they were transgressing they went beyond what was permissible to what was forbidden.
3:113: They meaning the People of the Book are not equal equal among the People of the Book is a community standing upright and steadfast on the truth like Abdullah bin Salam, may God be pleased with him and his companions reciting the verses of God during the hours of the night meaning during its hours and they prostrate praying, a state.
3:114: (They believe in God and the Last Day, enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong, and hasten to good deeds. And those) described by what God has mentioned are among the righteous, and among them are those who are not like that and are not among the righteous.
3:115: And whatever you do with the taa, O nation, and the yaa, meaning the existing nation of good, you will never be denied it with both faces, meaning you will not be deprived of its reward, but rather you will be rewarded for it and God is Knowing of the righteous
3:116: Indeed, those who disbelieve - neither their wealth nor their children will avail them at all against God that is, against His punishment at all. He mentioned them specifically because a person sometimes defends himself by ransoming his wealth and sometimes by seeking help from his children and those are the companions of the Fire; they will abide therein eternally.
3:117: Like an attribute of what they spend i.e. the disbelievers in this worldly life in enmity against the Prophet in charity and the like is like a wind in which there is a cold wind intense heat or cold that strikes the crops of a people who wronged themselves) through disbelief and disobedience and destroyed them so they did not benefit from it, so their expenditures are wasted and they do not benefit from them and God did not wrong them through the loss of their expenditures but they wrong themselves through disbelief that causes them to be lost
3:118: O you who have believed, do not take as intimates close friends to whom you reveal your secrets other than yourselves meaning other than you from among the Jews, Christians and hypocrites they will not spare you any corruption accusative by removing the preposition, meaning they will not fall short in corrupting you they wish they wished for what you have suffered meaning your suffering, which is severe harm the hatred the enmity towards you has already appeared) has become apparent from their mouths by slandering you and revealing your secret to the polytheists and what their breasts conceal of enmity (is greater. We have made clear to you the verses) about their enmity if you were to use reason so do not befriend them.
3:119: Ha to alert you O those believers love them for their kinship to you and their friendship but they do not love you for their opposition to you in religion and you believe in the entire Book that is, in all the books, but they do not believe in your Book and when they meet you, they say, We believe; but when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you the tips of their fingers out of rage the intensity of anger at what they see of your unity, and some of the fingertips express the intensity of anger metaphorically, even if there is no biting say, Die in your rage that is, remain in it until death, for you will not see what pleases you indeed, God is Knowing of that within the breasts what is in the hearts, including what these people conceal.
3:120: If good befalls you a blessing such as victory or spoils it distresses them it grieves them but if evil befalls you such as defeat or drought they rejoice at it and the conditional clause is connected to the condition before it and what is between them is an interjection and the meaning is that they are extreme in their enmity towards you so why did you not befriend them so avoid them and if you are patient in their harm and fear God in befriending them and other things their plot will not harm you with a kasra on the dad and a sukoon on the ra and a damma and a shaddah (their plot will not harm you at all. Indeed, God is encompassing of what they do) with a ya and a ta and is aware of what they do so He will reward them for it.
3:121: And remember, O Muhammad, when you went forth from your family from Medina to set up to alight for the believers in stations positions in which they would stand (for the battle. And God is Hearing) of your words and Knowing of your conditions. And it was the day of Uhud. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went out with a thousand or less than fifty men and the polytheists were three thousand and he camped in the valley on Saturday, the seventh of Shawwal, in the year three of the Hijra. He turned his back and his army to Uhud and straightened their ranks and seated an army of archers and put Abdullah ibn Jubayr in charge of them at the foot of the mountain and said: Sprinkle arrows on us so that they do not come from behind us and do not move, whether we are defeated or victorious.
3:122: When instead of the before it when Banu Salimah and Banu Haritha, the two wings of the army were about to lose heart were afraid to fight and turn back when Abdullah bin Abi the hypocrite and his companions turned back and said: Why should we kill ourselves and our children? And he said to Abu Jaber Al-Salami, who said to him: I ask you by God regarding your Prophet and yourselves: If we knew there would be fighting, we would have followed you. So God made them steadfast and they did not turn back. And God is their Guardian their Supporter. And in God let the believers put their trust to trust in Him and no one else.
3:123: And when they were defeated, it was revealed to remind them of God’s blessing: And God had already given you victory at Badr a place between Mecca and Medina and you were weak with few numbers and weapons so fear God that you may be grateful for His blessings.
3:124: When is an adverb for your victory You say to the believers You threaten them to reassure them Is it not sufficient for you that your Lord should reinforce you help you with three thousand angels sent down with emphasis and emphasis
3:125: Yes that is enough for you, and in Al-Anfal with a thousand because He first provided them with it, then it became three, then it became five, as God Almighty said If you are patient in meeting the enemy and fear God in disobedience and they come to you meaning the polytheists all at once their time this, your Lord will reinforce you with five thousand angels marked with a kasra on the waw and a fatha on it, meaning marked, and they were patient and God fulfilled His promise by having the angels fight with them on piebald horses wearing yellow or white turbans that they let down between their shoulders.
3:126: And God has not made it that is, the support except as good tidings for you of victory and that your hearts may be reassured so that they may be calmed by it so that they do not panic because of the large number of the enemy and your small number and victory is only from God, the Exalted in Might, the Wise He gives it to whomever He wills, and it is not by the large number of soldiers.
3:127: to cut off is related to your victory, meaning to destroy a portion of those who disbelieved by killing or capturing or to subdue them humiliate them by defeat so that they turn back return disappointed not having achieved what they sought.
3:128: And when his front tooth was broken, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his face was wounded on the Day of Uhud, and he said: How can a people succeed who have stained the face of their Prophet with blood? Then was revealed: You have no part in the matter, rather the matter belongs to God, so be patient or meaning until He turns to them in forgiveness with Islam or punishes them, for they are wrongdoers with disbelief.
3:129: And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth as kings, creation, and servants. He forgives whom He wills forgiveness for him and punishes whom He wills punishment. And God is Forgiving to His friends and Merciful to those who obey Him.
3:130: O you who have believed, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied by a thousand or less, by increasing the money when the due date comes and delaying the request and fear God by abandoning it that you may succeed win.
3:131: And fear the Fire prepared for the disbelievers lest you be tormented by it.
3:132: And obey God and the Messenger that you may receive mercy.
3:133: And hasten with waw or without it to forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as wide as the heavens and the earth that is, as wide as they were if you connected one of them to the other, and width is spaciousness prepared for the righteous of God by doing acts of obedience and abandoning sins.
3:134: Those who spend in obedience to God in prosperity and adversity ease and hardship and who restrain anger refrain from carrying it out even though they are able and who pardon the people of those whom they have wronged, meaning those who refrain from punishing them and God loves the doers of good through these actions, meaning He rewards them.
3:135: And those who, when they have committed an immorality a heinous sin such as adultery or wronged themselves with something less than that such as kissing they remember God that is, His threat and seek forgiveness for their sins - and none that is, none forgives sins except God - and they do not persist persist in what they did but rather desist while they know that what they have done is a sin
3:136: Their reward is forgiveness from their Lord and gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally a predicate, meaning destined to abide eternally in it if they enter it and excellent is the reward of the workers in obedience, this is the reward.
3:137: And it was revealed about the defeat of UHud 11:There have already passed gone before you ways of giving the disbelievers respite and then seizing them so travel O believers throughout the land and see how was the end of those who denied the messengers, meaning the end of their affair of destruction, so do not be sad about their defeat, for I will give them respite for their time.
3:138: This Qur’an is a clarification for the people all of them and a guidance from misguidance and an admonition for the righteous among them.
3:139: And do not weaken weaken from fighting the infidels and do not grieve over what happened to you at anyone and you will be superior by defeating them if you are truly believers and the answer to it is indicated by the sum of what preceded it.
3:140: If a wound should touch you if it touches you at Uhud with a wound with the qaf open or closed: an effort from a wound or the like there has already touched the people the infidels with a wound similar to it at Badr and those days We alternate We spend them among the people one day for one group and one day for another so that they may take heed and that God may know the knowledge of the appearance of those who believe were sincere in their faith from others and take from among you witnesses He honors them with martyrdom and God does not like the wrongdoers the infidels, meaning He punishes them, and what He bestows upon them is a lure.
3:141: And that God may purify those who believe purify them from sins by what befalls them and destroy destroy the disbelievers
3:142: Or rather did you think that you would enter Paradise while God has not yet knowledges those among you who strive knowledge of the appearance and knows the patient in hardships
3:143: And you were wishing in it, one of the two ta's was deleted in the original for death before you met it when you said: If only we had a day like the day of Badr so that we could attain what its martyrs attained But you have seen it that is, its cause is war and you are looking on that is, with insight, contemplating the situation, how it is, so why were you defeated? And it was revealed regarding their defeat when it was rumored that the Prophet was killed and the hypocrites said to them, If he was killed, then return to your religion:
3:144: (And Muhammad is no more than a messenger; many messengers have passed away before him. If he dies or is killed) like others will you turn back on your heels will you return to disbelief? The last sentence is the subject of a rhetorical question, meaning that what was worshipped, so you will turn back And whoever turns back on his heels will never harm God at all but he will only harm himself and God will reward the grateful for His blessings with steadfastness.
3:145: And no soul can die except by God's permission by His decree prescribed a source, meaning: God has decreed that deferred temporarily, neither advanced nor delayed. So why were you defeated? Defeat does not prevent death, and steadfastness does not cut short life. And whoever desires by his deeds the reward of this world meaning his recompense from it We will give him thereof what was allotted to him and noted for him in the Hereafter And whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter, We will give him thereof meaning from its reward And We will reward the grateful.
3:146: And how many how many of the prophets fought and in the reading {killed} and the subject is his pronoun with him the predicate of his subject many pious large groups but they did not weaken were cowardly for what afflicted them in the way of God from the locusts and the killing of their prophets and companions nor did they weaken from jihad nor did they submit submitted to their enemy as you did when it was said that the prophet was killed and God loves the patient in the face of affliction, meaning He rewards them
3:147: And their only statement when their prophet was killed, despite their steadfastness and patience was that they said, Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our transgression we exceeded the limit in our affair indicating that what happened to them was due to their evil actions and their humiliation and plant firmly our feet with the strength to fight and give us victory over the disbelieving people
3:148: So God gave them the reward of this world victory and spoils and the best reward of the Hereafter meaning Paradise, and its goodness is preference over what is deserved and God loves the doers of good
3:149: O you who have believed, if you obey those who disbelieve in what they command you they will turn you back to disbelief on your heels, and you will become losers
3:150: Rather, God is your protector He is your helper and He is the best of helpers so obey Him rather than them.
3:151: We will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve with the sukoon of the 'ayn and the damma of it, fear, and they resolved after their departure from Uhud to return and exterminate the Muslims, but they were terrified and did not return because they associated because of their association with God that for which He had not sent down any authority an argument for its worship, which is idols and their refuge is the Fire, and wretched is the residence of the wrongdoers the disbelievers are
3:152: And God has fulfilled His promise to you to you with victory when you were killing them by His permission) by His will until you lost heart you were afraid to fight and disputed you differed about the matter that is, the Prophet’s (may God bless him and grant him peace) command to stand at the foot of the mountain to shoot, so some of you said: We will go, for our companions have been victorious, and some of you: We will not disobey the Prophet’s (may God bless him and grant him peace) command and you disobeyed his command and left the position to seek spoils after God had shown you that which you love of victory and the answer to if what preceded it indicated it, that is, He prevented you from His victory among you is he who desires this world so he left the position for the spoils and among you is he who desires the Hereafter so he remained steadfast in it until he was killed like Abdullah ibn Jubayr and his companions then He turned you away in apposition to the answer to if that is understood, He returned you with defeat from them that is, the disbelievers to test you to test you and show the sincere from the infidels and He has pardoned you what You committed it and God is full of grace to the believers with forgiveness
