4:92: And it is not for a believer to kill a believer that is, he should not kill him except by mistake making a mistake in killing him without intending to and whoever kills a believer by mistake by intending to shoot someone else, such as a game or a tree, and he hits him or hits him with something that usually does not kill then the freeing the freeing of a believing slave is upon him and the blood money is due paid to his family that is, the heirs of the one killed unless they give it in charity they give it to him by pardoning it, and the Sunnah explained that it is one hundred camels, twenty young female camels, and also two-year-old female camels, two-year-old male camels, one-year-old female camels, and two-year-old female camels, and it is upon the killer’s clan, and they are his agnates in origin and branch, distributed among them over three years, the rich among them half a dinar and the middle-class a quarter of each year. If that is not enough, then it is from the public treasury, and if that is not possible, then it is upon the perpetrator and if the one killed is from a people at war with you and he is a believer, then the freeing of a believing slave is upon his killer as expiation and no blood money is paid to his family And if the one killed is from a people between whom and you there is a treaty such as the People of the Covenant a ransom for him delivered to his family which is one-third of the blood money of a believer if he is a Jew or a Christian and one-third of it if he is a Zoroastrian and the freeing of a believing slave on his killer but whoever does not find the slave by losing it or what he can obtain then fasting for two consecutive months upon him as expiation, and God Almighty did not mention the transition to food like in zihar and this is what Al-Shafi’i took in the most correct of his two opinions repentance from God a source accusative with its implied verb and God is Knowing of His creation Wise in what He planned for them
4:93: And whoever kills a believer intentionally meaning he intends to kill him with what usually kills with his faith his recompense is Hell, wherein he will abide eternally, and the wrath and the curse of God are upon him He has removed him from His mercy and has prepared for him a great punishment in the Fire. This is interpreted as referring to someone who considers it permissible or that this is his recompense if he is punished. There is no innovation in breaking the threat, as He says {And He forgives what is less than that, if He wills}. According to Ibn Abbas, it is based on its apparent meaning and that it abrogates other verses of forgiveness. The verse of the Cow clarifies that the intentional killer is killed with it and that he must pay blood money if he is pardoned and its amount has been determined. The Sunnah clarifies that between intentional and accidental killing is a killing called quasi-intentional killing, which is that he kills him with what usually does not kill, so there is no retaliation in it, but blood money like intentional killing in the description and mistake in the delay and the burden, and intentional killing is more deserving of expiation than mistake.
4:94: And it was revealed when a group of the Companions passed by a man from Banu Salim while he was driving sheep and he greeted them, so they said he did not greet us except as a precaution, so they killed him and drove away his sheep O you who believe, when you strike when you travel for jihad in the cause of God, then verify and in the reading {fa-ta-thabtu} in both places (and do not say to one who offers you peace) with an alif or without it, meaning greeting or submission with the word of testimony which is a sign of Islam you are not a believer and I only said this as a precaution for yourself and your wealth, so you kill him seek you seek for that the goods of worldly life its enjoyment from the spoils but with God are many spoils that suffice you from killing someone like him for his wealth thus were you before your blood and wealth are protected by simply saying the testimony but God has conferred favor upon you by being known for your faith and uprightness so verify that you do not kill a believer and do to the one who enters Islam as He did to you indeed God is ever, with what you do, Acquainted He will reward you with it.
4:95: Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home from jihad other than those who are disabled in the nominative case as an attribute and the accusative case as an exception from time or blindness and the like (and those who strive in the cause of God with their wealth and their lives. God has preferred those who strive with their wealth and their lives over those who sit (at home) due to harm in degree as a virtue because they are equal in intention and the mujahidin are more active and to each of the two groups God has promised the best Paradise and God has preferred those who strive over those who sit due to no harm a great reward
4:96: Degrees from Him stations, some above others, of honor and forgiveness and mercy are in the accusative case with their implied verb and God is Forgiving to His friends and Merciful to the people of His obedience. It was revealed about a group who converted to Islam but did not emigrate, so they were killed on the day of Badr with the infidels.
4:97: Indeed, those whom the angels take while they are wronging themselves by staying with the disbelievers and not emigrating they will say to them, rebuking in what were you what were you doing regarding your religion they will say apologizing we were oppressed unable to establish the religion in the land the land of Mecca they will say to them, rebuking was not the earth of God spacious enough for you to emigrate therein from the land of disbelief to another country as others did. God Almighty said they will have Hell as their refuge, and evil it is as a destination it is
4:98: Except for the weak among men, women and children who have no means of scheming have no strength to migrate nor the means of sustenance nor are they shown a way a path to the land of migration
4:99: (Then it is those whom God may pardon. And God is Pardoning and Forgiving.)
4:100: And whoever emigrates for the cause of God will find on the earth many [alternative] locations and abundance in provision. And whoever emigrates from his home as an emigrant to God and His Messenger and then death overtakes him on the way, as happened to Junda’ bin Damrah al-Laythi (has his reward with God. And God is Forgiving and Merciful)
4:101: And when you travel travel throughout the land, there is no blame upon you in shortening the prayer by reducing it from four to two if you fear that those who disbelieve may tempt you that is, that they may afflict you with harm () a statement of reality, and the Sunnah clarified that what is meant by the long journey is four rak'ahs, which are two stages, and it is taken from His statement {there is no blame upon you} that it is a dispensation, not an obligation, and Al-Shafi'i agreed with this indeed, the disbelievers are to you a clear enemy clear enmity
4:102: And if you O Muhammad, are present among them and you fear the enemy and lead them in prayer and this is in accordance with the Qur’an’s custom in addressing then let a party of them stand with you and let another party stay back and let them take that is, the party that stood with you their weapons with them and when they prostrate that is, pray then let them that is, the other party be behind you guarding until you finish the prayer and this party goes to guard and let another party come who have not prayed and let them pray with you and take their precautions and their weapons with them until you finish the prayer. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, did the same in Batn Nakhl, narrated by the two sheikhs Those who disbelieve wish that you would neglect when you stand for prayer your weapons and your baggage so that they could attack you with a single attack by attacking you and seizing you. This is the reason for the command to take weapons and there is no blame upon you if you are suffering from rain or you are ill if you lay down your weapons so do not carry them. This is useful Positive pregnancy when there is no excuse, and this is one of two opinions of Al-Shafi’i, and the second is that it is a Sunnah, and he preferred and take your precautions from the enemy, meaning, guard against him as much as you can for God has prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment of humiliation1
4:103: So when you have completed the prayer you have finished it then remember God by glorifying and praising Him standing, sitting, or lying on your sides that is, in every situation and when you are secure you are safe then establish prayer perform it with its rights Indeed, prayer has been decreed upon the believers a decree written, that is, imposed at specified times that is, its time is determined, so do not delay it. And it was revealed when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent a group in pursuit of Abu Sufyan and his companions when they returned from Uhud and complained of wounds.
4:104: And do not weaken weaken in seeking the people) the disbelievers so that you may fight them if you are suffering you are experiencing the pain of wounds they are suffering as you are suffering that is, like you, and they are not cowardly in fighting you and you hope you from God of victory and reward for it that which they do not hope so you increase them in that, so you should be more desirous of it than they are and God is Knowing of everything Wise in His creation.
4:105: And Ta'mah ibn Ubayraq stole a shield and hid it with a Jew, and it was found with him, so Ta'mah threw it at him and swore that he had not stolen it, so his people asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to argue on his behalf and acquit him, so it was revealed Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book the Qur’an in truth related to sent down so that you may judge between the people by what God has shown you taught you in it (and be not an advocate for the deceitful) like Ta'mah an advocate on their behalf
4:106: And ask forgiveness of God for what you intended to do Indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful
4:107: And do not argue on behalf of those who betray themselves who betray themselves by committing sins because the consequences of their betrayal are upon them Indeed, God does not like anyone who is a traitor who frequently betrays and sinful meaning He punishes him.
4:108: They hide that is, Ta'ama and his people out of shyness from the people, but they do not hide from God, and He is with them with His knowledge when they spend the night concealing that which does not please Him of speech of their determination to swear to deny the theft and accuse the Jew of it and God is encompassing of what they do with knowledge.
4:109: Here you are oh these addressing the people of Ta'mah you argued you disputed about them meaning about Ta'mah and his relatives and it was read {about him} (in the life of this world. So who will argue with God on their behalf on the Day of Resurrection) if He punishes them or who will be a guardian over them who will take charge of their affairs and defend them, meaning no one will do that.
4:110: And whoever does evil a sin that harms another, such as throwing food at a Jew or wrongs himself commits a sin that is limited to himself and then seeks forgiveness from God for it, that is, repents he will find God Forgiving of him Merciful to him.
4:111: And whoever commits sin a sin only commits it against himself because its consequences are upon him and it does not harm anyone else and ever is God Knowing and Wise in His creation.
4:112: And whoever commits a sin a small sin or a crime a great sin and then attributes it to an innocent person has taken upon himself) has borne a slander by accusing him and a manifest sin a clear sin that he has committed.
4:113: And had it not been for the grace of God upon you O Muhammad and His mercy in infallibility a group of them from the people of Ta'mah would have resolved concealed to mislead you from the judgment of truth by their deception of you and they mislead only themselves and they do not harm you at all because the consequences of their misleading them are upon them and God has sent down upon you the Book the Qur'an and wisdom what is in it of rulings and taught you what you did not know of rulings and the unseen and ever has the grace of God upon you in that and other things been great
4:114: There is no good in much of their private conversation meaning people, that is, what they whisper and talk about except private conversation of whoever enjoins charity or righteousness righteous work (or conciliation between people. And whoever does that) mentioned seeks seeking the pleasure of God and nothing else of worldly matters then We will give him with the letter noon and the letter ya’ meaning God a great reward
4:115: And whoever contradicts opposes the Messenger in what he brought of the truth after guidance has become clear to him the truth has become clear to him through miracles and follows a path other than the path of the believers that is, their path that they are on in religion by disbelief We will give him what he has taken We will make him a guardian of what he has taken of misguidance by leaving him alone with it in this world and drive him We will admit him in the Hereafter to Hell so that he will burn in it and evil it is as a destination a return.
4:116: (Indeed, God does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whomever He wills. And whoever associates with God has certainly strayed far astray) from the truth.
4:117: Indeed what the polytheists invoke worship besides Him meaning God, meaning other than Him except females idols, the feminine of which is Al-Lat, Al-Uzza, and Manat and what they invoke worship by worshipping them except a rebellious devil outside of obedience to their obedience to him in it, and he is Iblis.
4:118: May God curse him He distanced him from His mercy and he said meaning Satan I will surely take I will make for myself of Your servants a specific portion I will call them to obey me
4:119: And I will surely mislead them from the truth by whispering and I will surely arouse in them false hopes I will cast into their hearts throughout their lives that there will be no resurrection or reckoning and I will command them to slit cut off the ears of livestock and He did that with the lakes and I will command them to change the creation of God His religion by disbelief and making permissible what God has forbidden and forbidding what He has made permissible and whoever takes Satan as an ally who takes him as an ally and obeys him instead of God that is, other than Him he has certainly suffered a clear loss clear of his fate in the eternal Fire upon him.
4:120: He promises them long life and gives them hope of achieving their hopes in this world and that there will be no resurrection or reward and Satan does not promise them of that except delusion falsely.
4:121: Their abode is Hell, and they will find no escape from it. Modified
4:122: (And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide forever. The promise of God is true.) That is, God promised them that, and His truth is true. And who that is, no one is more truthful than God in statement that is, no one is more truthful in speech than God.
4:123: And it was revealed when the Muslims and the People of the Book boasted it is not the matter is connected to your desires nor to the desires of the People of the Book but to righteous deeds whoever does evil will be recompensed for it either in the Hereafter or this world with affliction and trials as mentioned in the hadith and he will not find for himself besides God that is, other than Him a protector to protect him nor a helper to prevent him from it
4:124: And whoever does anything of righteous deeds, whether male or female, while he is a believer - those will enter in the passive and active form Paradise and they will not be wronged, even as much as the speck on a date seed the size of a pit of a date seed.
4:125: And who meaning no one is better in religion than one who submits his face meaning submits and is sincere in his actions to God while being a doer of good monotheist and follows the religion of Abraham in accordance with the religion of Islam inclining toward truth a state of being, meaning inclined away from all religions to the upright religion and God took Abraham as a friend a sincere friend, pure in his love for Him.
4:126: And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth as kings, creation, and servants and God is encompassing of all things in knowledge and power, meaning He has always been described by that.
4:127: And they ask you for a legal opinion they ask you for a legal opinion concerning (women) and their inheritance say to them God gives you a legal opinion concerning them and what is recited to you in the Book the Qur’an from the verse on inheritance. He also gives you a legal opinion concerning orphan women to whom you do not give what has been prescribed an obligation for them of the inheritance and you desire O guardians to marry them because of their ugliness and you prevent them from marrying out of desire for their inheritance, that is, He gives you a legal opinion not to do that and concerning the weak the young children that you give them their rights and He commands you and to stand up for orphans with justice with fairness in inheritance and dowry and whatever good you do - indeed, God is ever Knowing of it so He will reward you for it.
4:128: And if a woman raised by a verb that explains it fears she expects from her husband her husband contempt out of arrogance towards her by not sleeping with her and falling short in providing for her because of his hatred for her and his desire for something more beautiful than her or turning away from her with his face (there is no blame upon them if they make peace) in which there is an assimilation of the ta’ in the original into the sad and in the reading {yusliha} from aslah (make peace between them) in the division and the provision by leaving him something in order to maintain the friendship. If she is satisfied with that, then if not, then the husband must fulfill her right or leave her and reconciliation is better than separation, contempt and turning away. God Almighty said in explaining what man is created with and souls are inclined to stinginess intense miserliness, meaning they are created with it so it is as if it is present with him and never absent from him, the meaning is that a woman hardly allows her share from her husband and a man hardly allows himself to her if he loves someone else and if they treat women well (and fear) injustice towards them (then indeed, God He is All-Knowing of what you do, so He will reward you for it.
4:129: And you will never be able to be equal be equal between women in love even if you should strive to do so (so do not incline completely) to the one you love in terms of the division of money and expenditure leaving her that is, leaving her alone as one in suspense who is neither widowed nor married and if you make amends in terms of justice and division and fear injustice then indeed, God is ever Forgiving of what is in your heart of inclination and Merciful to you in that.
4:130: And if they separate meaning the spouses by divorce God will enrich each from the other out of His bounty meaning His grace by providing her with a husband other than him and him with another and God is Bountiful to His creation in grace and Wise in what He has planned for them.
4:131: (And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And We have already enjoined upon those who were given the Scripture) meaning the Scriptures before you meaning the Jews and Christians and upon you O people of the Qur’an that meaning that fear God fear His punishment by obeying Him and We said to them and to you if you disbelieve in what you have been enjoined then to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth creation, dominion, and servants, so your disbelief will not harm Him and God is free from His creation and their worship Praiseworthy in His actions towards them.
4:132: And to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth He repeated it to confirm the statement of the necessity of piety And sufficient is God as Disposer of affairs as a witness that whatever is in them belongs to Him.
4:133: If He wills, He can do away with you O people and bring others in your place and God is ever competent to do that
4:134: Whoever desires through his actions the reward of this world, then with God is the reward of this world and the Hereafter for whoever desires it and not with anyone else. So why should any of you seek the worst? Why not seek the highest with sincerity for Him, since his desire is not found except with Him? And God is All-Hearing, All-Seeing
4:135: (O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm in justice, witnesses for God, even if the testimony is against yourselves, so bear witness against it by acknowledging the truth and not concealing it, or against parents and relatives. If the one being testified against is rich or poor, God is more worthy of them both than you and knows best what is in their best interests. So follow not [your own] inclination in your testimony, by showing favoritism toward the rich for his pleasure or toward the poor out of mercy for him, lest you deviate from the truth. And if you distort the testimony, and in the reading {tulwa} the first waw is deleted for ease, or you turn away from performing it, then indeed God is ever, with what you do, Acquainted, so He will reward you for it.
4:136: O you who have believed, believe persist in believing in God and His Messenger and the Book which He sent down upon His Messenger Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, which is the Qur’an and the Book which He sent down before upon the messengers, meaning the books, and in a reading with the construction of the subject in the two verbs and whoever disbelieves in God and His angels and His books and His messengers and the Last Day has certainly strayed far astray from the truth
4:137: Indeed, those who believed in Moses, that is, the Jews then disbelieved in their worship of the calf then believed after him then disbelieved in Jesus then increased in disbelief in Muhammad God will never forgive them what they persisted in nor will He guide them to a way a path to the truth.
4:138: Good report ofform, O Muhammad, the hypocrites that they will have a painful punishment painful punishment, which is the punishment of the Fire.
4:139: Those a substitute or description of the hypocrites take the disbelievers as allies instead of the believers because of what they imagine in them of strength Do they seek seek with them honor a question of denial, meaning they do not find it with them For indeed, honor belongs to God entirely in this world and the hereafter, and none attains it except His allies.
4:140: And He has revealed in the active and passive form upon you in the Book the Qur’an in Surat Al-An’am that is lightened and its subject is omitted, meaning that when you hear the verses of God the Qur’an being denied and ridiculed, then do not sit with them meaning the disbelievers and the mockers (until they engage in a conversation other than that. Indeed, if you) if you sit with them you will be like them in sin indeed, God will gather the hypocrites and the disbelievers in Hell all together just as they gathered in this world in disbelief and mockery.
4:141: Those instead of those before it wait waiting for you the circles and if you have a victory triumph and spoils from God they will say) to you were we not with you in religion and jihad, so give us from the spoils and if the disbelievers have a share of the victory over you they will say to them did we not gain control dominate over you and were able to take you and kill you, so we spared you and did we not prevent you from the believers from gaining control over you by discouraging them and corresponding with you with their report, so we have a favor upon you, the Most High said so God will judge between you and them on the Day of Resurrection by admitting you into Paradise and admitting them into Hell and God will not grant to the disbelievers a way over the believers a way of extermination
4:142: Indeed, the hypocrites seek to deceive God by showing the opposite of what they concealed of disbelief, thus repelling His worldly rulings from them. And it is He who deceives them He will reward them for their deception, so they will be disgraced in this world by God informing His Prophet of what they concealed, and they will be punished in the Hereafter. And when they stand up for prayer with the believers, they stand up lazily sluggishly showing off to the people with their prayer and they do not remember God they pray except a little hypocritically.
4:143: wavering hesitating between that disbelief and faith not attributed to these meaning the disbelievers nor to those meaning the believers and he whom God sends astray - you will never find for him a way a path to guidance.
4:144: (O you who have believed, do not take the disbelievers as allies instead of the believers. Do you wish to give God over you) by befriending them a clear authority a clear proof of your hypocrisy?
4:145: Indeed, the hypocrites will be in the lowest depths of the Fire, which is its bottom. And you will not find for them a helper to prevent them from the punishment.
4:146: Except for those who repent from hypocrisy and amend their actions and hold fast and trust in God and devote their religion to God from hypocrisy they will be with the believers in what they are given and God will give the believers a great reward in the Hereafter, which is Paradise.
4:147: What would God do with your punishment if you are grateful for His blessings and believe in Him? The question is in the sense of negation, meaning He will not punish you and God is ever Appreciative for the deeds of the believers by rewarding them and Knowing of His creation.
4:148: God does not like the public mention of evil from anyone, that is, He punishes him for it except for one who has been wronged so He does not punish him for publicizing it by telling about the wrong of his oppressor and praying against him and God is Hearing of what is said Knowing of what is done.
4:149: If you disclose show good righteous deeds or conceal it do it secretly or pardon an evil injustice then indeed, God is ever Pardoning and Powerful
4:150: Indeed, those who disbelieve in God and His messengers and wish to make distinction between God and His messengers by believing in Him without them and say, We believe in some of the messengers (and disbelieve in others of them and wish to adopt a path between that disbelief and belief a way a path to which they go
4:151: Those are the disbelievers, truly A source confirming the content of the sentence before it And We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment of humiliation, which is the punishment of the Fire.
4:152: And those who believed in God and His messengers all of them and made no distinction between any of them - those will be given with the letter ya and the letter nun their rewards the reward for their deeds and God is Forgiving to His friends and Merciful to the people of His obedience.
4:153: The People of the Book ask you O Muhammad the Jews (to bring down to them a book from the heaven) just as it was sent down to Moses, as a challenge. If you are arrogant about that they asked that is, their fathers Moses was greater greater than that, and they said, Show us God plainly clearly so the thunderbolt seized them death as a punishment for them for their wrongdoing when they were stubborn in their question then they took the calf as a god after the clear proofs had come to them the miracles of the oneness of God so We pardoned that and did not uproot them and We gave Moses a clear authority clear and apparent authority over them when he ordered them to kill themselves in repentance, and they obeyed him.
4:154: And We raised above them the Mount the mountain with their covenant because of the covenant taken from them so that they would fear, so they accepted it and We said to them and it shaded them Enter the gate the gate of the village prostrating prostration of bowing and We said to them, Do not transgress and in a reading with the opening of the ‘ayn and the emphasis on the dal, and in it the assimilation of the ta’ in the original into the dal {transgress} meaning do not transgress on the Sabbath by hunting whales on it and We took from them a firm covenant on that, so they broke it.
4:155: Because of their breach Ma is redundant and the Ba is for causality related to something omitted, meaning we cursed them because of their breach of their covenant and their disbelief in the signs of God and their killing of the prophets without right and their saying to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, Our hearts are covered they do not comprehend your words rather, God has sealed them with their disbelief) so they do not comprehend an admonition so they do not believe except a few of them like Abdullah bin Salam and his companions
4:156: And their disbelief secondly in Jesus, and the letter ba was repeated to separate it from what was conjoined to it, and their saying against Mary a great slander when they accused her of adultery.
4:157: And their saying boasting Indeed, we killed the Messiah, Jesus, the son of Mary, the Messenger of God in their claim, meaning that we punished them with all of that. God Almighty said, denying them in killing him And they killed him not, nor crucified him, but it was made to appear to them the one killed and crucified, who is their companion, to Jesus, meaning God cast his resemblance upon him, so they thought he was him And indeed, those who differ concerning it meaning concerning Jesus are in doubt concerning it about killing him, as some of them said when they saw the one killed, the face was the face of Jesus and the body was not his body, so it was not him, and others said rather it was him They have no knowledge concerning it about killing him except following conjecture an interrupted exception, meaning but they follow the conjecture they imagined and they did not kill him for certain a state confirming the denial of killing
4:158: Rather, God raised him up to Himself, and God is Exalted in Might in His kingdom and Wise in His creation.
4:159: And there is none of the People of the Scripture but that he will believe in him) in Jesus before his death meaning the People of the Scripture when he sees the angels of death and his belief will not benefit him, or before the death of Jesus when he descends near the Hour as mentioned in the hadith and on the Day of Resurrection he will be Jesus against them a witness for what they did when he was sent to them.
4:160: Then because of the injustice that is, because of the injustice of those who were Jews they are the Jews We forbade them good things which had been lawful to them which are what is in the Almighty’s saying: {We forbade every animal with claws} the verse and because they averted people from the way of God His religion, averting much
4:161: And their taking of usury, although they were forbidden from it in the Torah and their consuming of people’s wealth unjustly by bribery in judgment and We have prepared for the disbelievers among them a painful punishment painful
4:162: But those who are firmly rooted in knowledge among them such as Abdullah bin Salam and the believers the Muhajireen and Ansar believe in what has been revealed to you and what was revealed before you of the books and those who establish prayer in the accusative case for praise and it is read in the nominative case and those who give zakat and those who believe in God and the Last Day - those We will give with the nun and the ya a great reward which is Paradise.
4:163: Indeed, We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him and as We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, his two sons, and Jacob, the son of Isaac, and the tribes, his sons, and Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. And We gave to David the Psalms, with the fat-ha the name of the book that was given, and with the damma it is a source meaning written.
4:164: And We sent and messengers We have mentioned to you before and messengers We have not mentioned to you It was narrated that the Almighty sent eight thousand prophets, four thousand from Israel and four thousand from the rest of the people. The Sheikh said this in Surat Ghafir and God spoke to Moses without an intermediary speaking
4:165: Messengers instead of messengers before him bringing good tidings of reward to those who believe and warning of punishment to those who disbelieve. We sent them so that mankind would have no argument against God said after sending the messengers to them, so that they would say: {Our Lord, if only You had sent to us a messenger so that we could follow Your verses and be among the believers} So We sent them to cut off their excuse and ever is God Exalted in Might in His kingdom Wise in His creation.
4:166: And it was revealed when the Jews were asked about his prophethood, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they denied it but God bears witness He clarifies your prophethood to what He has revealed to you of the miraculous Qur’an He has revealed it ambiguous with His knowledge meaning He knows it or it contains His knowledge and the angels bear witness to you also and sufficient is God as Witness to that
4:167: Indeed, those who disbelieve in God and avert people from the way of God the religion of Islam by concealing the description of Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and they are the Jews have certainly gone far astray from the truth.
4:168: Indeed, those who disbelieved in God and wronged His Prophet by concealing his description God will never forgive them nor guide them to a way of the ways.
4:169: Except the path to Hell meaning the path leading to it abiding therein destined to remain there forever, and that, for God, is easy) pleasant.
4:170: O mankind meaning the people of Mecca there has come to you the Messenger Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace with the truth from your Lord, so believe in him and intend it is better for you than what you are in and if you disbelieve in him then to God belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth as kings, creation, and servants, so your disbelief will not harm Him and God is ever Knowing of His creation Wise in what He does with them.
4:171: O People of the Book the Gospel do not go to extremes exceed the limit in your religion and do not say about God except the truth statement of His being free from having a partner or a son The Messiah, Jesus, the son of Mary, was only a Messenger of God and His Word which He cast God conveyed it to Mary and a spirit that is, one with a spirit from Him added to Him, the Most High, to honor Him, and not as you claimed, the Son of God or a god with Him or the third of three because the one with a spirit is composed and the god is free from composition and from attributing the composite to Him So believe in God and His messengers and do not say the gods are three God, Jesus and his mother desist from that and do that which is better for you than it, which is monotheism God is only one God; glory be to Him freeing Him from having a son; to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth creation, dominion, and servants, and dominion contradicts prophethood and sufficient is God as Disposer of affairs as a witness to that
4:172: The Messiah whom you claimed was a god will not be disdainful and will be proud of being a slave to God, nor will the angels brought near to God be disdainful of being slaves. This is one of the best digressions mentioned in response to those who claimed that they were gods or daughters of God, just as he responded with what preceded it to the Christians who claimed that this was the intended address to them and whoever disdains His worship and is arrogant, He will gather them all together to Him in the Hereafter.
4:173: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, He will pay them their rewards in full and will increase them from His bounty what no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and no human heart has conceived. But as for those who disdain and are arrogant about worshipping Him, He will punish them with a painful punishment painful, which is the punishment of the Fire. And they will not find for themselves besides God that is, other than Him a protector to repel Him from them nor a helper to prevent Him from them.
4:174: O mankind, there has come to you a proof an argument from your Lord against you, and he is the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and We have sent down to you a clear light clear, and it is the Qur’an.
4:175: But as for those who believe in the Qur’an in God and hold fast to Him, He will admit them to mercy from Him and grace and guide them to Himself by a straight path a path which is the religion of Islam.
4:176: They ask you about the kalalah (Say: God gives you a legal opinion about the kalalah. If a man) raised by a verb that explains it dies he died leaving no child that is, no father, which is the kalalah and he has a sister from both parents or a father she shall have half of what he left, and he that is, the brother likewise shall inherit from her all of what she left if she has no child if she has a male child, he shall have nothing, or a female child, he shall have whatever is left of her share, and if the sister or brother is from the mother, his share is one-sixth, as mentioned at the beginning of the surah then if they that is, the sisters are two that is, or more, because it was revealed about Jabir and he died leaving sisters they shall have two-thirds of what he left the brother and if they that is, the heirs brothers, men and women, then the male of them (shall have the equivalent of the share of two females. God makes clear to you) the laws of your religion so that you do not go astray, and God is Knowing of all things) and from this is inheritance. The two sheikhs narrated on the authority of al-Baraa’ that it was the last verse revealed, that is, from Obligations
5:1: 1 - O you who have believed, fulfill contracts the confirmed covenants between you and God and the people Lawful to you are the beast of grazing livestock camels, cows, and sheep as food after slaughter except what is recited to you its prohibition in {He has forbidden you dead animals} the verse, so the exception is disconnected and it is permissible for it to be connected and the prohibition is for what occurred of death and the like except for the game while you are in a state of ihram meaning in a state of ihram and the accusative of ghayr is in the state of the pronoun for you Indeed, God decrees what He wills of permissibility and other than that, there is no objection to it
5:2: 2 - O you who have believed, do not violate the rites of God plural of rite, meaning the landmarks of His religion by hunting during the state of ihram nor the sacred month by fighting in it nor the sacrificial animals what was presented to the sanctuary from the livestock by exposing it nor the necklaces plural of necklace, which is what was worn from the trees of the sanctuary to make it safe, meaning do not expose them or their owners nor violate the rites intending the Sacred House by fighting them seek bounty provision from their Lord through trade and approval from Him by intending it according to their corrupt claim, and this was abrogated by the verse of innocence and when you have exited from the state of ihram then hunt a command of permission and let not hatred with the opening of the noon and its sukoon, the hatred of a people cause you) to incite you because they prevented you from the Sacred Mosque from transgressing against them by killing and other things and cooperate in righteousness by doing what you have been commanded to do. And piety by abandoning what you have been forbidden from and do not cooperate in it, one of the two ta’s was deleted in the original in sin disobedience and aggression transgression of the limits of God and fear God fear His punishment by obeying Him indeed, God is severe in penalty for whoever disobeys Him.
5:3: 3 - Forbidden to you are dead animals that is, eating them and blood that is, spilled as in livestock and the flesh of swine and that which has been dedicated to other than God by slaughtering in the name of someone else and the strangled that which is strangled and the beaten that which is killed by being struck and the one which falls that which falls from a height to a lower one and dies and the gored that which is killed by another goring it and that which a wild animal has eaten of it except that which you slaughter that is, you have found the soul in it from these things and slaughtered it and that which has been slaughtered on the name of al-Nusab the plural of Nisab, which are idols and that you should seek to divide to ask for division and judgment with arrows the plural of Zalam with the opening of the zay and its damma with the opening of the lam, Qidh with the breaking of the qaf, small, without feathers or blade, and there were seven with the custodian of the Kaaba on which there were flags and they used to rule over it. If you commanded them, they would obey, and if you forbade them, they would desist that is transgression disobedience, and it was revealed on the Day of Arafah in the year of Hajj Farewell: Today those who disbelieved have despaired of [defeating] your religion that you would turn away from it after their hope for that when they saw its strength (so fear them not but fear Me. Today I have perfected for you your religion) its rulings and obligations, and no lawful or unlawful thing was revealed after that and completed My favor upon you by completing it, and it was said by entering Mecca in safety and have chosen that is, I have chosen (for you Islam as religion. So whoever is forced by severe hunger) famine to eat something that was forbidden to him and eats it without inclining inclined to sin disobedience then indeed, God is Forgiving of him for what he ate and Merciful to him in permitting it, unlike the one who inclines to sin, that is, the one who is involved in it, like a highwayman or a rebel, for example, so it is not permissible for him to eat.
5:4: 4 - They ask you O Muhammad what is lawful for them of food (Say: Lawful for you are the good things) the delicious things and the game which you have taught of the birds of prey the predators of dogs, wild beasts and birds training them a state of lkalbabtu al-kalb with emphasis, meaning I sent it out to hunt teaching them a state of the pronoun mukallibeen, meaning you discipline them from what God has taught you of the etiquette of hunting so eat of what they catch for you even if they kill it if they do not eat from it, unlike the untrained ones, whose hunting is not lawful, and their sign is that they are released when they are sent and deterred when deterred and hold the game and do not eat from it, and the minimum that they are known for is three times, and if they eat from it, then it is not from what they caught for their owner, so it is not lawful to eat it as in the hadith of the two Sahihs, and in it is that the hunting of an arrow if it is sent and the name of God is mentioned over it is like the hunting of a trained bird of prey and mention the name of God over it when sending it and fear God; indeed, God is swift in account
5:5: 5 - Today, lawful to you are the good things the delicious things and the food of those who were given the Scripture that is, the slaughtered animals of the Jews and Christians is lawful lawful for you and your food theirs is lawful for them and chaste women from among the believers and chaste women free women from among those who were given the Scripture before you it is lawful for you to marry them when you have given them their dowries their bridal-gifts in chastity married not fornicating announcing adultery with them nor taking lovers from them who secretly commit adultery with them and whoever disbelieves in faith that is, apostatizes his work has become worthless the good work before that, so it is not counted and he is not rewarded for it and in the Hereafter he is among the losers if he dies upon that
5:6: 6 - O you who have believed, when you rise that is, you want to rise for prayer while you are in a state of minor ritual impurity wash your faces and your forearms up to the elbows that is, along with them, as the Sunnah has explained and wipe over your heads the ba’ is for adhesion, that is, stick the wiping to them without pouring water, and it is a generic name, so the least that is true of it is sufficient, which is wiping some of the hair, and this is what Al-Shafi’i has said and your feet in the accusative case in apposition to your hands and in the genitive case in reference to proximity to the ankles that is, along with them, as the Sunnah has explained, and they are the two protruding bones in each foot at the joint of the shin and foot, and the separation between the washed hands and feet by the wiped head indicates the necessity of order in purifying these limbs, and this is what Al-Shafi’i has said, and it is taken from the Sunnah that the intention is necessary in it like other acts of worship and if you are in a state of major ritual impurity, then purify yourselves so wash yourselves and if you are ill an illness that is harmed by water or on a journey that is, travelers or one of you comes from the toilet that is, has passed urine or (If you have touched women something similar has been mentioned previously in the verse of An-Nisa and you do not find water after seeking it then perform tayammum meaning clean earth pure soil and wipe your faces and your hands including the elbows with it with two strokes. The ba’ is for attachment, and the Sunnah has made clear that what is meant is to include both limbs by wiping God does not want to make difficulty for you hardship by what He has imposed on you of ablution, ritual washing, and tayammum but He wants to purify you from ritual impurities and sins and to complete His favor upon you with Islam by explaining the laws of the religion that you may be grateful for His favors.
5:7: 7 - And remember the favor of God upon you with Islam and His covenant His pledge by which He bound you He made a covenant with you when you said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, when you pledged allegiance to him We hear and obey in everything you command and forbid of what we like and dislike and fear God in His covenant that you break it Indeed, God is Knowing of that within the breasts what is in the hearts, so other than that is more deserving.
5:8: 8 - O you who have believed, be persistently standing firm standing firm for God in His rights witnesses in justice with fairness and do not let hatred of a people that is, the disbelievers incite you not to be just so that you do not take revenge on them because of their enmity be just with regard to the enemy and the friend it that is, justice (is nearer to righteousness. And fear God. Indeed, God is Acquainted with what you do) so He will reward you for it.
5:9: 9 - God has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds a good promise for them is forgiveness and a great reward which is Paradise.
5:10: 10 - And those who disbelieve and deny Our signs - those are the companions of Hellfire.
5:11: 11 - O you who have believed, remember the favor of God upon you when a people they are the Quraysh intended to extend their hands to attack you but He restrained their hands from you and protected you from what they intended for you and fear God, and upon God let the believers put their trust.
5:12: 12 - And God took a covenant from the Children of Israel with what is mentioned after and We sent in it a shift from the third person, We established from among them twelve leaders from each tribe a leader who would be responsible for his people in fulfilling the covenant, as a guarantee for them and He said to them God, I am with you with aid and victory if the lam of an oath you establish prayer and give zakah and believe in My messengers and support them you support them and lend God a goodly loan by spending in His way (I will surely remove from you your misdeeds and admit you to gardens beneath which rivers flow. But whoever disbelieves after that) the covenant from among you has certainly strayed from the right path he has strayed from the path of truth, and the right path is originally the middle.
5:13: 13 - So they broke the covenant. God Almighty said: So because they broke their covenant ma is redundant We cursed them We distanced them from Our mercy and made their hearts hard They do not soften to accept faith They distort the words in the Torah describing Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, and others from their places which God placed them in, meaning they change them and they forgot they left a portion a share of what they were reminded of they were commanded to do in the Torah of following Muhammad and there will not cease addressing the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace to see appear on treachery meaning betrayal from them by breaking the covenant and other things except a few of them of those who submitted (so pardon them and overlook. Indeed, God loves the doers of good) This was abrogated by the verse of the sword.
5:14: And among those who said, We are Christians is related to His statement We took their covenant as We took from the Children of Israel, the Jews and they forgot a portion of what they were reminded of in the Gospel of faith and other things, and they broke the covenant so We stirred up We caused among them enmity and hatred until the Day of Resurrection through their division and the difference in their desires, so each group disbelieves in the other and God will inform them in the Hereafter of what they used to do and He will punish them for it.
5:15: O People of the Scripture Jews and Christians There has come to you Our Messenger Muhammad making clear to you much of what you used to conceal conceal of the Scripture the Torah and the Gospel, such as the verse on stoning and its description and pardoning much of that, so he does not make it clear if there is no benefit in it except your disgrace There has come to you from God a light he is the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and a Book the Qur’an clear clear in appearance
5:16: God guides with it that is, with the Book those who follow His pleasure by believing (to the paths of peace) the paths of safety and brings them out from darkness disbelief into the light faith by His permission by His will and guides them to a straight path the religion of Islam
5:17: They have certainly disbelieved who say, God is the Messiah, the son of Mary since they made him a god, and they are the Jacobites, a group of Christians. Say, Who has the power to prevent (anything from the punishment of God if He willed to destroy the Messiah, the son of Mary, and his mother, and everyone on the earth entirely?") That is, no one has the power to do that, and if the Messiah were a god, He would have been able to do it. (And to God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them. He creates what He wills, and God is over all things) what He wills Powerful.
5:18: 18 - And the Jews and the Christians say meaning each of them We are the sons of God meaning like His sons in closeness and status, and He is like our father in mercy and compassion (and His beloved ones. Say) to them, O Muhammad Then He has not punished you for your sins if you are truthful in that, and a father does not punish his son, nor a beloved his beloved, and He has punished you, so you are liars Rather, you are human beings from among those from among those He created from among the human beings. You have what they have and upon you is what is upon them. He forgives whom He wills forgiveness is for him and He punishes whom He wills His punishment is not objectionable and to God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, and to Him is the final destination the reference.
5:19: 19 - O People of the Scripture, there has come to you Our Messenger Muhammad making clear to you the laws of the religion after a period of interruption of messengers since there was no messenger between him and Jesus, and the period of that was five hundred and sixty-nine years so that you do not say when you are punished there has not come to us any redundant (bringer of good tidings or warner. But there has come to you a bringer of good tidings and a warner) so you have no excuse then and God is over all things competent and from that is your punishment if you do not follow him.
5:20: 20 - And remember when Moses said to his people, O my people, remember the favor of God upon you when He made among you meaning from among you (prophets and made you kings with servants and retinue and gave you what He had not given to anyone in the worlds of manna and quails and splitting the sea and other things.
5:21: 21 - O my people, enter the Holy Land the purified one which God has decreed for you the command to enter it, which is the Levant and do not turn back fleeing in fear of the enemy and become losers in your efforts.
5:22: 22 - They said, O Moses, indeed therein is a people of mighty men. from the remnants of the Ad tribe, tall and strong. (And indeed, we will not enter it until they leave it. But if they leave it, then we will enter it.)
5:23: 23 - He said to them two men from among those who feared disobeying God’s command, and they were Joshua and Caleb, two of the leaders whom Moses had sent to uncover the conditions of the tyrants God had bestowed upon them infallibility, so they concealed what they had learned about their condition from anyone except Moses, unlike the rest of the leaders, who divulged it and were cowards Enter upon them through the gate the gate of the village and do not fear them, for they are bodies without hearts and when you have entered it, you will be victorious they said that out of certainty of God’s victory and the fulfillment of His promise and upon God rely, if you should be believers
5:24: 24 - (They said, "O Moses, we will never enter it as long as they are within it. So go, you and your Lord, and fight." They (we are sitting right here. He said) about fighting.
5:25: 25 - He said Moses then My Lord, I have no control over anyone except myself and (my brother) and I have no control over anyone other than them, so force them to obey so separate separate between us and the wicked people
5:26: 26 - He said the Most High to him Then it meaning the Holy Land is forbidden to them to enter it for forty years, wandering confused in the land which is nine farsakhs, Ibn Abbas said So do not grieve be sad for the wicked people It was narrated that they were walking seriously, and when morning came, they were in the place from which they started, and they walked like that during the day until they all died out except for those who had not reached twenty. It was said that they were six hundred thousand, and Aaron and Moses died in the wilderness, and it was a mercy for them and a torment for those, and Moses asked his Lord upon his death to bring him close to the Holy Land, a stone’s throw away, so He brought him close as in the hadith, and Joshua was prophesied after forty and was commanded to fight the tyrants, so he marched with those who remained with him and fought them, and it was a Friday, and the sun stood still for him for an hour until he finished fighting them, and Ahmad narrated in his Musnad the hadith The sun was not held back for any human being except for Joshua, for the nights he walked to the Holy House.
5:27: 27 - And recite O Muhammad upon them to your people the report of the two sons of Adam Abel and Cain in truth related to recite when they offered a sacrifice to God, which was a ram for Abel and a crop for Cain and it was accepted from one of them which was Abel, by a fire descending from the sky and consuming his sacrifice and it was not accepted from the other which was Cain, so he became angry and harbored envy in his soul until Adam performed Hajj he said to him I will kill you he said: Why? He said: So that you accept your sacrifice and not me (he said: God only accepts from the righteous)
5:28: 28 - If the oath lam you extend extended (your hand to me to kill me, I will not extend my hand to you to kill you. Indeed, I fear God, Lord of the worlds) in killing you.
5:29: 29 - I want you to bear return with my sin with the sin of killing me and your sin that you committed before and be among the companions of the Fire and I do not want to bear your sin if I kill you and be among them, God Almighty said and that is the recompense of the wrongdoers
5:30: 30 - So his soul made him willing made it seem good to him to kill his brother, so he killed him and became) so he became among the losers by killing him and he did not know what to do with him because he was the first death on the face of the earth from the children of Adam, so he carried him on his back.
5:31: 31 - Then God sent a crow digging in the ground digging up dirt with its beak and feet and stirring it up over a dead crow until it covered him to show him how to cover conceal the shame of his brother’s corpse. He said, Woe to me! Am I unable to be like this crow and cover my brother’s shame? So I became among those who regretted carrying him, digging for him, and covering him.
5:32: 32 - Because of that what Cain did We decreed for the Children of Israel that that is, the matter whoever kills a soul unless for a soul he killed it or without corruption that came to him in the land from disbelief or adultery or highway robbery or the like (it is as if he had killed mankind entirely. And whoever saves a life) by refraining from killing it it is as if he had saved mankind entirely Ibn Abbas said: in terms of violating its sanctity and protecting it and there had certainly come to them that is, the Children of Israel Our messengers with clear proofs miracles then indeed many of them after that are transgressors in the land exceeding the limit by disbelief and murder and other things.
5:33: 33 - And it was revealed about the two Uranians when they came to Madinah and they were sick, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, gave them permission to go out to the camels and drink from their urine and milk. When they recovered, they killed the shepherd of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and drove away the camels. The only reward of those who wage war against God and His Messenger by fighting the Muslims and strive to cause corruption in the land by cutting off roads is that they be killed or crucified or have their hands and feet on opposite sides cut off meaning their right hands and left feet or be exiled from the land or to arrange the circumstances, so killing is for the one who killed only, crucifixion is for the one who killed and took the money, cutting off is for the one who took the money and did not kill, and exile is for the one who only frightened. This was said by Ibn Abbas, and Al-Shafi’i agreed with him, and the most correct of his two opinions is that crucifixion is three times after the killing, and it was said a little before it. And what is similar to it in punishment, such as imprisonment and other things, is attached to the exile. That is the mentioned reward. For them is disgrace humiliation in this world, and for them in the Hereafter is a great punishment which is the punishment of the Fire.
5:34: 34 - Except for those who repent from the warriors and bandits (before you are able to overpower them. So know that God is Forgiving) to them for what they did Merciful to them. He used this without saying do not punish them to indicate that nothing is dropped from him by his repentance except the limits of God and not the rights of people. This is what appeared to me and I did not see anyone who addressed it, and God knows best. So if he kills and takes the money, he is killed and cut off and not crucified, and this is the most correct of the two sayings of al-Shafi’i. His repentance does not benefit him at all after being able to overpower him, and this is also the most correct of his two sayings.
5:35: 35 - O you who have believed, fear God fear His punishment by obeying Him and seek ask the means of approach to Him that which will bring you closer to Him through His obedience and strive in His cause to exalt His religion that you may succeed win.
5:36: 36 - (Indeed, those who disbelieve - if it were proven that they had all that is in the earth and as much more with it to ransom themselves thereby from the punishment of the Day of Resurrection - it would not be accepted from them, and they will have a painful punishment.)
5:37: 37 - They want they wish to get out of the Fire, but they will not get out of it, and for them is a lasting punishment eternal.
5:38: 38 - And the male thief and the female thief The al in them is a relative pronoun, the subject, and because of its resemblance to a condition, the fa’ entered its predicate, which is cut off their hands, meaning the right hand of each of them from the elbow. The Sunnah explained that what is cut off is a quarter of a dinar or more, and that if he repeats it, his left foot is cut off from the ankle joint, then the left hand, then the right foot. After that, he is punished as a punishment in the accusative case as a source for what they have earned as a deterrent a punishment for them from God, and God is Exalted in Might dominant over His affair Wise in His creation.
5:39: 39 - But whoever repents after his wrongdoing returns from stealing and amends his actions (then indeed, God will accept his repentance. Indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful) In the expression of this what was mentioned above, then his repentance does not cancel the human right to amputation and the return of the money. Yes, the Sunnah has made clear that if he pardons him before referring him to the Imam, the amputation is canceled, and Al-Shafi’i agreed with that.
5:40: 40 - Do you not know The question in it is for confirmation (that God has the dominion of the heavens and the earth. He punishes whom He wills) His punishment and forgives whom He wills His forgiveness and God is over all things competent and from it are punishment and forgiveness
5:41: 41 - O Messenger, let not those who hasten into disbelief grieve you who fall into it quickly, i.e., show it if they find an opportunity of for clarification those who say, We believe with their mouths with their tongues related to said but whose hearts do not believe and they are the hypocrites and of those who are Jews a people who listen to the lie which their rabbis fabricated, listening to it with acceptance listening from you to a people for the sake of a people others from the Jews who did not come to you and they are the people of Khaybar, among whom were two married men who committed adultery, so they disliked stoning them, so they sent the Qurayza to ask the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, about their ruling distorting the words which are in the Torah, like the verse of stoning from their places which God placed them, i.e., they change them saying to those who sent them If you are given this the distorted ruling, i.e., the flogging that Muhammad issued a fatwa for you then take it so accept it and if you are not given it Rather, he has given you a fatwa contrary to it. So beware of accepting it. And whomsoever God wills to put to trial to misguide him you will not possess for him anything against God in repelling it. Those are the ones whose hearts God did not intend to purify from disbelief. Had He intended it, it would have been for them in this world is disgrace humiliation through scandal and tribute and for them in the Hereafter is a great punishment.)
5:42: 42 - They are listeners to falsehood, devourers of illicit gain with the letter ha’ with a damma or sukoon, meaning what is forbidden, such as bribery. So if they come to you to judge between them, then judge between them or turn away from them this choice is abrogated by His statement {and that you judge between them} the verse, so it is obligatory to judge between them if they bring their case to us, and this is the more correct of the two sayings of al-Shafi’i. So if they bring their case to us with a Muslim, it is obligatory by consensus. (And if you turn away from them, they will not harm you at all. And if you judge) between them, then judge between them with justice with fairness. Indeed, God loves those who are just those who are fair in judgment, meaning He rewards them.
5:43: 43 - And how can they make you judge while they have the Torah, in which is the judgment of God of stoning, a question of astonishment, meaning that they did not intend by that to know the truth, but rather what is easier for them and then they turn away they turn away from your judgment of stoning, which is in accordance with their book after that of arbitration and those are not believers

The sā’ibah is the one that they used to leave for their gods, so nothing is carried on it. The wasīlah is the virgin she-camel that gives birth to a female first in the first generation of camels, then gives birth to a second female later, and they used to leave it for their tyrants if one of them is mated to another, and there is no male between them. The Ḥām is the male camel that strikes the numbered pairs. When it has finished striking, they leave it for the tyrants and exempt it from having anything carried on it, and they call it the Ḥāmī. If the heirs doubt them, then they should claim They betrayed by taking something or giving it to someone claiming that the deceased had bequeathed it to him, so let them swear, etc. If he finds evidence of their denial and they claim to have paid it, the closest heirs should swear to their lie and believe what they claim. The ruling is established for the two testators and is abrogated for the two witnesses. Likewise, the testimony of non-Muslims is abrogated. The consideration of the afternoon prayer is for severity, and the oath in the verse is specific to two of the closest heirs for the specific incident for which it was revealed, which is what Al-Bukhari narrated that a man from Banu Sahm went out with Tamim Al-Dari and Adi bin Bada’, meaning they were Christians. 7:190: 190 - So when He gave them a righteous son, they ascribed to Him partners and in a reading with a kasra on the shin and the tanween, meaning a partner in what He gave them by naming him Abd al-Harith, and he should not be a slave except to God, and it is not a partnership in servitude due to the infallibility of Adam. Samurah narrated on the authority of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, who said: “When Eve gave birth, Satan went around her and she did not have a living child, so he said: Name him Abd al-Harith, for he will live. So she named him, and that was from the inspiration and command of Satan.” Narrated by al-Hakim, who said it was authentic, and by al-Tirmidhi, who said it was good and strange. So God is exalted above what they associate meaning the people of Mecca with Him of idols. The sentence is a cause, in apposition to your creation, and what is between them is an objection.
7:191: 191 - Do they associate with Him in worship that which does not create anything and they themselves are created?
7:192: 192 - And they cannot help them meaning their worshippers nor can they help themselves by preventing them from those who want to harm them by breaking or otherwise, and the question is for rebuke.

7:198: 198 - And if you call them meaning the idols to guidance, they do not hear, and you see them meaning the idols, O Muhammad looking at you meaning they face you like a looker while they do not see

7:201: 201 - Indeed, those who fear God - when a specter touches them afflicts them from Satan, they remember) God’s punishment and reward and at once they see the truth from other than it, so they return.

8:1: 1 - They ask you O Muhammad about the spoils of war to whom they belong Say to them The spoils of war belong to God He places them wherever He wills and the Messenger He distributes them by the command of God, so he, may God bless him and grant him peace, distributed them among them equally. 8:2: 2 - The believers are only those those with perfect faith who, when God is mentioned that is, His threat their hearts become fearful afraid and when His verses are recited to them, it increases their faith belief and upon their Lord they rely in Him they trust, not in anyone else.
8:3: 3 - Those who establish prayer perform it with its rights and from what We have provided them We gave them they spend in the obedience of God
8:4: 4 - Those described as mentioned are the true believers truly, without a doubt. For them are degrees mansions in Paradise with their Lord and forgiveness and generous provision in Paradise.
8:5: 5 - As your Lord brought you out from your home in truth is related to brought out and indeed a party of the believers were averse to go out and the sentence is a state of the kaf brought you out and as is the predicate of a deleted subject, meaning this state in their aversion to it is like your expulsion in the state of their aversion and it was better for them so it is also like that and that is because Abu Sufyan brought a camel from the Levant and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and his companions went out to seize it and the Quraysh knew so Abu Jahl and the fighters of Mecca went out to defend it and they were the mobilization and Abu Sufyan took the camel by the coastal road and it was saved so it was said to Abu Jahl go back but he refused and went to Badr so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, consulted his companions and said God promised me one of the two groups so they agreed with him to fight the mobilization and some of them disliked that and said we did not prepare for it as God Almighty said
8:6: 6 - They argue with you about the truth after it has become clear it has become clear to them as if they were being driven to death while they are looking at it clearly in their hatred of it.

8:8: 8 - That the truth may be established and falsehood may be nullified disbelief may be obliterated even though the criminals the polytheists hate it.

8:12: 12 - When your Lord inspired to the angels whom He had provided for the Muslims that I am that is, that I am with you with aid and victory so strengthen those who believe with aid and good tidings I will cast terror fear so strike above the necks that is, the heads and strike from them every fingertip that is, the tips of the hands and feet. 8:49: 49 - When the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease say weakness of belief These people meaning the Muslims have been deceived by their religion when they went out with their small number to fight the large crowd, imagining that they would be victorious because of it. 8:50: 50 - And if you could but see O Muhammad when the angels take the souls with the letter ya and the letter ta of those who disbelieve - they strike with a state their faces and their backs with iron clubs and say to them And taste the punishment of the Burning Fire meaning the Fire. 8:53: 53 - That meaning the punishment of the disbelievers because meaning because God would not change a favor which He had bestowed upon a people exchanging it with punishment until they change what is within themselves exchanging their favor for disbelief, like the disbelievers of Mecca exchanging their food for hunger and their security for fear and sending the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to them for disbelief and turning people away from the path of God and fighting the believers and that God is Hearing and Knowing

8:60: 60 - And prepare against them to fight them whatever you are able of power The Prophet (peace and blessings of God be upon him) said: It is archery Narrated by Muslim and of tethering horses a source meaning holding them back in the way of God with which you may terrify frighten the enemy of God and your enemy meaning the infidels of Mecca and others besides them meaning other than them and they are the hypocrites or the Jews (whom you do not know but God knows. 8:65: 65 - O Prophet, urge urge the believers to fight the disbelievers if there are twenty among you, patient, they will overcome two hundred of them and if there are with the letter ya and the letter ta among you a hundred, they will overcome a thousand of those who disbelieve because they that is, because they are a people who do not understand and this is report of the sense of a command, that is, let the twenty among you fight the two hundred and the one hundred thousand and stand firm against them, then it was abrogated when they became numerous by His saying
8:66: 66 - Now God has lightened your burden and He knows that there is weakness in you - with the dhammah and fathah - in fighting ten like you so if there are with the ya and the ta of you a hundred patient, they will overcome two hundred of them and if there are a thousand of you, they will overcome two thousand by God’s permission by His will and it is report of the sense of a command, meaning that you will meet and stand firm against them and God is with the patient with His help.
