8:68: 68 - Had it not been for a previous decree from God by making the spoils and captives permissible for you a great punishment would have afflicted you for what you took of ransom)
8:69: 69 - (So eat of what you have taken as spoils, lawful and good, and fear God. Indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful.)
8:70: 70 - O Prophet, say to those in your hands of the captives and in another reading: {the captives} If God knows good in your hearts faith and sincerity He will give you better than what was taken from you of ransom by doubling it for you in this world and rewarding you in the Hereafter and forgive you your sins and God is Forgiving and Merciful
8:71: 71 - And if they intend that is, the captives to betray you with what they have shown of speech they have already betrayed God before before Badr by disbelief and He gave them power at Badr by killing and capturing, so let them expect the same if they return and God is Knowing of His creation and Wise in His making.
8:72: 72 - Indeed, those who believed and emigrated and struggled with their wealth and their lives in the cause of God and they are the emigrants and those who gave shelter the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and aided and they are the Ansar they are allies of one another in aid and inheritance and those who believed but did not emigrate - you have no guardianship over them with a kasra or fatha at all so there is no inheritance between you and them and they have no share in the spoils until they emigrate and this was abrogated at the end of the surah and if they seek your aid in religion, then you must aid them from the disbelievers except against a people between you and whom there is a treaty a treaty, so do not aid them against them and break their treaty and God is Seeing of what you do
8:73: 73 - And those who disbelieve are allies of one another in support and inheritance, so there is no inheritance between you and them unless you do so meaning take the Muslims as allies and suppress the disbelievers there will be turmoil on earth and great corruption due to the strength of disbelief and the weakness of Islam.
8:74: 74 - (And those who believed and emigrated and fought in the cause of God and those who gave shelter and aided - those are the believers, truly. For them is forgiveness and noble provision) in Paradise.
8:75: 75 - And those who believed after that is, after those who were the first to believe and emigrate and emigrated and fought with you - those are from you O emigrants and helpers and those of kinship those of kinship are more worthy of one another in inheritance than inheritance in faith and emigration mentioned in the previous verse in the Book of God the Preserved Tablet Indeed, God is Knowing of all things and from it is the wisdom of inheritance.
9:1: 1 - A declaration of innocence from God and His Messenger and a continuation to those of the polytheists with whom you made a treaty an absolute treaty or less than four months or more than that and the breach of the treaty is what is mentioned in His saying
9:2: 2 - So travel freely travel in peace, you polytheists in the land for four months the first of which is Shawwal, as evidenced by what will come, and there is no security for you after that and know that you will not escape God that is, you will not escape His punishment and that God will disgrace the disbelievers He will humiliate them in this world by killing and in the Hereafter by fire.
9:3: 3 - And an announcement informing from God and His Messenger to the people on the day of the greater pilgrimage the day of sacrifice that that is, that God is free from the polytheists and their covenants and His Messenger is also free “and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, sent Ali in the year, which is the ninth year, so he announced on the day of sacrifice in Mina with these verses and that no polytheist should perform Hajj after this year and no one should circumambulate the House naked” narrated by Al-Bukhari So if you repent from disbelief it is better for you, but if you turn away from faith (then know that you cannot cause failure to God. And give good tidings) to those who disbelieve of a painful punishment) painful, which is killing and captivity in this world and Hell in the Hereafter
9:4: 4 - Except for those of the polytheists with whom you made a treaty and then they did not fail you in anything of the conditions of the treaty and did not support aid anyone of the disbelievers against you. So fulfill your treaty with them until) the expiration of their term that you made a treaty with for God loves the righteous by fulfilling the treaties.
9:5: 5 - So when the sacred months have passed the end of the period of postponement then kill the polytheists wherever you find them in a sacred or lawful place and take them captive in castles and fortresses until they are forced to be killed or to convert to Islam and lie in wait for them at every place of ambush a path they take. The accusative of each is to remove the preposition but if they repent from disbelief and establish prayer and give zakah, then leave their way and do not interfere with them for God is Forgiving and Merciful to those who repent.
9:6: 6 - And if any of the polytheists seeks your protection raised by a verb that explains it then grant him protection grant him security so that he may hear the word of God the Qur’an then escort him to a place of safety which is the home of his people if he does not believe so that his matter may be considered that mentioned is because they are a people who do not know the religion of God, so they must hear the Qur’an in order to learn.
9:7: 7 - How that is, there is no covenant with God and His Messenger while they are disbelievers in God and His Messenger and treacherous except those with whom you made a covenant at the Sacred Mosque on the day of Hudaybiyyah, and they are the Quraysh and those who were exempted before so as long as they are upright with you they remain committed to the covenant and do not break it then be upright with them in fulfilling it, and what is conditional for God loves the righteous and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was upright in his covenant with them until they broke it by aiding Banu Bakr against Khuza'ah
9:8: 8 - How can they have a covenant while if they gain power over you they overcome you they do not observe they do not observe with you anything except kinship nor covenant a covenant, but they harm you as much as they can. The conditional clause is a state they please you with their mouths with their good speech but their hearts refuse to fulfill it and most of them are wicked breakers of the covenant
9:9: 9 - They have purchased with the verses of God the Qur’an a small price of this world, meaning they have abandoned following them for the sake of desires and whims and have averted [people] from His way His religion Indeed, evil wretched is what they were doing this work of theirs.
9:10: 10 - (They do not observe towards a believer any pact of kinship or covenant. And it is those who are the transgressors.)
9:11: 11 - (But if they repent and establish prayer and give zakah, then they are your brothers in religion. And We detail the verses for a people who know.) who reflect.
9:12: 12 - And if they break their oaths their covenants after their covenant and attack your religion criticize it then fight the leaders of disbelief its leaders, in it the apparent is placed in place of the implicit for them there are no oaths covenants in order that they may desist from disbelief. And in a reading with the kasra perhaps they will desist from disbelief.
9:13: 13 - (You are fighting a people who broke their oaths and intended to expel the Messenger from Mecca when they consulted about it in Dar al-Nadwa. And they initiated the fighting against you the first time, when they fought Khuza'ah, your allies with Banu Bakr. So what prevents you from fighting them? Do you fear them? But God has more right that you fear Him, if you are believers.)
9:14: 14 - Fight them; God will punish them kill them by your hands and disgrace them humiliate them by captivity and subjugation and give you victory over them and heal the hearts of a believing people by what Banu Khuza'ah did to them.
9:15: 15 - And He removes the anger of their hearts their distress and God accepts the repentance of whomever He wills by returning to Islam like Abu Sufyan and God is Knowing and Wise
9:16: 16 - Or meaning the hamza of denial you thought that you would be left alone and God did not know He knew the appearance of those among you who strived sincerely and did not take besides God or His Messenger or the believers as allies confidants and friends, the meaning is that the sincere ones did not appear and they are the ones described by what was mentioned from others and God is Aware of what you do
9:17: 17 - It is not for the polytheists to maintain the mosques of God in the singular and plural by entering it and sitting in it (bearing witness against themselves of disbelief. Those, their deeds have become worthless) have become void and in the Fire they will abide eternally
9:18: 18 - (The mosques of God shall be maintained only by those who believe in God and the Last Day and establish prayer and give zakah and do not fear anyone except God - it is expected that those will be among the [rightly] guided.)
9:19: 19 - Have you considered the provision of water to the pilgrims and the maintenance of the Sacred Mosque meaning the people of that equal to one who believes in God and the Last Day and strives in the cause of God? They are not equal in the sight of God in virtue and God does not guide the wrongdoing people the disbelievers. It was revealed in response to the one who said that, and he was Al-Abbas or someone else.
9:20: 20 - Those who believed and emigrated and fought in the cause of God with their wealth and their lives are greater in degree with God than others and it is those who are the successful the victorious with good.
9:21: 21 - Their Lord gives them good tidings of mercy from Him and approval and gardens for them wherein is lasting pleasure eternal.
9:22: 22 - Khalideen is an implied state (therein forever. Indeed, God has with Him a great reward)
9:23: 23 - And it was revealed about those who left emigration for the sake of their families and their trade: O you who have believed, do not take your fathers and your brothers as allies if they so desire they chose (disbelief over faith. And whoever among you takes them as allies - then it is those who are the wrongdoers)
9:24: 24 - (Say: If your fathers, your sons, your brothers, your wives, your kindred, your relatives, your wealth that you have acquired, your commerce in which you fear decline, your dwellings which you are pleased with, are dearer to you than God and His Messenger and striving in His cause, for which you have held back from migration and striving, then wait until God brings about His command, as a threat to them. God does not guide the wicked people.)
9:25: 25 - God has already given you victory in many battlefields such as Badr, Qurayzah, and Nadir and mention the day of Hunayn a valley between Mecca and Taif, that is, the day you fought Hawazin in it, and that was in Shawwal of the year eight when instead of day your great number pleased you so you said we will not be defeated today because of our small number and they were twelve thousand and the infidels were four thousand but it did not avail you at all and the earth became strait for you with its vastness ma is a source meaning with its vastness that is its height so you did not find a place to feel secure in because of the intensity of the fear that befell you then you turned back in flight defeated, and the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, stood firm on his white mule and there was no one with him except Al-Abbas and Abu Sufyan holding onto his stirrup
9:26: 26 - Then God sent down His tranquility His reassurance upon His Messenger and upon the believers so they returned to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, when Al-Abbas called them with his permission and they fought and He sent down soldiers you did not see angels and He punished those who disbelieved by killing and taking them captive and that is the reward of the disbelievers
9:27: 27 - Then God will accept the repentance of whomever He wills of them by Islam and God is Forgiving and Merciful
9:28: 28 - O you who have believed, indeed the polytheists are unclean filthy due to the filth of their inner being so let them not approach the Sacred Mosque that is, let them not enter the sanctuary after this year of theirs the ninth year of the Hijra and if you fear poverty poverty due to the cessation of their trade with you then God will enrich you from His bounty if He wills and He enriched them through conquests and the jizyah indeed God is Knowing and Wise
9:29: 29 - Fight those who do not believe in God or in the Last Day otherwise they would have believed in the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and do not forbid what God and His Messenger have forbidden such as alcohol and do not follow the religion of truth the established one that abrogates other religions, which is the religion of Islam of those a clarification of those who were given the Scripture meaning the Jews and Christians until they pay the jizyah the tax imposed on them every year with their own hands a state, meaning obedient or with their own hands, not entrusted with it and they are submissive humiliated and obedient to the rule of Islam.
9:30: 30 - And the Jews said, Ezra is the son of God, and the Christians said, The Messiah Jesus (is the son of God. That is their statement with their mouths.) They have no evidence for it, but rather they imitate they resemble in it the statement of those who disbelieved before from their fathers, imitating them. (God has cursed them. How) how are they turned away from the truth when evidence has been established.
9:31: 31 - They have taken their rabbis the Jewish scholars and their monks the Christian worshippers as lords besides God as they followed them in making lawful what God has forbidden and prohibiting what He has made lawful (and the Messiah, the son of Mary. And they were not commanded) in the Torah and the Gospel except to worship that is, to worship (one God; there is no god but Him. Glory be to Him) purifying Him from what they associate with Him
9:32: 32 - They want to extinguish the light of God His law and His proofs with their mouths with their statements about Him but God refuses except to perfect to make manifest His light, even though the disbelievers dislike it
9:33: 33 - It is He who has sent His Messenger Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace with guidance and the religion of truth to manifest it to make it superior over all religion all religions that oppose it even though the polytheists dislike it that.
9:34: 34 - O you who have believed, indeed many of the rabbis and monks devour take the wealth of people unjustly such as bribery in judgment and avert people from the way of God His religion and those subject who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it that is, the hoards in the way of God that is, they do not pay its due for zakat and charity so give them tidings inform them of a painful punishment painful
9:35: 35 - On the Day when it will be heated in the fire of Hell, their foreheads, their flanks, and their backs will be branded with it it will be burned and their skins will be stretched out until they are all covered with it, and it will be said to them (This is what you hoarded for yourselves, so taste what you used to hoard meaning its recompense.
9:36: 36 - The number of months that is considered for the year with God is twelve months in the Book of God the Preserved Tablet (on the Day He created the heavens and the earth. Of them) that is, the months four are sacred that are sacred, they are Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, and Rajab That that is, their prohibition is the upright religion the straight one so wrong not yourselves during them that is, the sacred months with sins, for they are of greater burden, and it was said in all the months (and fight the polytheists all together all together in all the months as they fight you all together, and know that God is with the righteous with aid and victory.
9:37: 37 - The postponement i.e. the delay of the sanctity of a month to another, as the Jahiliyyah used to do by delaying the sanctity of Muharram when it became due while they were fighting until Safar is only an increase in disbelief for their disbelief in the ruling of God concerning it He misguides with the dammah and fathah of the ya’ those who disbelieve thereby, they make it lawful i.e. the postponement one year and forbid it another year in order to agree to make lawful one month and forbid another in its place the number of what God has forbidden of the months, so they do not increase the prohibition of four nor decrease it and they do not look at their specifics (and forbid what God has forbidden. The evil of their deeds has been made attractive to them) so they think it is good and God does not guide the disbelieving people.
9:38: 38 - And it was revealed when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called the people to the Battle of Tabuk and they were in hardship, distress and heat, so it was difficult for them O you who have believed, what is [the matter] with you that when you are told to go forth in the cause of God you cling heavily by assimilating the ta’ in the original into the triangle and bringing the hamzat al-wasl, meaning you are slow and turn away from jihad to the land and sitting in it, and the question is for rebuke Are you satisfied with the life of this world and its pleasures instead of the Hereafter meaning instead of its pleasures Then what is the enjoyment of the life of this world compared to the enjoyment of the Hereafter except little insignificant
9:39: 39 - Except by assimilating La into the conditional Nun of In in both places If you go forth go out with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, for jihad He will punish you with a painful punishment painful and will replace you with another people that is, He will bring them in your place and do not harm Him that is, God or the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace at all by not supporting him, for God is the supporter of His religion and God is over all things competent and from that is the support of His religion and His Prophet
9:40: 40 - If you do not aid him meaning the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace God has aided him when when those who disbelieved expelled him from Mecca, meaning they forced him to go out when they wanted to kill him or imprison him or banish him to Dar al-Nadwa the second of two a state, meaning one of two and the other is Abu Bakr, meaning God aided him in such a state and will not let him down in any other when a substitute for when before it they were in the cave a tunnel in Mount Thawr when a second substitute he said to his companion Abu Bakr when he said to him when he saw the feet of the polytheists: If one of them looked under his feet he would see us Do not grieve, God is with us with His aid so God sent down His tranquility His reassurance upon him it was said upon the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and it was said upon Abu Bakr and supported him meaning the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace with soldiers you did not see angels in the cave and the places where he fought and made the word of those who disbelieved meaning the call to polytheism the lowest The defeated and the word of God meaning the word of testimony is supreme the apparent and dominant and God is Exalted in Might in His kingdom and Wise in His creation
9:41: 41 - Go forth, whether light or heavy active or inactive, and it was said strong and weak or rich and poor, and it was abrogated by the verse {There is no blame upon the weak} (and strive with your wealth and your lives in the cause of God. That is better for you, if you only knew) that it is better for you, so do not be sluggish.
9:42: 42 - And it was revealed about the hypocrites who stayed behind: If what you called them to had been a worldly pleasure near easy to obtain and a straight journey moderate they would have followed you seeking the spoils but the distance became too far for them so they stayed behind and they will swear by God when you return to them if we were able to go out we would have gone out with you they destroy themselves) with the false oath and God knows that they are liars in saying that.
9:43: 43 - And the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had given permission to a group of people to stay behind, based on his own effort, so he was rebuked and pardoned, to reassure his heart May God pardon you for giving them permission to stay behind, and why did you not leave them until it becomes clear to you who are truthful in the excuse and you know the liars in it.
9:44: 44 - Those who believe in God and the Last Day do not ask your permission to stay behind (to strive with their wealth and their lives. And God is Knowing of the righteous)
9:45: 45 - Only those ask your permission to stay behind who do not believe in God and the Last Day and whose hearts are in doubt doubt about the religion (so in their doubt they waver) are confused.
9:46: 46 - And if they had intended to go out with you they would have prepared for it some equipment equipment and provisions but God disliked their going out that is, He did not want them to go out so He held them back their laziness and it was said to them Stay with those who stay behind the sick, the women and the children, that is, God Almighty decreed that.
9:47: 47 - If they had gone forth with you, they would not have increased you except in confusion corruption by discouraging the believers and they would have spread gossip among you that is, they would have hastened among you by spreading slander seeking to cause discord among you by sowing enmity and among you are those who listen to them what they say is an acceptance and God is Knowing of the wrongdoers
9:48: 48 - They had sought for you discord before when you first arrived in the city and had upset matters for you that is, they thought about plotting against you and invalidating your religion until the truth victory came and became apparent about the command of God) His religion while they were averse to it, so they entered into it openly.
9:49: 49 - And among them is he who says, Give me leave to stay behind and do not tempt me and he is Al-Jadd bin Qais. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, said to him: Would you like to fight the yellow people? He said, I am infatuated with women and I fear that if I see the yellow people, I will not be able to bear them and will be tempted. God Almighty said, Indeed, they have fallen into temptation) by staying behind, and it was read as fell and indeed, Hell will encompass the disbelievers there is no escape for them from it.
9:50: 50 - If good befalls you such as victory or spoils it distresses them, but if a disaster befalls you severe they say, We took our decision with firmness when we lagged behind (before before this sin and they turn away while they are rejoicing at what has befallen you.
9:51: 51 - Say to them Nothing will happen to us except what God has decreed for us an occurrence He is our protector our supporter and the one who takes care of our affairs and in God let the believers put their trust
9:52: 52 - (Say: Do you wait for us except one of the two good outcomes while we are waiting for God to afflict you with a punishment from Himself or by our hands?) by a calamity from the sky or by our hands. So wait for us for that for we are with you, waiting.
9:53: 53 - (Say: Spend) in obedience to God (willingly or unwillingly. He will not accept from you) what you have spent for you are a people of immorality. The command here means report.
9:54: 54 - And what prevented their expenditures from being accepted from them with the letter ya and the letter ta except that they is the subject, and that taqbal is the object disbelieved in God and in His Messenger and did not come to prayer except while they were lazy sluggish and did not spend except while they were reluctant the expenditure because they considered it a loss
9:55: 55 - So let not their wealth or their children impress you meaning, do not approve of Our blessings upon them, for they are a lure God only intends to punish them meaning, to punish them with them in the life of this world with the hardship and calamities they encounter in collecting it and that their souls depart come out while they are disbelievers so He punishes them in the Hereafter with the most severe punishment.
9:56: 56 - And they swear by God that they are of you meaning believers but they are not of you, but they are a people who fear they fear that you will treat them like the polytheists, so they swear out of fear.
9:57: 57 - If they could find a refuge to which they could resort or caves catacombs or an entrance a place to enter they would turn to it, rushing they would rush to enter it and leave you in a haste that nothing can stop, like the horses’ hordes.
9:58: 58 - And among them are those who criticize you criticize you regarding the division (of the alms. If they are given from it, they are pleased; but if they are not given from it, at once they become angry.)
9:59: 59 - And if they had been satisfied with what God and His Messenger had given them of spoils and the like (and had said, “Sufficient for us is God. God will give us from His bounty and so will His Messenger” of other spoils that will suffice us (indeed, we are desirous of God) that He should enrich us, and the answer to if only would have been better for them.
9:60: 60 - The alms Zakat is only for the poor) who do not find what is sufficient for them and the needy who do not find what is sufficient for them and those employed to collect it i.e. the alms from the collector, the distributor, the scribe and the gatherer and those whose hearts are to be reconciled to convert to Islam or to establish their Islam or to convert their peers or to defend the Muslims. The first and last are not given today according to Al-Shafi’i, may God be pleased with him, due to the glory of Islam, unlike the others, who are given according to the most correct opinion and for the release of slaves i.e. those who are freed and the debtors those who are in debt if they borrowed for a reason other than disobedience or repented and they do not have the means to repay or to reconcile the matter even if they are rich and in the cause of God i.e. those who are engaged in jihad and do not have the means to reconcile even if they are rich and for the wayfarer who is stranded on his journey an obligation made accusative by his action that is estimated from God, and God is Knowing of His creation Wise in His creation, so it is not permissible to spend it for anyone other than these nor to prevent a category If he finds it, the imam divides it among them equally, and he has the right to prefer some individuals of the category over others. The lam indicates the obligation to include all of its individuals, but it is not obligatory for the owner of the money if he divides it due to his difficulty. Rather, it is sufficient to give three of each category, and it is not sufficient to give less than that, as the plural form indicates. The Sunnah clarified that the condition of the one given from it is Islam and that he not be a Hashemite or a Mutalibi.
9:61: 61 - And among them meaning the hypocrites are those who harm the Prophet by criticizing him and transmitting his hadith and say when they are forbidden from doing so lest it reach him He is an ear meaning he hears everything that is said and accepts it, so if we swear to him that we did not tell the truth Say He is an ear a listener of good for you not a listener of evil who believes in God and believes truthfully for the believers in what they told him, not for others, and the lam is added to differentiate between the faith of submission and other than it and mercy in the nominative case in apposition to ear and in the genitive case in apposition to good for those who believe among you, and those who harm the Messenger of God will have a painful punishment
9:62: 62 - They swear to you by God O believers, regarding what has reached you about them of the harm they have done to the Messenger, that they did not come to him to please you, but God and His Messenger are more deserving that they please Him with obedience if they are truly believers and the unification of the pronoun is due to the connection between the two satisfactions, and the report of God or His Messenger is omitted.
9:63: 63 - Do they not know that that is, the matter whoever opposes contradicts God and His Messenger - for him is the fire of Hell as a recompense to abide therein - that is the great disgrace
9:64: 64 - The hypocrites are warned fear that a Surah will be sent down upon them that is, the believers informing them of what is in their hearts of hypocrisy, while they still mock. (Say: Mock, for God will bring out) reveal what you fear of your hypocrisy.
9:65: 65 - And if the oath lam you ask them about their mockery of you and the Qur’an while they were walking with you to Tabuk they will surely say apologetically We were only conversing and joking in the conversation to pass the way and we did not intend that Say to them Was it at God and His verses and His Messenger that you were mocking
9:66: 66 - Do not apologize for it for you have disbelieved after your belief meaning your disbelief has become apparent after showing faith If We pardon with the letter yaa in the passive form and the letter nun in the active form a party among you for their sincerity and repentance like Jahsh bin Himyar We will punish with the letter taa and the letter nun a party because they were criminals persisting in hypocrisy and mockery.
9:67: 67 - The hypocrites, men and women, are of one another meaning they are similar in religion, like parts of one thing. They enjoin what is wrong disbelief and sins and forbid what is right faith and obedience and they withhold their hands from spending in obedience. They have forgotten God they have abandoned His obedience so He has forgotten them He abandoned them out of His kindness. Indeed, the hypocrites are the wicked.
9:68: 68 - (God has promised the hypocrites, men and women, and the disbelievers the fire of Hell, wherein they will abide eternally. It is sufficient for them. God has cursed them, and for them is a lasting punishment.)
9:69: 69 - You, O hypocrites, are like those before you who were stronger than you and had more wealth and children. They enjoyed their portion of this world. You, O hypocrites, are like those who were before you. You enjoyed your portion of this world as those before you enjoyed their portion. And you plunged into falsehood and slander against the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, like those who plunged into it. Their deeds have become worthless in this world and the Hereafter, and it is those who are the losers.
9:70: 70 - Did there not come to them the report of those before them - the people of Noah and 'Aad the people of Hud and Thamud the people of Salih and the people of Abraham and the companions of Madyan the people of Shu'ayb and the overturned cities the cities of the people of Lot, meaning their people their messengers came to them with clear proofs with miracles, but they denied them and were destroyed so it was not God who would wrong them by punishing them without sin but they were wronging themselves by committing sin
9:71: 71 - (And the believing men and believing women are allies of one another. They enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong and establish prayer and give zakah and obey God and His Messenger. Those - God will have mercy upon them. Indeed, God is Exalted in Might and Wise.) Nothing is too difficult for Him to fulfill His promise and His threat. Wise. He does not put anything except in its proper place.
9:72: 72 - (God has promised the believing men and believing women gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they abide eternally, and pleasant dwellings in gardens of perpetual bliss. And the pleasure of God is greater. That is what is the great attainment.)
9:73: 73 - O Prophet, strive against the disbelievers with the sword and the hypocrites with the tongue and argument and be harsh upon them with rebuke and hatred and their refuge is Hell, and wretched is the destination The reference is
9:74: 74 - They swear that is, the hypocrites by God that they did not say what you have heard about them of insults and they did say the word of disbelief and disbelieved after their Islam they showed disbelief after showing Islam and they intended what they did not achieve of killing the Prophet on the night of Al-Aqabah when he returned from Tabuk and they were a few dozen men, so Ammar bin Yasir struck the faces of the camels when they deceived him and they turned back and they did not find fault they denied except that God and His Messenger enriched them from His bounty with spoils after their great need, and the meaning is that they did not gain from Him except this and it is not something to be found fault with so if they repent from hypocrisy and believe in you it will be better for them, but if they turn away from faith God will punish them with a painful punishment in this world by killing and in the Hereafter with fire and they will have on earth no protector to protect them from Him nor helper to prevent them
9:75: 75 - And among them is he who made a covenant with God, If He should give us from His bounty, we will surely give in charity in which the ta' in the original is assimilated into the sad and we will surely be among the righteous and he is Tha'laba bin Hatib, he asked the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to pray for him that God would provide him with wealth and give from it to every person who has a right to it his right, so he prayed for him and was given plenty, so he stopped attending Friday prayers and congregational prayers and withheld zakat, as God Almighty said:
9:76: 76 - But when He gave them of His bounty, they were stingy with it and turned away from obeying God and they were averse
9:77: 77 - So He made their end (hypocrisy fixed in their hearts until the Day they meet Him meaning God, which is the Day of Resurrection because they broke their promise to God and because they used to lie about it. Then after that, his blessings came to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he said, God has prevented me from accepting it from you, so he began to throw dirt on his head. Then he came to Abu Bakr, but he did not accept it, then to Umar, but he did not accept it, then to Uthman, but he did not accept it, and he died during his time.
9:78: 78 - Do they not know that is, the hypocrites that God knows their secrets what they concealed in themselves and their private conversations what they whisper among themselves and that God is Knower of the unseen what is hidden from sight. And when the verse on charity was revealed, a man came and gave a lot of charity, so the hypocrites said, This is hypocrisy. And a man came and gave a sa` of charity, so they said, God is independent of this man's charity. So the verse was revealed.
9:79: 79 - Those subject who criticize criticize those who volunteer those who give voluntary charity among the believers and those who find only their effort their energy so they bring it and they ridicule them and the predicate God ridiculed them He rewarded them for their ridicule and for them is a painful punishment
9:80: 80 - Ask forgiveness O Muhammad for them or do not ask forgiveness for them He gave him the choice of asking forgiveness or not. He said, may God bless him and grant him peace: I was given a choice and I chose, meaning asking forgiveness. Narrated by Al-Bukhari If you ask forgiveness for them seventy times, God will not forgive them. It was said that what is meant by seventy is the exaggeration in the frequency of asking forgiveness. In Al-Bukhari, there is a hadith: If I knew that if I increased to seventy, I would be forgiven, I would have increased to that. It was also said that what is meant is the specific number in his hadith: And I will increase to seventy. So he explained to him the decisiveness of forgiveness with the verse: It is the same for them whether you ask forgiveness for them or do not ask forgiveness for them. That is because they disbelieved in God and His Messenger, and God does not guide the wicked people.
9:81: 81 - Those who stayed behind from Tabuk rejoiced at their sitting behind that is, their sitting behind the Messenger of God that is, after the Messenger of God, and they disliked to strive with their wealth and their lives in the cause of God, and they said that is, some of them said to each other Do not go forth go out to jihad (in the heat. Say, the fire of Hell is more intense in heat) than Tabuk, so it is better for them to protect themselves from it by not staying behind if they had understood knew that, they would not have stayed behind.
9:82: 82 - So let them laugh a little in this world and weep in the Hereafter much as recompense for what they used to earn report about their condition in the imperative form

17:1: 1 - Glory be to Him meaning glorification who took His servant Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, by night is an accusative of circumstance, and the night journey is the night journey, and the benefit of mentioning it is to indicate by its indefiniteness the reduction of its duration from the Sacred Mosque meaning Mecca to the Farthest Mosque Jerusalem because of its distance from it whose surroundings We have blessed with fruits and rivers to show him of Our signs the wonders of Our power He is the Hearing, the Seeing meaning the One who knows the sayings and actions of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, so He bestowed upon him the Night Journey, which included his meeting with the prophets and his ascension to the heavens and seeing the wonders of the kingdom and his communion with God Almighty, for he, may God bless him and grant him peace,
17:2: 2 - God Almighty said And We gave Moses the Scripture the Torah and made it a guide for the Children of Israel, Do not take besides Me a guardian entrusting their affairs to him. In the reading, take with the upper particle, as a shift, because an is redundant and the statement is implied.
17:3: 3 - The descendants of those whom We carried with Noah in the ship He was a grateful servant very grateful to Us, praising Us in all his circumstances.
17:4: 4 - And We decreed We revealed to the Children of Israel in the Book the Torah that you will surely cause corruption in the land the land of Ash-Sham with sins twice and you will surely reach a state of great haughtiness seeking great transgression.

17:6: 6 - Then We gave you back the upper hand the state and victory over them after a hundred years by killing Goliath and We reinforced you with wealth and children and made you more numerous clan

If we called upon any god other than God, it would be obligatory.

18:16: And when you have withdrawn from them and what they worship except God, then take refuge in the cave. Your Lord will spread out for you from His mercy and will prepare for you from your affair ease.) With a kasra on the m and a fatha on the f and vice versa, what you ease with of lunch and dinner.
18:17: And you will see the sun, when it rises, inclining away from their cave on the right, towards it, and when it sets, passing them by on the left, while they are in an open space thereof, where the coolness and breeze of the wind reach them. That, mentioned, is among the signs of God, evidence of His power. He whom God guides is the one who is guided, and he whom He sends astray - you will never find for him a protector to guide him.
18:18: And you would think them, if you saw them, to be awake, i.e. alert because their eyes are swollen, plural of yaqiz with a kasra on the qaf, while they are asleep, sleeping, plural of raqid, and We turn them to the right and to the left, lest the earth consume their flesh, and their dog stretches out his forelegs, his hands, at the entrance, in the courtyard of the cave. And when they turned, he turned like them in sleep and wakefulness, and if you had looked at them, you would have turned away from them in flight, and you would have been filled, with terror, with sukoon and damma on the ‘ayn. God prevented them with terror from anyone entering upon them.
18:19: And likewise) as We did to them what We mentioned We sent them We woke them up that they might ask each other about their condition and the length of their stay (One of them said, How long did you stay? They said, We stayed a day or part of a day because they entered the cave at sunrise and were sent at sunset, so they thought that it was sunset on the day of entry. Then (they said) hesitating about that Your Lord knows best how long you stayed, so send one of you with your silver with a silent ra' and a broken ra' this to the city it is said that it is now called Tartus with a fatha on the ra' and let him see which of them is the best food meaning the foods of the city are the most permissible and let him bring you provision from it and let him be gentle and not let anyone know about you
18:20: If they gain the upper hand over you, they will stone you or return you to their religion. And you will never succeed if you return to their religion.
18:21: 21 - And thus as We resurrected them We made known We informed them their people and the believers that they might know that is, their people that the promise of God of resurrection is true by way of the fact that He who is able to put them to sleep for a long time and keep them in their condition without food is able to revive the dead and that there is no doubt there is no doubt about the Hour, when the object of We made known they dispute that is, the believers and the disbelievers among themselves their affair the affair of the young men to build around them and they said that is, the disbelievers Build over them that is, around them a structure that will conceal them their Lord is most knowing of them. Those who prevailed over their affair) the affair of the young men, that is, the believers, said We will surely build over them around them a mosque in which to pray, and that was done at the entrance to the cave.

None knows them except a few." Ibn Abbas said, I am one of the few, and he mentioned them as seven (so do not dispute) argue about them except an outward dispute about what was revealed to you and do not ask a fatwa about them from the People of the Book, the Jews anyone and the people of Mecca asked him about the story of the People of the Cave, so he said, I will tell you about it tomorrow. 18:23: And do not say of anything that is, for the sake of something I will do that tomorrow that is, in the future.

Al-Hasan and others said as long as he is in the assembly and say, perhaps my Lord will guide me to something nearer than this from the story of the people of the cave in indicating my prophethood to right guidance guidance, and God did that.
18:25: And they remained in their cave for three hundred) with the tanween years an explanatory apposition for three hundred. These three hundred years are solar years according to the People of the Book, and the lunar years are nine years more than them according to the Arabs. It was mentioned in His statement and they added nine meaning nine years. So three hundred solar years are three hundred and nine lunar years.
18:26: Say: God knows best how long they stayed of those about whom they differed, which is what was mentioned previously. To Him belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth, meaning His knowledge. He sees it, meaning of God, which is a form of astonishment. And He hears it, meaning what I see and what I hear, and they are both metaphorical. What is meant is that nothing is hidden from His sight and hearing. They have, for the people of the heavens and the earth, besides Him, no protector, no helper, and He does not share in His rule anyone, because He is independent of a partner.
18:27: And recite what has been revealed to you of the Book of your Lord. None can change His words, and you will not find besides Him any refuge.
18:28: And be patient with those who call upon their Lord morning and evening, desiring His countenance, not worldly pleasures, and they are the poor. And do not turn your eyes away from them, desiring adornments of worldly life, and do not obey one whose heart We have made heedless of Our remembrance, that is, the Qur’an, namely, Uyaynah ibn Hisn and his companions, and who follows his own desire, in polytheism, and whose affair is ever [in] excess.

Al-Bukhari narrated a hadith that Moses stood up as a preacher among the Children of Israel and was asked who among the people is the most knowledgeable, so he said I am. So God rebuked him because he did not refer the knowledge to Him, so God revealed to him that I have a servant at the confluence of the two seas who is more knowledgeable than you. Moses said, O Lord, how can I deal with him? He said, Take a fish with you and put it in a basket, and wherever you lose the fish, it is there. So he took a fish and put it in a basket, then he set out, and his servant Joshua bin Nun set out with him until they came to the rock and put their heads down and slept, and the fish stirred in the basket, so it came out of it and fell into the sea, so it took its way in the sea as a tunnel, and God held back the fish from moving in the water, so it became like a canopy over it. When he woke up, his companion forgot to tell him about the fish, so they set out for the rest of their day. And their night, until it was morning, Moses said to his boy, Bring us our lunch, until His saying, And he took his way in the sea, a wonder. He said, And the fish had a school, and Moses and his boy were a wonder.

24:1: 1 - This Surat Anzalnahu wa Furqanahu is light and heavy due to the abundance of what is imposed in it and We sent down in it clear verses with clear meanings that you may remember by assimilating the second ta’ into the dhal, so that you may be admonished.
24:2: 2 - The adulterer and the adulteress who are not married are to be stoned according to the Sunnah. Al in what was mentioned is a relative pronoun and it is the subject. Because of its resemblance to the condition, the fa’ entered into its predicate, which is so flog each of them with a hundred lashes a blow. It is said that he flogged him, he flogged him. And in addition to that, according to the Sunnah, a year’s exile, and the slave is to be given half of what was mentioned. And let not pity for them detain you in the matter of God’s religion His ruling that you leave some of their punishment if you believe in God and the Last Day the Day of Resurrection. In this there is an incitement to what preceded the condition and it is its answer or indicates its answer. And let a group of believers witness their punishment the flogging () it was said three and it was said four, the number of witnesses to adultery.
24:3: 3 - The adulterer shall not marry he marries except an adulteress or a polytheist, and the adulteress shall not marry except an adulterer or a polytheist that is, what is appropriate for each of them is what was mentioned and that is forbidden that is, marrying adulteresses to the believers the good ones. This was revealed because they were poor immigrants, that they should marry the prostitutes of the polytheists, even though they were well-off, so that they could spend on them. It was said that the prohibition was specific to them, and it was said that it was general, and it was abrogated by the Most High’s saying: And marry those among you who are single.
24:4: 4 - And those who accuse chaste women of adultery and do not produce four witnesses to their adultery by seeing them — flog them each one of them with eighty lashes and do not accept their testimony concerning anything (ever after. And those are the wicked) for committing a major sin.

24:11: 11 - Indeed, those who brought the slander the worst lie against Aisha, may God be pleased with her, the mother of the believers, by slandering her are a group among you a group of believers, she said Hassan bin Thabit, Abdullah bin Ubayy, Mastah, and Hamna bint Jahsh Do not think it O believers other than the group is evil for you; rather, it is good for you May God reward you for it and show the innocence of Aisha and whoever came with her from it, and he is Safwan, for she said: I was with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, in a raid after the veil was revealed, so he finished it and returned and approached Madinah and gave the call to depart one night, so I walked and finished my business and the women came, lightly, We only eat a necklace, so I went back to look for it and they carried my litter on my camel, thinking I was in it, and the women were lightly, We only eat a necklace, which is with a damma on the muhlah and a sukoon on the lam, from food, meaning a little, and I found my necklace and came after they had departed, so I sat in the inn where I was and I thought that the people They will miss me and return to me, so my eyes overcame me and I slept. Safwan had gone out from behind the army, so he went down late at night to rest, so he set out from it and in the morning he was at his home and saw the blackness of a sleeping person, that is, his person, so he recognized me when he saw me. He used to see me before the veil, so I woke up with his I seek refuge in God when he recognized me, that is, his saying, Indeed we belong to God and to Him we shall return. So I covered my face with my cloak, that is, I covered it with the sheet. By God, he did not say a word to me nor did I hear a word from him except his I seek refuge in God when he dismounted his mount and stepped on its hand, so I mounted it, and he set off leading the mount with me until we reached the army after they had dismounted, raging at noon, that is, those who were raging were standing in a place and raging from the intense heat, so those who perished perished, and the one who bore the brunt of it among them was Abdullah bin Ubayy bin Salul. End of her statement, narrated by the two sheikhs. God Almighty said, For every man among them that is, upon him is what he has earned of sin in that and he among them who took upon himself the greater part of it that is, he bore most of it, so he began to delve into it and spread it, and he is Abdullah bin Ubayy for him is a great punishment which is the Fire in The Hereafter

And in the verse there is a predominance of males over females

And God is All-Encompassing) of His creation and Knowing of them.
24:33: 33 - And let those who find not the means for marriage abstain what they can marry with of dowry and maintenance from fornication until God enriches them He gives them plenty of His bounty so they can marry. And those who seek a contract meaning a free contract from among those whom your right hands possess of male and female slaves then free them if you know good in them meaning trustworthiness and the ability to earn to pay the money for the contract. Its formula is, for example, he freed you for two thousand in two months, one thousand each month. If you pay them, you are free. He says, I accept. And give them an order to the masters from the wealth of God which He has given you what they can use to help them in fulfilling what they have committed to you. And do not compel your female slaves your female slaves to prostitution fornication if they desire chastity to abstain from it. This desire is the subject of compulsion, so there is no meaning to the condition. That you may seek by compulsion the goods of worldly life. It was revealed about Abdullah bin Ubayy who forced his female slaves to Earning money through adultery and whoever compels them - then indeed, after their compulsion, God is Forgiving and Merciful to them.
24:34: 34 - And We have certainly sent down to you clear verses with the opening and closing of the ya’ in this surah, in which He explained what was mentioned or a clear proof and an example of amazing report, which is the report of Aisha from those who passed on before you that is, from the same kind as their examples, that is, their amazing report, like the report of Joseph and Mary and an admonition for the righteous in the words of God Almighty: And let not pity for them detain you in the religion of God. 29:8: 8 - And We have enjoined upon man, concerning his parents, good treatment meaning, a good recommendation that he be dutiful to them And if they endeavor to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge of associating with Me do not obey them in associating with Me that of which you have no knowledge) in accordance with reality and has no meaning and they are not to be obeyed in associating partners to Me is your return, and I will inform you about what you used to do and I will reward you for it.
29:9: 9 - And those who believe and do righteous deeds - We will surely admit them among the righteous the prophets and saints by gathering them with them.
29:10: 10 - And among the people is he who says, We have believed in God, but when he is harmed for the sake of God, he considers the trial of the people that is, their harm to him to be the punishment of God in fear of him, so he obeys them and becomes a hypocrite. And if the lam of an oath there comes to the believers from your Lord and they take spoils, he will surely say the nun of the subject was deleted from it due to the succession of the nuns and the waw is the plural pronoun due to the meeting of two quiescent letters Indeed, we were with you in faith, so let us share in the spoils. God Almighty said Is not God most knowing that is, most knowledgeable of what is in the breasts of the worlds their hearts of faith and hypocrisy? Yes.
29:11: 11 - And God will surely know those who believe in their hearts and He will surely know the hypocrites so He will punish both groups. The lam in the two verbs is a lam of oath.
29:12: 12 - And those who disbelieved said to those who believed, Follow our way our religion and we will bear your sins in following us if it was and the command has the meaning of report. God Almighty said (But they will not bear any of their sins. Indeed, they are liars) in that.
29:13: 13 - And they will bear their burdens their sins and other burdens besides their burdens by saying to the believers, Follow our path, and by leading astray their imitators and they will be asked on the Day of Resurrection about what they used to invent lying about God, a question of rebuke. The lam in the two verbs is a lam of oath, and their subject is omitted, the waw, and the noun of the subject is the noun of the subject.
29:14: 14 - And We had certainly sent Noah to his people when he was forty years old or more and he remained among them a thousand years minus fifty years calling them to the Oneness of God, but they denied him and the flood overtook them the abundant water swirled around them and overcame them, so they drowned while they were wrongdoers polytheists.
29:15: 15 - So We saved him Noah and the companions of the ship who were with him in it and made it a sign a lesson for the worlds for those who came after them among the people if they disobeyed their messengers. Noah lived after the flood for sixty years or more until the people became numerous.

29:17: 17 - You only worship idols besides God meaning other than Him and you invent falsehood you falsely say that idols are partners of God Indeed, those you worship besides God do not possess for you any provision they are not able to provide you so seek provision from God ask it from Him (and worship Him and be grateful to Him. To Him you will be returned)
29:18: 18 - And if you deny that is, you deny me, O people of Mecca then nations before you have denied before me and the Messenger has no duty except to convey clearly except to convey clearly in these two stories as consolation for the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and God Almighty said about his people
29:19: 19 - Have they not seen how with the letter Ya and the letter Taa they look God begins creation then it is with the letter Damma and it is read with the letter Fatha from Badaa and Abadaa meaning that is, He creates them from the beginning He repeats it it is if creation as He began them that is easy for what was mentioned of the first and second creation God. Say) how do they deny the second?
29:20: 20 - Say, Travel through the land and observe how He began creation for those who came before you and caused them to die Then God will produce the final creation lengthened and shortened with the sukoon of the sheen Indeed, God is over all things competent and from this comes the beginning and the return.
29:21: 21 - He punishes whom He wills His punishment and has mercy on whom He wills His mercy and to Him you will be returned you will be returned
29:22: 22 - And you will not be able to escape your Lord from comprehending you on the earth or in the heaven if you were in them, that is, you would not escape Him and you have no protector or helper besides God other than Him to protect you from Him (or helper) to protect you from His punishment.
29:23: 23 - And those who disbelieve in the signs of God and the meeting with Him meaning the Qur’an and the Resurrection those have despaired of My mercy My Paradise and those will have a painful punishment painful
29:24: 24 - God Almighty said in the story of Abraham, peace be upon him: And the answer of his people was only that they said, ‘Kill him or burn him.’ But God saved him from the Fire into which they had thrown him by making it cool and safe for him. Indeed in that his saving from it are signs which are that it does not affect him despite its greatness and its extinguishing and the creation of a garden in its place in a short time for a people who believe who believe in the Oneness of God and His power because they are the ones who benefit from it.
29:25: 25 - And Abraham said, You have only taken idols besides God to worship them, and what is a source of affection between you, the predicate of in and in the accusative reading it is an object for it, and what is the entire meaning: you have loved one another in worshipping them in the life of this world, then on the Day of Resurrection some of you will disbelieve in others the leaders will disown the followers and some of you will curse others the followers will curse the leaders and your refuge the destination of all of you is the Fire, and you will have no helpers from it.
29:26: 26 - So Lot believed in him he believed in Abraham and he said Abraham Indeed, I am an emigrant from my people to my Lord as my Lord had commanded me and he left his people and emigrated from the blackness of Iraq to the Levant Indeed, it is He who is the Exalted in Might in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
29:28: 28 - And mention and Lot when he said to his people, Indeed, you by making the two hamzas pronounced, facilitating the second, and inserting an alif between them in both cases in both places will certainly commit an immorality such as no one in the worlds has preceded you with mankind and jinn.
29:29: 29 - Do you indeed approach men and block the way the path of passers-by by your indecent actions towards those who pass by you, so people leave you and commit in your assemblies your talk the evil some of you committing indecent acts towards others So the answer of his people was only that they said, Bring us the punishment of God, if you should be of the truthful in the ugliness of that and that the punishment will descend upon its doers
29:30: 30 - He said, My Lord, support me by fulfilling my words regarding bringing down punishment on the corrupting people who disobeyed by coming to men. So God answered his prayer.
29:31: 31 - And when Our messengers came to Abraham with the good tidings of Isaac and Jacob after him they said, Indeed, we will destroy the people of this city the city of Lot indeed, its people were wrongdoers disbelievers.
29:32: 32 - He said Abraham (Indeed, Lot is in it. They said) the messengers (We know best who is in it. We will surely save him) with the emphasis and the emphasis and his family, except his wife; she was among those who remained behind those who remained in the punishment.
29:33: 33 - And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed because of them he was saddened by them and was straitened by them because they were handsome in the form of guests, so his people feared for them, so they informed him that they were messengers of his Lord (and they said, “Do not fear and do not grieve. 29:34: 34 - Indeed, We will send down with emphasis and stress upon the people of this city a punishment a torment from the sky because of the action with which they were defiant that is, because of their defiantness.
29:35: 35 - And We have certainly left of it a clear sign apparent, which is the traces of its destruction for a people who understand who reflect.

29:37: 37 - But they denied him, so the earthquake seized them a severe earthquake and they became within their homes, kneeling kneeling on their knees, dead.
29:38: 38 - We destroyed ‘Aad and Thamud with the declension and leaving it with the meaning of the neighborhood and the tribe and it has become clear to you their destruction from their dwellings in Al-Hijr and Yemen and Satan made their deeds pleasing to them of disbelief and sins and averted them from the path the path of truth and they were discerning with insight
29:39: 39 - We destroyed Qarun, Pharaoh, and Haman, and Moses had already come to them before with clear proofs the clear arguments but they were arrogant in the land and were not forerunners in avoiding Our punishment.
29:40: 40 - So each of them of those mentioned (We seized for his sin. 29:41: 41 - The example of those who take protectors other than God idols from whom they hope to benefit is like the example of the spider who takes a home for herself to which she takes refuge. And indeed, the weakest of homes is the spider’s home it does not protect from heat or cold. Thus the idols do not benefit their worshippers. If they only knew that, they would not have worshipped them.
29:42: 42 - Indeed, God knows what meaning what they call upon they worship with the letter ya and the letter ta other than Him other than Him of anything, and He is the Exalted in Might in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
29:43: 43 - And these examples in the Qur’an We present We make them for the people, but none will grasp them understand them except those of knowledge who reflect.
29:44: 44 - God created the heavens and the earth in truth truly There is certainly in that a sign an indication of His Almighty power for the believers They were specifically mentioned because they are the ones who benefit from it in faith, unlike the disbelievers.
29:45: 45 - Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book the Qur’an (and establish prayer. 29:49: 49 - Rather, it meaning the Qur’an that I brought are clear signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge meaning the believers who memorize it and none reject Our signs except the wrongdoers meaning the Jews, and they rejected them after they were made clear to them.

29:51: 51 - Was it not sufficient for them in what they asked that We sent down to you the Book which is recited the Qur’an (to them. 29:55: 55 - On the Day the punishment will cover them from above them and from beneath their feet, and He will say in it with the letter nun We command to say, and with the letter ya the one in charge of the punishment will say Taste what you used to do meaning its reward, so do not miss out on us.

29:69: 69 - And those who strive for Us for Our sake We will surely guide them to Our ways the path to travel to Us and indeed, God is with the doers of good the believers in victory and support.
30:1: 1 - Alif, Lam, Meem God knows best what He intends in that.
30:2: 2 - The Romans were defeated and they are the People of the Book. The Persians defeated them and they are not People of the Book but they worship idols. So the infidels of Mecca rejoiced at that and said to the Muslims, We will defeat you as the Persians defeated the Romans.
30:3: 3 - in the lowest part of the land the closest land of the Romans to Persia in the island where the two armies met and the Bedouin invaded the Persians and they the Romans after their defeat the source was added to the object, meaning the Persians’ defeat of them will defeat Persia
30:4: 4 - In a few years is between three to nine or ten. Then the two armies met in the seventh year of the first encounter, and the Romans defeated the Persians. To God belongs the command before and after Before the Romans were defeated and after that, the meaning is that the Persians were defeated first and the Romans were defeated second by God’s command, that is, His will. And on that day the day the Romans are defeated the believers will rejoice.
30:5: 5 - With God’s victory for them over Persia, and they rejoiced over that and learned of it on the day of its occurrence, the day of Badr, with the descent of Gabriel about it in it, along with their rejoicing over their victory over the polytheists in it He gives victory to whom He wills, and He is the Exalted in Might the Victorious the Merciful to the believers.
30:6: 6 - God’s promise is a source of action instead of the word. The original is that God promised them victory. God does not fail in His promise to him. But most of the people the infidels of Mecca do not know His promise of their victory.
30:7: 7 - They know only the outward aspect of the life of this world its livelihoods of trade, agriculture, construction, planting, and other things and they are heedless of the Hereafter repetition of them is an emphasis.

Do they not) believe in the resurrection after death
30:9: 9 - Have they not traveled through the land and observed how was the end of those before them? And there came to them that is, the disbelievers of Mecca (their messengers with clear proofs. 30:11: 11 - God begins creation that is, He creates the creation of people then He repeats it He creates them after their death then to Him you will be returned with the letter ya and the letter ta
