30:17: 17 - Glory be to God meaning glorify God, meaning pray when you reach the evening you enter the evening, and in it are two prayers: the sunset and the evening and when you rise you enter the noon prayer in the morning, and in it is the dawn prayer
30:18: 18 - And to Him be praise in the heavens and the earth An objection, the meaning of which is that their people praise Him and in the evening An appositive of when and in it is the afternoon prayer and when you appear you enter the noonday and in it is the noon prayer
30:19: 19 - He brings forth the living from the dead like the human from the sperm and the bird from the egg and brings forth the dead the sperm and the egg from the living and revives the earth with plants after its death its drying up and thus the bringing forth you will come forth from the graves in the active and passive construction
30:20: 20 - And among His signs the Most High, which indicate His power is that He created you from dust meaning your origin is Adam then suddenly you are human beings of blood and flesh dispersing throughout the earth
30:21: 21 - And among His signs is that He created for you mates from among yourselves by creating Eve from Adam’s rib and all other women from the sperm of men and women that you may dwell in tranquility with them and be at peace with them and He placed between you all of you (affection and mercy. Indeed in that) what is mentioned are signs for a people who give thought to the creation of God Almighty.
30:22: 22 - And among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and the diversity of your languages your languages, such as Arabic, foreign, and others and your colors such as white, black, and others, and you are the children of one man and one woman Indeed in that are signs indications of His Almighty power for those of knowledge with the opening of the lam and its breaking, meaning those with intellects and those of knowledge.
30:23: 23 - And among His signs is your sleep by night and by day by His will, for comfort for you and your seeking by day of His bounty that is, your disposition to seek a livelihood by His will Indeed in that are signs for a people who listen listening with contemplation and consideration.
30:24: 24 - And among His signs is that He shows you that is, He shows you the lightning, causing fear for the traveler from thunderbolts and hope for the resident for rain and He sends down from the sky water and revives thereby the earth after its lifelessness that is, He dries it out by causing it to grow. Indeed in that what is mentioned are signs for a people who reason who reflect.
30:25: 25 - And among His signs is that the heaven and the earth stand by His command by His will without intention then when He calls you with a single call from the earth by Israfil blowing the trumpet for resurrection from the graves at once you will emerge from it alive. So your emergence from it by a call is among His signs, the Most High.
30:26: 26 - And to Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth as kings, creation, and servants all are obedient to Him obedient.
30:27: 27 - And it is He who begins creation for mankind then He will repeat it after their destruction and that is easier for Him than beginning it, in view of what those addressed believe, that repeating something is easier than beginning it, otherwise they are equal in ease before God Almighty and to Him belongs the highest description in the heavens and the earth meaning the highest attribute, which is that there is no god but God and He is the Exalted in Might in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.
30:28: 28 - He has given you O polytheists an example of yourselves, which is Do you have partners in what your right hands possess that is, of your slaves of yours, of money and other things that We have provided for you, so you are equal in it, fearing them) like yourselves from the free people, and the question is in the sense of negation, meaning your slaves are not your partners to the end with you, so how do you make some of God’s slaves partners with Him like your fear of yourselves? Thus We explain it in such detail We detail the verses so that they may ponder.
30:29: 29 - Rather, those who do wrong by associating partners with God (follow their own desires without knowledge. So who will guide he whom God has led astray?) That is, he has no guide. And they will have no helpers. To protect them from God’s punishment.
30:30: 30 - So direct O Muhammad your face toward the religion, inclining toward it, meaning, make your religion sincere for God, you and those who follow you. The nature of God His creation upon which He has created mankind and it is His religion, meaning, adhere to it. There is no change in the creation of God His religion, meaning, do not change it by associating partners with God. That is the right religion the straight one, the oneness of God, but most of the people the disbelievers of Mecca do not know the oneness of God.
30:31: 31 - repenting returning to Him the Most High in what He has commanded and forbidden. This is a state of the subject of establish and what is meant by it is, establish and fear Him fear Him and establish prayer and do not be among those who associate others with God
30:32: 32 - Of those substituted with the return of the preposition who divided their religion by their differences in what they worship and became sects sects in that each party of them rejoicing in what they have with them with them happy. And in the reading they departed, meaning they abandoned their religion which they were commanded to follow.
30:33: 33 - And when people are touched the infidels of Mecca and others by hardship severe they supplicate their Lord, turning back returning to Him and no one else then when He makes them taste mercy from Him by rain at once a party of them associate others with their Lord
30:34: 34 - To disbelieve in what We have given them This is intended as a threat So enjoy yourselves, for you are going to know the consequences of your enjoyment in it, a shift from the absence
30:35: 35 - Or meaning the hamza of denial We sent down upon them authority an argument and a book so it speaks speaks by implication about what they used to associate with it meaning it orders them to associate with God, not
30:36: 36 - And when We let the people taste the infidels of Mecca and others of mercy a blessing they rejoice therein a joy of arrogance but if evil befalls them hardship for what their hands have put forth, at once they despair they lose hope in mercy. It is the nature of the believer to be grateful when receiving a blessing and to hope in his Lord when facing hardship.
30:37: 37 - Have they not seen that they know that God expands provision for whom He expands it and restricts it as a test if He constricts it for whom He wills to test him (in that are signs for a people who believe, so bring)
30:38: 38 - Then give to the relative the kin his right of righteousness and kinship and to the needy and the traveler of charity and the nation of the Prophet follows him in that that is best for those who desire the face of God His reward for what they know and it is those who are the successful the winners.
30:39: 39 - And whatever you give in interest by giving something as a gift or present in order to ask for more from it, so it is called by the name of what is sought from the increase in the transaction so that it may increase within the wealth of the people the givers, that is, increase it does not increase it does not grow with God there is no reward in it for the givers and whatever you give in zakat charity intending thereby the countenance of God - it is those who will receive double their reward for what they wanted in it is a shift from the address
30:40: 40 - (God is He who created you, then provided for you, then will cause you to die, then will bring you to life. Is there any of your associates) from those you associated with God who does any of that? No. Glory be to Him, and He is exalted above what they associate with Him.
30:41: 41 - Corruption has appeared on land the deserts due to drought and scarcity of rain and the sea the lands on rivers due to scarcity of water because of what the hands of people have earned of sins that He may make them taste with the letter ya and the letter nun some of what they have done its punishment that they may return repent.
30:42: 42 - Say to the infidels of Mecca, (Travel through the land and observe what was the end of those before. Most of them were polytheists.) So they were destroyed because of their polytheism, and their dwellings and homes were empty.
30:43: 43 - So direct your face toward the right religion the religion of Islam before there comes a Day from God for which there is no repelling which is the Day of Resurrection On that Day they will be split apart in it the assimilation of the ta’ in the original into the sad and they will be dispersed after the reckoning to Paradise and Hell.
30:44: 44 - Whoever disbelieves, his disbelief will be upon him and the consequence of his disbelief, which is Hellfire and whoever does righteousness, it is for themselves that they prepare they prepare their home in Paradise
30:45: 45 - To reward related to they are disturbed (those who believe and do righteous deeds out of His bounty) He rewards them Indeed, He does not like the disbelievers He punishes them.
30:46: 46 - And among His signs the Most High is that He sends the winds as good tidings meaning to give you good tidings of rain and that He may let you taste with them of His mercy rain and fertility and that the ships may sail by them by His command by His will and that you may seek ask of His bounty provision by trading at sea and perhaps you will be grateful for these blessings, O people of Mecca, so declare Him One.
30:47: 47 - And We had certainly sent before you messengers to their people, and they came to them with clear proofs with clear arguments for their truthfulness in their message to them, but they denied them and We took retribution from those who committed crimes We destroyed those who denied them and it was incumbent upon Us to support the believers over the disbelievers by destroying them and saving the believers
30:48: 48 - God is He who sends the winds, and they stir up clouds that disturb Him and He spreads them out in the sky as He wills from little to much and makes them into fragments with the opening of the seen or its sukoon, scattered pieces and you see the rain the rain emerging from within it its middle and when He causes it to fall with the rain on whomever He wills of His servants at once they rejoice they rejoice at the rain
30:49: 49 - And they were before it was sent down to them confirmation in despair of its being sent down
30:50: 50 - So look at the traces and in reading the traces of God’s mercy His blessing with rain how He revives the earth after its death its dryness by producing vegetation Indeed, that is the One Who revives the dead, and He is over all things competent
30:51: 51 - And if the lam of an oath We send a wind harmful to plants and they see it yellow, they will continue to be they become the answer to the oath after it after it has turned yellow disbelieve they deny the blessing of rain.
30:52: 52 - For indeed, you cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs by realizing the two hamzas and facilitating the second between them and the ya’ and they turn their backs
30:53: 53 - (And you cannot guide the blind from their misguidance. You only hear those who believe in Our verses, the Qur’an, and they are Muslims, sincere in their belief in the Oneness of God.)
30:54: 54 - God is He who created you from weakness of insignificant water then made after weakness another, which is the weakness of childhood strength the strength of youth then made after strength weakness and gray hair the weakness of old age and the gray hair of senility and weakness in the three with the first letter pronounced with a damma and a fatha He creates what He wills from weakness and strength and youth and gray hair and He is the Knowing of the management of His creation the Able to do what He wills.
30:55: 55 - And on the Day the Hour comes, the criminals and disbelievers will swear swear that they did not remain in the graves except for an hour. God Almighty said: Thus were they lying. They were distracted from the truth of the Resurrection, just as they were distracted from the truth of the truth during the period of remaining.
30:56: 56 - And those who were given knowledge and faith from the angels and others said, You have remained in the Book of God in what He wrote in His prior knowledge (until the Day of Resurrection. So this is the Day of Resurrection) which you denied but you did not know of its occurrence.
30:57: 57 - On that Day, those who did wrong will not be helped by their excuse with the letter ya and the letter ta nor will they be asked to repent in their denial of him nor will they be asked to seek repentance they will not be asked to repent, that is, to return to what pleases God.
30:58: 58 - And We have certainly presented We made for the people in this Qur’an from every [kind of] example as a warning to them and if the lam of an oath you bring them O Muhammad a sign like the staff and the hand of Moses those who disbelieve will surely say the nun of the subject was deleted from it due to the succession of the nuns and the waw is a plural pronoun due to the meeting of two quiescent letters you are Muhammad and his companions except falsifiers people of falsehoods
30:59: 59 - Thus does God set a seal on the hearts of those who do not know monotheism, just as He set a seal on the hearts of these people.
30:60: 60 - (So be patient. Indeed, the promise of God) of your victory over them (is true. And do not let those who have no certainty) of the Resurrection make you impatient. Let them not make you frivolous and rash by abandoning patience. That is, do not abandon it.
31:1: 1 - Alif, Lam, Meem God knows best what He means by it.
31:2: 2 - Those are the verses verses of the Book the Qur’an of wisdom full of wisdom and the addition means from
31:3: 3 - It is guidance and mercy in the nominative case for the doers of good and in the general reading in the accusative case as a state of the verses in which the meaning of the indication is applied.
31:4: 4 - Those who establish prayer a statement for the doers of good and give zakat and they are certain of the Hereafter they are the second confirmation
31:5: 5 - Those are upon guidance from their Lord, and it is those who are the successful ones the winners
31:6: 6 - And among the people is he who buys the amusement of talk that is, what distracts from what he means to mislead with the opening of the ya’ and the closing of it from the way of God the way of Islam without knowledge and takes it in the accusative in apposition to mislead and in the nominative in apposition to buys in ridicule mocked by it those will have a humiliating punishment one who is humiliated.
31:7: 7 - And when Our verses are recited to him the Qur’an he turns away arrogantly haughtily as if he had not heard them, as if there was deafness in his ears deafness. The two similes are two states of the pronoun turn away or the second is an explanation of the first so give him tidings informing him of a painful punishment painful. And mentioning the tidings is a mockery of him. He is Al-Nadr ibn Al-Harith. He used to come to Al-Hirah to trade, so he would buy books of the history of the Persians and tell them to the people of Mecca, saying, Muhammad tells you stories of ‘Aad and Thamud, and I tell you stories of the Persians and the Romans. They would find his stories interesting and stop listening to the Qur’an.
31:8: 8 - Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds will have Gardens of Pleasure.
31:9: 9 - They will abide therein eternally a predicate noun, meaning that their eternity in it is predestined if they enter it. The promise of God is true meaning that God promised them that, and His right is true. And He is the Exalted in Might Whom nothing can overcome and prevent Him from fulfilling His promise and threat. The Wise Who does not place anything except in its proper place.
31:10: 10 - He created the heavens without pillars that you can see Pillars is the plural of Imad, which is a column, and it is true that there are no pillars at all and He cast into the earth firmly set mountains high mountains so that it does not shake) move with you and dispersed therein all kinds of creatures and sent down in this is a shift from the third person from the sky water and We caused to grow therein every noble kind a good kind
31:11: 11 - This is the creation of God His creation so show me tell me, O people of Mecca what those other than Him have created other than Him, that is, their gods, until you associated them with Him, the Most High. And what is an interrogative of denial, a subject, and that in the sense of that is its predicate, and show me is suspended from the action, and what follows it replaces the two objects. Rather for the transition the wrongdoers are in manifest error between their associating others with Him, and you are among them.
31:12: 12 - And We certainly gave Luqman wisdom including knowledge, religion, and correct speech. There is much wisdom that is well-known. He used to issue fatwas before the mission of David, but he lived to see his mission and took knowledge from him and stopped issuing fatwas. He said about that: Shall I not be satisfied if I am satisfied? And he was asked: Which of the people is evil? He said: The one who does not care if the people see him doing wrong? That and We said to him: Be grateful to God for what He has given you of wisdom. And whoever is grateful is grateful for himself because the reward of his gratitude is for him. And whoever is ungrateful for the blessing then indeed, God is free of need of His creation. Praiseworthy in His creation.
31:13: 13 - And remember and when Luqman said to his son while he was advising him, O my son diminutive of compassion (do not associate anything with God. Indeed, association) with God is a great injustice so he returned to him and submitted.
31:14: 14 - And We have enjoined upon man, concerning his parents We commanded him to be dutiful to them his mother carried him and weakened in weakness upon weakness she weakened from the pregnancy and weakened from the labor pains and weakened from the birth and his weaning that is, his weaning is in two years and We said to him (that you be grateful to Me and to your parents. To Me is the final destination) that is, the return.
31:15: 15 - And if they endeavor to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge in accordance with reality do not obey them but accompany them in this world with appropriate kindness with appropriate righteousness and kinship and follow the way of whoever turns back) returns to Me with obedience then to Me is your return, and I will inform you about what you used to do then I will reward you for it. The whole of the commandment and what follows it is an interruption.
31:16: 16 - O my son, indeed the bad trait if it be the weight of a mustard seed and be in a rock or in the heavens or in the earth that is, in a more hidden place than that God will bring it forth and He will hold you accountable for it Indeed, God is Kind in extracting it Aware of its location.
31:17: 17 - O my son, establish prayer, enjoin what is right, forbid what is wrong, and be patient over what befalls you because of the command and prohibition Indeed, that mentioned is among the matters of resolve its resolved matters that one resolves upon because of its obligation.
31:18: 18 - And do not turn your cheek away from people do not turn your face away from them in arrogance and do not walk on the earth exultantly in conceit for God does not like everyone who is arrogant haughty in his walk boasting towards people.
31:19: 19 - And be moderate in your pace Be moderate between crawling and hurrying, and be calm and dignified and lower your voice lower your voice, for the most disagreeable of sounds is the braying of donkeys, the beginning of which is a sigh and the end of which is an inhalation.
31:20: 20 - Have you not seen O you who are being addressed that God has subjected to you whatever is in the heavens of the sun, moon, and stars so that you may benefit from them and whatever is on the earth of fruits, rivers, and animals and has bestowed abundantly and completely upon you His favors, apparent which is the beauty of the form and the proportion of the limbs and other things and hidden which is knowledge and other things and among the people the people of Mecca is he who disputes about God without knowledge or guidance from a messenger or an enlightening Book revealed by God, but rather by imitation
31:21: 21 - And when it is said to them, Follow what God has revealed, they say, Rather, we will follow what we found our fathers doing. God Almighty said, Even if Satan were inviting them to the punishment of the Blaze meaning its causes, no.
31:22: 22 - And whoever submits his face to God accepts His obedience while he is a doer of good a monotheist has grasped the most trustworthy handhold the most trustworthy end that he does not fear breaking and to God is the outcome of all matters their return
31:23: 23 - And whoever disbelieves, let not his disbelief grieve you O Muhammad do not worry about his disbelief. (To Us is their return, and We will inform them of what they did. Indeed, God is Knowing of that within the breasts) what is in them, like others, so He will punish him.
31:24: 24 - We grant them enjoyment in this world for a little during the days of their lives then We will force them in the Hereafter to a severe punishment which is the punishment of the Fire from which they will find no escape.
31:25: 25 - And if the lam of an oath you ask them who created the heavens and the earth, they will surely say, God The nun of the subject was deleted from it due to the succession of similar letters and the waw of the pronoun due to the meeting of two quiescent letters say, Praise be to God on the clear proof against them of monotheism but most of them do not know its obligation upon them
31:26: 26 - To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth as dominion, creation, and servants. No one deserves to be worshipped in them except Him. Indeed, God is the One free of need of His creation. The Praiseworthy is the One praised for His creation.
31:27: 27 - And if all the trees on the earth were pens and the sea were ink in apposition to the name of that and He were to replenish it after it with seven more seas ink the words of God that express His knowledge by writing it with those pens with that ink, nor with more than that, because His knowledge is infinite Indeed, God is Exalted in Might nothing is beyond His power Wise nothing is beyond His knowledge and wisdom.
31:28: 28 - He did not create you and resurrect you except as a single soul creation and resurrection because with the word Be and it is Indeed, God is Hearing He hears everything that is heard Seeing He sees everything that is seen, nothing distracts Him from anything else.
31:29: 29 - Have you not seen O you who is being addressed that God merges He merges the night into the day and merges the day He merges it into the night so that each of them increases by what the other has decreased and He has subjected the sun and the moon, each of them running in its orbit for a specified term which is the Day of Resurrection and that God is Acquainted with what you do
31:30: 30 - That mentioned that God is the truth the established and that what they call upon with the letter ya and the letter ta they worship besides Him is falsehood the vanishing and that God is the Most High over His creation by force the Great the Mighty
31:31: 31 - Have you not seen that the ships the ships run through the sea by the grace of God that He may show you O you who are addressed by this of His signs? Indeed in that are signs an expression for everyone patient about the disobedience of God grateful for His blessings.
31:32: 32 - And when a wave covers them that is, the disbelievers rise like canopies like mountains that provide shade from beneath them they supplicate God, devoting their religion to Him sincerely supplicating to save them, that is, they do not supplicate to anyone other than Him but when He delivers them to the land, some of them are moderate intermediate between disbelief and faith, and some of them remain in their disbelief and none rejects Our signs including salvation from the wave except every traitor treacherous ungrateful for the blessings of God the Most High.
31:33: 33 - O mankind the people of Mecca (fear your Lord and fear a Day when no father will avail his son at all, nor will a son avail his father at all. Indeed, the promise of God is true) of resurrection so let not the worldly life deceive you about Islam and let not the Deceiver deceive you about God in His forbearance and respite the Deceiver Satan.
31:34: 34 - Indeed, God has knowledge of the Hour when it will come and He sends down with emphasis and emphasis the rain at a time that He knows and He knows what is in the wombs male or female, and no one knows one of the three except God the Most High and no soul perceives what it will earn tomorrow of good or evil, and God the Most High knows it and no soul perceives in what land it will die and God the Most High knows it indeed, God is Knowing of everything Acquainted with its inner as its outer. Al-Bukhari narrated on the authority of Ibn Umar the hadith of the keys of the unseen, five: Indeed, God has knowledge of the Hour until the end of the surah.
32:1: 1 - Alif, Lam, Meem God knows best what He means by it.
32:2: 2 - The revelation of the Book The Qur’an is the subject there is no doubt doubt about it is the first predicate from the Lord of the worlds is the second predicate
32:3: 3 - Or rather they say, He invented it Muhammad. No. Rather, it is the truth from your Lord that you may warn with it a people to whom no warner came before you, that they may be guided by your warning.
32:4: 4 - God is He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days the first of which is Sunday and the last of which is Friday then He established Himself on the Throne which is in the language the bed of the king, an establishment befitting Him you have O disbelievers of Mecca besides Him other than Him no protector or intercessor the name of what is added to it, meaning a supporter nor intercessor to ward off His punishment from you so will you not remember this and believe
32:5: 5 - He directs the affair from the heaven to the earth for the duration of this world then it ascends the affair and the administration return to Him in a Day the extent of which is a thousand years of those which you count in this world and in Surat He asked fifty thousand years and it is the Day of Resurrection due to the severity of its terrors for the disbeliever. As for the believer, it will be easier for him than an obligatory prayer that he prays in this world as came in the hadith.
32:6: 6 - That is the Creator, the Manager Knower of the unseen and the witnessed that is, what is hidden from creation and what is present the Exalted in Might the Invincible in His kingdom the Merciful to those who obey Him.
32:7: 7 - He who perfected everything which He created with the opening of the lam as a past tense verb as an adjective and with the sukoon as a substitute for inclusion and He began the creation of man Adam from clay
32:8: 8 - Then He made his offspring his offspring from the lineage of a clinging clot from despised water weak, which is the sperm.
32:9: 9 - Then He proportioned him created Adam and breathed into him of His spirit made him alive and sensitive after he had been inanimate and made for you for his descendants hearing and sight and hearts the hearts little do you give thanks ma is an extra word that emphasizes the little.
32:10: 10 - And they said denying the resurrection If we are lost in the earth disappearing in it by becoming dust mixed with its dust Shall we then be in a new creation a question of denial by realizing the two hamzas and facilitating the second and inserting an alif between them in both ways in the two places. God Almighty said Rather, they are, in the meeting with their Lord in the resurrection disbelievers
32:11: 11 - Say to them The Angel of Death who is assigned to you will take your souls that is, he will take your souls then to your Lord you will be returned alive, and He will reward you for your deeds.

But that will not benefit them, nor will they return. The answer to if you had seen is a terrible matter.
32:13: 13 - And if We willed, We could have given every soul its guidance so that it would be guided by faith and obedience by its own choice but the word from Me will come into effect which is I will surely fill Hell with jinn jinn and people all together and the keepers will say to them when they enter it
32:14: 14 - So taste the punishment for having forgotten the meeting of this Day of yours by abandoning belief in it Indeed, We have forgotten you We have left you in the punishment and taste the punishment of eternity for what you used to do) of disbelief and denial.
32:15: 15 - Only those believe in Our verses the Qur’an who, when they are reminded are admonished by them, fall down in prostration and exalt with praise of their Lord) they say, Glory be to God and His praise and they are not arrogant about faith and obedience
32:16: 16 - Their sides forsake their beds they raise their beds the places where they lie down with their bedding for their night prayers to supplicate their Lord in fear of His punishment and in hope of His mercy and from what We have provided them they spend they give charity
32:17: 17 - No soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for their eyes what will comfort their eyes. In the reading with a silent ya’, it is a present tense verb as reward for what they used to do.
32:18: 18 - Is he who is a believer like he who is a sinner? They are not equal. That is, the believers and the sinners.
32:19: 19 - As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, for them are the Gardens of Refuge as a lodging is what is prepared for the guest for what they used to do
32:20: 20 - And as for those who disbelieved by disbelief and denial (their refuge is the Fire. Every time they wish to emerge from it, they will be returned to it and it will be said to them, Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny.)
32:21: 21 - And We will make them taste of the lesser punishment the punishment of this world through killing, captivity, years of drought, and diseases before before the greater punishment the punishment of the Hereafter that perhaps they that is, those of them who remain will return to the faith.
32:22: 22 - And who is more unjust than he who is reminded of the verses of his Lord the Qur’an and then turns away from them No one is more unjust than him Indeed, We will take retribution from the criminals the polytheists)
32:23: 23 - And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture the Torah so be not in doubt about meeting him) and We met on the Night of the Isra and We made it Moses or the Scripture a guide a guide for the Children of Israel
32:24: 24 - And We made from among them leaders by realizing the two hamzas and replacing the second with a yaa of leaders guiding the people by Our command when they were patient in their religion and in the affliction of their enemy. And in a reading with a kasra on the lam and a light mim and they were certain of Our signs indicating Our power and Our oneness.
32:25: 25 - Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ of the matter of religion.
32:26: 26 - Have they not seen how many generations We destroyed before them? That is, it has become clear to the disbelievers of Mecca that We destroyed many generations walking nations with their disbelief in a state of the pronoun for them (their dwellings. Indeed) in their travels to Syria and elsewhere, so they consider (in that are signs. Do they not then) evidence of Our power hear? Have they not a hearing of contemplation and admonition?
32:27: 27 - Have they not seen that We drive the water to the barren land and bring forth therein crops from which their livestock and themselves eat? Then will they not see and say? This, and know that We are able to bring them back?
32:28: 28 - And they say to the believers When will this conquest come between us and you if you should be truthful?
32:29: 29 - Say, on the Day of Conquest by bringing down the punishment upon them those who disbelieved will not benefit from their faith, nor will they be reprieved to repent or excuse themselves.
32:30: 30 - So turn away from them and wait for the punishment to be brought down upon them for they are waiting for you to cause death or to be killed so that they may find rest from you. This was before the command to fight them.
33:1: 1 - O Prophet, fear God persist in fearing Him and do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites in what contradicts your law Indeed, God is ever Knowing of what will be before it comes into being Wise in what He creates.
33:2: 2 - And follow what is revealed to you from your Lord meaning the Qur’an Indeed, God is ever, with what you do, Acquainted and in a reading with the upper letter
33:3: 3 - And put your trust in God in your affair and sufficient is God as Disposer of affairs to protect you and your nation follows Him in all of that.
33:4: 4 - And God has not made for any man two hearts within him in response to those of the infidels who said that he has two hearts, each of which has a better mind than Muhammad’s mind And He has not made your wives, whom you with a hamza and a ya’ and without a ya’ of them without an alif before the ha’ and with it and the second ta’ in the original is assimilated into the dha’ your mothers that is, like mothers in forbidding her by that which was considered in the pre-Islamic era as divorce, and expiation is only required for it with its condition as mentioned in Surat Al-Mujadilah And He has not made your adopted sons the plural of da’i’, which is someone who is claimed as a son by someone other than his father your sons in reality that is your statement from your mouths that is, the Jews and the hypocrites said when the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, married Zaynab bint Jahsh, who was the wife of Zayd ibn Haritha, whom the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, adopted, they said that Muhammad married his son’s wife, so God the Most High belied them in that and God speaks the truth in that and He guides to the way the way of truth
33:5: 5 - But call them by their fathers' names; that is more just more equitable (in the sight of God. But if you do not know their fathers, then they are your brothers in religion and your allies) your paternal cousins and there is no blame upon you for that in which you erred in that but in what your hearts intended in it is after the prohibition and God is Forgiving of what you said before the prohibition and Merciful to you in that
33:6: 6 - The Prophet is closer to the believers than they are to themselves in what he called them to and their selves called them to do otherwise and his wives are their mothers in the sanctity of marrying them and those of kinship those of kinship are closer to one another in inheritance in the Book of God than the believers and the emigrants that is, from the inheritance by faith and emigration which was at the beginning of Islam and was abrogated except but that you do to your relatives a kindness by a will, so it is permissible that was the abrogation of the inheritance by faith and emigration by the inheritance of those of kin inscribed in the Book and what is meant by the Book in both places is the Preserved Tablet
33:7: 7 - And remember and when We took from the prophets their covenant when they were brought forth from the loins of Adam like an atom, the plural of an atom, which is smaller than an ant and from you and from Noah and Abraham and Moses and Jesus, the son of Mary that they would worship God and call to His worship, and he mentioned the five from the connection of the specific to the general and We took from them a firm covenant strong to fulfill what they were charged with, which is the oath by God Almighty, then He took the covenant
33:8: 8 - That God may ask (the truthful about their truthfulness) in conveying the message, as a rebuke to those who disbelieve in them. And He has prepared the Most High for the disbelievers in them a painful punishment painful, which is an apposition to Our taking
33:9: 9 - O you who have believed, remember the favor of God upon you when there came to you armies of the disbelievers, grouped together during the days of digging the trench and We sent upon them a wind and armies you did not see of the angels and God is ever, of what you do with the ta’ of digging the trench and with the ya’ of grouping the polytheists Seeing
33:10: 10 - When they came upon you from above you and from below you from the top of the valley and its bottom from the east and the west and when the eyes turned away turned away from everything towards their enemy from every side and the hearts reached the throats the plural of hanjarah is the end of the throat from the intensity of fear and you thought about God various thoughts of victory and despair
33:11: 11 - There the believers were tested they were tested to distinguish the sincere from the wicked and they were shaken they were moved with a severe earthquake from the intensity of the fear
33:12: 12 - And remember when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is disease say weak belief God and His Messenger have promised us nothing but delusion falsely.
33:13: 13 - And when a party of them said meaning the hypocrites O people of Yathrib it is the land of Medina and it is not inflected for the action and the weight of the verb there is no place for you with the damma or fatha on the meem, no residence or place so return to your homes from Medina and they had gone out with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to Sal’ mountain outside Medina to fight and a party of them asked permission of the Prophet to return saying, Our houses are vulnerable not fortified and feared for. God Almighty said (and they are not vulnerable. They only want to flee) from the fighting.
33:14: 14 - And if the city had entered upon them from its regions its districts and they were asked those who entered asked them the trial of polytheism they would have given it with extension and shortening, they would have given it and done it and they would not have remained in it except a little
33:15: 15 - And they had already made a covenant with God before that they would not turn their backs, and the covenant of God will be questioned about fulfilling it.
33:16: 16 - (Say: Flight will not benefit you if you flee from death or killing. And if you flee, you will not enjoy [yourselves] except for a little while.)
33:17: 17 - (Say: Who is it that can protect you from God if He intends evil for you, destruction and defeat, or afflicts you with harm if God intends good for you? And they will not find for themselves besides God any protector or helper who can benefit them.)
33:18: 18 - God knows those among you who hold back who discourage and those who say to their brothers, Come to us, but they do not come to the battle (except a little for show and to be heard.
33:19: 19 - They are stingy with you with the help of the plural of stingy, which is the state of the pronoun they come so when fear comes, you will see them looking at you, their eyes rolling like one like the look or like the rolling of one who faints from death that is, its throes so when the fear has gone and the spoils are obtained they will smite you they will hurt you or strike you with sharp tongues, stingy with good that is, the spoils they seek those who have not believed in reality so God has rendered worthless their deeds, and that the nullification is easy for God by His will
33:20: 20 - They think that the parties of the disbelievers did not go to Mecca because of their fear of them and if the parties come another time they would wish they wish that they were in the desert among the bedouins living in the desert asking about your report your report with the disbelievers and if they had been among you this time they would not have fought except a little hypocrisy and fear of expression
33:21: 21 - Indeed, in the Messenger of God you have an excellent example to follow with a kasra or damma on the hamza a good example following his example in fighting and steadfastness in his situations for whoever in place of for you has hope in God fears Him and the Last Day and remembers God often unlike whoever is not like that
33:22: 22 - And when the believers saw the parties of the disbelievers they said, This is what God and His Messenger promised us of trial and victory (and God and His Messenger spoke the truth in their promise and it only increased them in faith) in belief in God’s promise and submission to His command
33:23: 23 - Among the believers are men true to what they pledged to God of steadfastness with the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and among them is he who has fulfilled his vow died or was killed in the way of God and among them is he who awaits that and they have not changed at all in the pledge and they are in the state of the hypocrites.
33:24: 24 - That God may reward the truthful for their truthfulness and punish the hypocrites if He wills by causing them to die in their hypocrisy (or accept their repentance. Indeed, God is Forgiving) to those who repent and Merciful to them.
33:25: 25 - And God repelled those who disbelieved the Confederates in their fury, they did not obtain any good their desire of victory over the believers and God spared the believers the fighting with the wind and the angels and God is Powerful in bringing about what He wants Mighty dominant in His affair
33:26: 26 - And He sent down those who supported them from among the People of the Scripture that is, Quwayzah from their fortresses their castles, the plural of saisayyah, which is what one fortifies oneself with and cast terror into their hearts fear some of whom you killed and they are the fighters and you took captive a group of them that is, the children.
33:27: 27 - And He caused you to inherit their land, their houses, their wealth, and a land which you had not yet trodden which was Khaybar, which was taken after the Qurayza and God is over all things competent
33:28: 28 - O Prophet, say to your wives and they were nine and they asked him for worldly adornments that he did not have If you desire the life of this world and its adornments, then come, I will provide for you meaning the enjoyment of divorce and release you with gracious release I will divorce you without causing harm.
33:29: 29 - And if you desire God and His Messenger and the home of the Hereafter Paradise then indeed, God has prepared for the good-doers among you by desiring the Hereafter a great reward Paradise, so choose the Hereafter over this world.
33:30: 30 - O wives of the Prophet! Whoever among you commits a clear immorality with the opening of the yaa’ and its breaking, meaning it is clear or it is clear it will be doubled. In another reading, it is doubled with emphasis, and in another, it is doubled with the nun with it and the punishment is accusative for her is a double punishment double the punishment of others, meaning twice as much and that is easy for God.
33:31: 31 - And whoever among you is obedient obeys to God and His Messenger and does righteousness - We will give her her reward twice over twice the reward of other women. And in a reading with the lower letter in works and we will give her and We have prepared for her a noble provision in Paradise, an increase.
33:32: 32 - O wives of the Prophet, you are not like anyone like a group (of women. If you fear) God, then you are greater and do not be soft in speech to men lest he in whose heart is a disease hypocrisy and speak in an appropriate manner without submission.
33:33: 33 - And stay with a kasra or a fatha on the kaf in your houses from qarār, and its origin is iqrārān with a kasra or a fatha on the ra’ from qarārāt with a fatha on the ra’ and a kasra, the vowel of the ra’ was transferred to the qaf and was deleted with the hamzat al-wasl and do not display yourselves by leaving out one of the two ta’s from its origin the display of the first Jahiliyyah meaning before Islam, when women would show their beauty to men, and the display after Islam is mentioned in the verse and do not display their adornment except what is apparent thereof (and establish prayer and give zakāh and obey God and His Messenger. God only desires to remove from you the uncleanness) sin, O People of the House the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace and purify you from it a thorough purification
33:34: 34 - And remember what is recited in your houses of the verses of God the Qur’an and wisdom the Sunnah for God is ever Kind to His friends and Aware of all His creation.
33:35: 35 - (Indeed, the Muslim men and Muslim women, the believing men and believing women, the obedient men and obedient women, the truthful men and truthful women, the patient men and patient women, the humble men and humble women, the charitable men and charitable women, the fasting men and fasting women, the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard from what is forbidden, and the men who remember God often and the women who remember - for them God has prepared forgiveness and a great reward for their acts of obedience.
33:36: 36 - And it is not for a believing man or a believing woman, when God and His Messenger have decided a matter, that they should have any option with the letter ta’ and the letter ya’ in their decision against the command of God and His Messenger. It was revealed about Abdullah ibn Jahsh and his sister Zaynab. The Prophet proposed to her for Zayd ibn Haritha. They were dissatisfied with that when they learned, because they thought before that the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, had proposed to her for himself. Then they agreed to the verse And whoever disobeys God and His Messenger has certainly strayed into clear error. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, married her to Zayd. Then his sight fell on her after a while, and love for her fell into his heart, while hatred for her fell into Zayd’s heart. Then he said to the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, I want to divorce her. He said, Keep your wife for yourself, as God the Most High said.
33:37: 37 - And when in the accusative case of mention you said to him whom God had bestowed favor upon with Islam and whom you had bestowed favor upon with emancipation, and he was Zayd ibn Haritha, who was among the captives of the Jahiliyyah. The Messenger of God, may God bless him and grant him peace, bought him before the mission, emancipated him, and adopted him Keep your wife and fear God in the matter of divorcing her and you conceal within yourself what God is about to reveal His manifestation of his love for her, and that if Zayd had left her, she would have married her and you feared the people that they would say he married his son’s wife and God has more right that you should fear Him in everything, and he married her and you do not have to worry about what the people say, then Zayd divorced her and her waiting period expired. God the Most High said So when Zayd had no longer any need for her a need We married her to you so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, went in to her without permission and fed the Muslims bread and meat so that there will be no blame upon the believers concerning the wives of their adopted sons when they have no longer any need for them, and it is a matter decided by God
33:38: 38 - (There is no blame upon the Prophet for what God has enjoined upon him. It is the way of God.) That is, like the way of God, so the subject is removed by removing the preposition. With regard to those who passed on before of the prophets, that there is no blame upon them for that, giving them flexibility in marriage. And the command of God His action is a decree decreed. Decided.
33:39: 39 - Those is an adjective for those before him who convey the messages of God and fear Him and do not fear anyone except God so they do not fear what people will say about what God has made lawful for them and sufficient is God as Accountant as Guardian of the deeds of His creation and their accountability
33:40: 40 - Muhammad is not the father of any of your men so he is not the father of Zayd, i.e. his father, so it is not forbidden for him to marry his wife Zaynab but he was the Messenger of God and the Seal of the Prophets so he would not have a male son after him who would be a prophet. And in a reading with the opening of the ta’ like the sealing tool, i.e. they sealed with him and God is Knowing of all things including that there is no prophet after him, and if the Master Jesus descends he will rule according to his law.
33:41: 41 - O you who have believed, remember God with much remembrance
33:42: 42 - And glorify Him in the morning and the evening at the beginning and end of the day.
33:43: 43 - It is He who sends blessings upon you has mercy upon you and His angels ask forgiveness for you that He may bring you out to perpetuate His bringing you out from darkness disbelief into the light faith and He is ever Merciful to the believers
33:44: 44 - Their greeting from Him, the Most High, (on the Day they meet Him will be, “Peace”) in the tongue of the angels, and He has prepared for them a noble reward which is Paradise.
33:45: 45 - O Prophet, indeed We have sent you as a witness over whom you were sent and a bringer of good tidings to those who believe you of Paradise and a warner to those who deny you of the Fire.
33:46: 46 - And an inviter to God to His obedience by His permission by His command and an illuminating lamp that is, one like Him in being guided by it
33:47: 47 - And give good tidings to the believers that they will have from God great bounty is Hannah
33:48: 48 - And do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites in what contradicts your law and leave leave their harm do not punish them for it until you are commanded to do something about them and put your trust in God for He is sufficient for you and sufficient is God as Disposer of affairs entrusted to Him.
33:49: 49 - O you who have believed, when you marry believing women and then divorce them before you have touched them and in another reading: touch them meaning have intercourse with them then you have no waiting period to count for them you count it by menstrual periods or other things then provide for them give them what they can enjoy, that is, if no dowries were specified for them, otherwise they are only entitled to half of what was specified. Ibn Abbas said, and Al-Shafi’i agreed with him and release them with gracious release set them free without causing harm.
33:50: 50 - O Prophet, indeed We have made lawful to you your wives to whom you have given their dowries their bridal-money and those your right hand possesses from what God has given you from the infidels through captivity, such as Safiyyah and Juwayriyyah and the daughters of your paternal uncles and the daughters of your paternal aunts and the daughters of your maternal uncles and the daughters of your maternal aunts who emigrated with you unlike those who did not emigrate and a believing woman if she gives herself to the Prophet if the Prophet wishes to marry her he requests to marry her without a dowry exclusively for you, not for the believers marriage in the form of a gift without a dowry We have already known what We have enjoined upon them meaning the believers regarding their wives of rulings that they not exceed four women and that they not marry except with a guardian and witnesses and a dowry and and those their right hands possess of female slaves by purchase or otherwise, such that the female slave is one of those who are permissible for her owner, such as a woman of the Book, unlike a Zoroastrian or a pagan, and that she be purified before intercourse so that related to what came before that (it will not be There is no difficulty for you in marriage, but God is Forgiving and Merciful in what is difficult to avoid.
33:51: 51 - You may postpone with the hamza and the ya’ as a substitute whoever you wish of them that is, your wives from her turn and take you include to yourself whomever you wish of them so that you come to her and whoever you desire you seek of those whom you have set aside from the division there is no blame upon you in seeking her and including her with you. It is better in that after the division was obligatory upon him that the choice is nearer closer to that their eyes may be comforted and they may not grieve and be satisfied with what you have given them what the one who has the choice mentioned in it all of them emphasis for the agent in they are satisfied and God knows what is in your hearts regarding the matter of women and the inclination towards some of them. We only gave you the choice regarding them to make it easy for you in everything you wanted and God is Knowing of His creation Forbearing about punishing them
33:52: 52 - It is not lawful for you with the letter ta’ and the letter ya’ to take other women after them after the nine that I have chosen for you nor for you to exchange by leaving out one of the two originally for them other wives by divorcing them or some of them and marrying another in place of the one you divorced even though their beauty pleases you, except those your right hand possesses of female slaves, then it is lawful for you. And after them, the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, took Mariyah as his wife, and she bore him Ibrahim, and he died during his lifetime and God is ever, over all things, a Watcher Preserver.
33:53: 53 - O you who have believed, do not enter the houses of the Prophet except when permission is given to you to enter by invitation for food then enter not waiting waiting for its preparation but when you are invited, then enter. And when you have eaten, disperse and do not stay indulgent in conversation of one another. Indeed, that) staying used to annoy the Prophet, so he would be shy of you to expel you. And God is not shy of the truth to expel you, that is, He does not leave out its statement. It was read as shy with one ya. And when you ask them meaning the wives of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, for something, ask them from behind a partition a screen. That is purer for your hearts and their hearts from suspicious thoughts. And it is not for you to annoy the Messenger of God with anything (or to marry his wives after him, ever. Indeed, that would be in the sight of God) a sin tremendous.)
33:54: 54 - If you disclose anything or conceal it of their marriage after him then indeed, God is ever, of all things, Knowing and He will reward you for it.
33:55: 55 - There is no blame upon them concerning their fathers, or their sons, or their brothers, or their brothers’ sons, or their sisters’ sons, or their women meaning the believing women or those their right hands possess of female slaves and male slaves, that they see them and speak to them without a veil and fear God in what you have been commanded for God is ever, over all things, a Witness nothing is hidden from Him.
33:56: 56 - Indeed, God and His angels send blessings on the Prophet Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace. O you who have believed, send blessings on him and salute him with a worthy salutation. That is, say, “O God, send blessings on Muhammad and grant him peace.”
33:57: 57 - Indeed, those who abuse God and His Messenger who are the disbelievers, they describe God with what He is far removed from, such as having a son or a partner, and they deny His Messenger God has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter He has distanced them and has prepared for them a humiliating punishment of humiliation, which is the Fire.
33:58: 58 - And those who harm believing men and believing women for [something] other than what they have earned accuse them of something other than what they have done have certainly borne upon themselves a slander they have borne a lie and a manifest sin evident.
33:59: 59 - O Prophet, tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the believers to bring down over themselves [part] of their outer garments The plural of jilbab is the sheet that a woman covers herself with, that is, to let some of it hang down over her face when she goes out for her needs, except for one eye that is more suitable closer that they will be known that they are free women and not be bothered by being exposed to them, unlike the slave women, who do not cover their faces, so the hypocrites used to expose themselves to them and ever is God Forgiving for what they did in the past by not covering and Merciful to them, since He covered them
33:60: 60 - If the lam of an oath the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a disease and those who spread rumors in the city the believers, saying, The enemy has come to you and your troops have been killed or defeated, We will surely incite you against them to give you power over them and then they will not be your neighbors live with you therein except for a little while
33:61: 61 - Then they will go out cursed and banished from mercy wherever they are found, they will be seized and killed a severe slaughter) meaning this ruling regarding them is according to the command to do so.
33:62: 62 - The way of God meaning that is the way of God with those who passed on before of the past nations with their hypocrites who spread rumors among the believers and you will not find in the way of God any change therein.
33:63: 63 - The people ask you the people of Mecca about the Hour when it will be Say, Its knowledge is only with God, and what makes you know? He knows it to you, meaning you do not know it Perhaps the Hour will occur soon.
33:64: 64 - Indeed, God has cursed the disbelievers He has distanced them and has prepared for them a Blaze a severe fire which they will enter.
33:65: 65 - (They will abide therein forever. They will not find any protector or helper to protect them from it.)
33:66: 66 - On the Day their faces will be turned over in the Fire, they will say, Oh, would that we had obeyed God and obeyed the Messenger
33:67: 67 - And they said meaning the followers among them Our Lord, indeed we obeyed our masters and in the reading of our masters, it is the plural of the plural and our dignitaries, and they led us astray from the path the path of guidance.
33:68: 68 - Our Lord, give them double the punishment double our punishment and curse them punish them with a great curse its number and in a reading with the unified letter meaning great
33:69: 69 - O you who have believed, do not be with your Prophet like those who harmed Moses by saying, for example, that he did not wash except that he had urinated. But God cleared him of what they said by placing his garment on a stone to wash, but the stone flew away with him until he stood among a group of the Children of Israel. Moses caught up with him, took his garment, and covered himself with it, so they saw him, and he did not have urinary incontinence, which is a swelling in the testicle. And he was in the sight of God of prestige. Among the things with which our Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, was harmed was that he divided a portion, and a man said, This is a division not intended for the sake of God Almighty. So the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, became angry at that and said, “May God have mercy on Moses. He was harmed with more than this,” but he was patient. Narrated by Al-Bukhari.
33:70: 70 - O you who have believed, fear God and speak words of appropriate justice correct
33:71: 71 - He will rectify your deeds for you He will accept them (and forgive you your sins. And whoever obeys God and His Messenger has certainly achieved a great victory) He has attained a desired goal.
33:72: 72 - Indeed, We offered the Trust the prayers and other things that bring reward for doing them and punishment for leaving them to the heavens and the earth and the mountains by creating in them understanding and speech and they declined to undertake it and feared it they were afraid of it. But man undertook it) Adam after it was offered to him he was indeed unjust to himself in what he undertook and ignorant of it.
33:73: 73 - That God may punish the lam is related to our offering, which resulted in Adam’s pregnancy the hypocrites, men and women, and the polytheists, men and women who neglected the trust and God will accept the repentance of the believing men and women who fulfilled the trust and God is Forgiving to the believers and Merciful to them.
34:1: 1 - Praise be to God God Almighty praised Himself for that, and what is meant by it is praise with its content of affirming praise, which is the description of God Almighty with beauty to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth in possession and creation and to Him is praise in the Hereafter like this world, His friends praise Him when they enter Paradise and He is the Wise in His actions the Acquainted with His creation.
34:2: 2 - He knows what enters into the earth, such as water and other things, and what comes out of it, such as plants and other things, and what descends from the sky of provisions and other things, and what ascends therein, of deeds and other things. And He is the Most Merciful, the Most Forgiving to His friends.
34:3: 3 - And those who disbelieve say, The Hour will not come to us Resurrection Say to them, Yes, by my Lord, the Knower of the Unseen will surely come to you) in the accusative case as an adjective and the nominative case as a predicate of the subject and ‘Alam in the accusative case not an atom’s weight the smallest ant’s weight in the heavens or on the earth or anything smaller than that or larger but that it is in a clear book clear, it is the Preserved Tablet.
34:4: 4 - To reward therein (those who believe and do righteous deeds. For them is forgiveness and noble provision) good in Paradise.
34:5: 5 - And those who strive to invalidate Our verses the Qur’an failing and in a reading here and in what follows failing meaning they estimate our failure or race us so that they miss us because they think that there is no resurrection or punishment those will have a punishment of a painful punishment a bad punishment painful painful in the accusative and nominative as an attribute of a punishment or torment
34:6: 6 - And those who have been given knowledge see know that which has been revealed to you from your Lord that is, the Qur’an is a clear truth and guides to the path of the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy that is, God, the Possessor of Might, the Praiseworthy.
34:7: 7 - And those who disbelieved said meaning some of them said to each other in amazement Shall we direct you to a man who is Muhammad who will inform you tell you that when you are torn apart cut up each piece of it meaning torn apart you will indeed be in a new creation
34:8: 8 - Has he invented with the opening of the hamza for interrogation and it is sufficient for the hamza of connection against God a lie in that or is there madness in him madness that he imagined that in him. God Almighty said Rather, those who do not believe in the Hereafter which includes resurrection and punishment are in the punishment in it and far astray from the truth in this world.
34:9: 9 - Have they not seen looking at what is before them and what is behind them what is above them and what is below them of the heaven and the earth? If We will, We can cause the earth to swallow them up or cause a fragment of the heaven to fall upon them with a silent or open sin of the heaven and in a reading in the three verbs with a ya’ Indeed in that what is seen is a sign for every servant who turns back referring to his Lord, indicating God’s ability to resurrect and whatever He wills.
34:10: 10 - And We certainly gave David favor from Us prophecy and a Book, and We said O mountains, echo return with him in glorification and the birds in the accusative case in apposition to the location of the mountains, meaning We called them to glorify with him and We made iron soft for him so it was in his hand like dough.
34:11: 11 - And We said, Make from it full coats of mail that the wearer will drag on the ground, and measure out the links. That is, the weaving of the mail. The one who makes it is called sirrad, meaning, make it so that its links fit together. And work, that is, the family of David with him, “righteously. Indeed, I, of what you do, am Seeing.” So I will reward you for it.
34:12: 12 - We subjected to Solomon the wind and the reading of the nominative case is with the estimation of subjecting its morning its journey from morning meaning morning until noon a month and its evening its journey from noon until sunset a month meaning its journey and We made flow We melted for him a spring of molten copper so it flowed for three days and nights like the flow of water and the work of people until today from what was given to Solomon and there were jinn who worked before him by permission by the command of his Lord and whoever deviates turns away from Our command to him by obeying him We will make him taste of the punishment of the Blaze the Fire in the Hereafter and it was said in this world that an angel will strike him with a whip from it with a blow that will burn him
34:13: 13 - They make for him whatever he wishes of sanctuaries tall buildings that are ascended by stairs and statues the plural of statue, which is anything that you represent with something, i.e. pictures of copper, glass, and marble. Making pictures is not forbidden in its haste and basins the plural of jafanah like a basin the plural of jabiyyah, which is a large basin where a thousand men gather around the basin and eat from it and fixed pots fixed with legs that do not move from their places, made from the mountains in Yemen, to which one can ascend by stairs. And We said, Work O the family of David in obedience to God in gratitude to Him for what He has given you and few of My servants are grateful the one who works in obedience to Me in gratitude for My blessings.
34:14: 14 - So when We decreed death for him for Solomon, that is, he died and remained standing on his staff for a year dead, and the jinn were doing those difficult tasks as usual, not realizing his death until the termites ate his staff and he fell dead. Nothing informed them of his death except a creature of the earth the source of the word arrayta is the wooden object in the passive form, the termites ate it eating his staff with the hamza and leaving it with the alif of his staff because it is yansa’a which means to drive away and deter with it. So when he fell down dead, the jinn realized it was revealed to them that had they known the unseen including what was hidden from them about Solomon’s death they would not have remained in the humiliating punishment the difficult work for them because they thought he was alive contrary to their thought. Knowledge of the unseen and knowledge of it being a year is calculated by counting what the termites ate of the staff after his death, for example, a day and a night.
34:15: 15 - There was for Sheba with or without morphology, a tribe named after an ancestor of theirs from the Arabs in their dwelling place in Yemen a sign indicating the power of God Almighty two gardens instead of on the right and on the left on the right and left of their valley, and it was said to them Eat from the provision of your Lord and be grateful to Him for the blessings He provided you in the land of Sheba a good land in which there is no salt marsh, nor a mosquito, nor a fly, nor a flea, nor a scorpion, nor a snake, and a stranger passes by it with lice on his clothes and dies due to the goodness of its air and a Forgiving Lord
34:16: 16 - So they turned away from thanking Him and disbelieved so We sent upon them the flood of the dam the plural of dam is what holds water from a building or other things until the time of its need, meaning the flood of their valley held back as mentioned, so it drowned their gardens and their wealth and We replaced their two gardens with two gardens of their own dual of their own, singular according to the original of bitter food passed by adding dam with the meaning of something eaten and leaving it and in conjunction with it and tamarisk and some of the lote trees, few
34:17: 17 - That the change We recompensed them for their disbelief for their disbelief And do We recompense except the disbeliever with the ya and the noon with the kasra of the zay and the accusative of the disbeliever, meaning that none but he is to be questioned
34:18: 18 - And We made between them Sheba, which is in Yemen and between the towns which We had blessed with water and trees, which are the towns of Ash-Sham to which they travel for trade towns which are visible continuous from Yemen to Ash-Sham and We measured the journey between them so that they would rest in one and spend the night in another until the end of their journey and they would not need to carry provisions and water in it, meaning, We said Travel therein nights and days in security not fearing at night or during the day.
34:19: 19 - And they said, Our Lord, separate (and in another reading, send (between our journeys" to the Levant, make them deserts so that they may outstrip the poor by riding camels and carrying provisions and water, so they were arrogant about the blessing and wronged themselves by disbelief and We made them stories for those who came after them in that and We dispersed them completely We scattered them throughout the lands completely there are certainly signs in that mentioned for everyone patient from sins grateful for the blessings.
34:20: 20 - And Satan believed with the emphasis and the lightening upon them meaning the disbelievers among them, Saba in his assumption that they would follow him because of his deception so they followed him so he believed with the emphasis in his assumption, meaning he found him truthful except with the meaning of but a group of believers for clarification, meaning they are the believers who did not follow him
34:21: 21 - And he had no authority over them authority except that We might know knowledge of the appearance who believes in the Hereafter from him who is in doubt about it so that We might punish each of them and your Lord is, over all things, Guardian Watcher.
34:22: 22 - Say O Muhammad to the infidels of Mecca Call upon those whom you claim that is, you claim them to be gods besides God other than Him, so that they may benefit you according to your claim. God Almighty said about them They do not possess an atom’s weight of good or evil in the heavens or on the earth, and they have no partner in either of them partnership nor does He Almighty have (among them of the gods any supporter a helper.
34:23: 23 - And no intercession will avail with Him The Most High responded to their saying that their gods intercede with Him except for him whom He permits with the opening of the hamza and its closing until, when fear is cast from their hearts He removes the fear from them by permitting it they said some of them said to each other in good tidings What did your Lord say about it they said the word the truth meaning He has permitted it and He is the Most High above His creation by force the Great the Mighty
34:24: 24 - (Say: Who provides for you from the heavens) the rain and the earth the plants? (Say: God) if they do not say it there is no answer other than it and either we or you meaning one of the two groups is upon guidance or in manifest error He made clear in the ambiguity, being gentle with them, calling them to faith if they are guided to it.
34:25: 25 - (Say: You will not be asked about what we committed) our sin nor will we be asked about what you do because we are innocent of you.
34:26: 26 - (Say: Our Lord will gather us together) on the Day of Resurrection then He will judge between us with truth) so that those who are right will enter Paradise and those who are wrong will enter the Fire and He is the Opener the Judge the Knower of what He judges.
34:27: 27 - (Say: Show me) inform me those whom you have associated with Him in worship No! No! No! No! Rather, He is God, the Exalted in Might the Supreme in His affair the Wise in His management of His creation, so He has no partner in His kingdom.
34:28: 28 - And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings to mankind giving good tidings to the believers of Paradise and a warner warning the disbelievers of the punishment but most of the people meaning the disbelievers of Mecca do not know that.
34:29: 29 - And they say, When will this promise come to pass? of punishment if you should be truthful about it?
34:30: 30 - (Say: You have an appointment on a Day from which you will not delay an hour, nor will you advance it) and it is the Day of Resurrection.
34:31: 31 - And those who disbelieved from the people of Mecca said We will not believe in this Qur’an nor in that which was before it that is, before it, such as the Torah and the Gospel, which indicate the resurrection, because they denied it. God Almighty said about them And if you could but see O Muhammad when the wrongdoers the disbelievers (will be made to stand before their Lord, referring to each other’s statement. Those who were oppressed) the followers will say to those who were arrogant the leaders If it were not for you you turned us away from belief we would have been believers in the Prophet
34:32: 32 - Those who were arrogant said to those who were oppressed, Did we avert you from guidance after it had come to you? No, but you were criminals within yourselves.
34:33: 33 - And those who were oppressed said to those who were arrogant, Rather, it was the plot of the night and the day meaning, a plot in both of them from you against us when you ordered us to disbelieve in God and attribute to Him equals partners and they concealed meaning both groups regret for abandoning belief in Him when they saw the punishment meaning, each concealed it from his companion for fear of interpretation and We placed shackles on the necks of those who disbelieved in the Fire are they only recompensed for what they used to do) in this world
34:34: 34 - And We did not send to any city a warner but its affluent people said, Indeed, we, in that with which you have been sent, are disbelievers.
34:35: 35 - And they said, We have more wealth and children than those who believed, and we will not be punished.
34:36: 36 - (Say: Verily, my Lord enlarges provision) He expands it for whomever He wills as a test and restricts it He restricts it for whomever He wills as a trial but most of the people that is, the disbelievers of Mecca do not know that.
34:37: 37 - And it is not your wealth or your children that bring you near to Us nearness, that is, drawing near except but whoever believes and does righteousness - for those will be a double reward for what they did that is, the reward for a good deed, for example, ten or more and they will be in the chambers of Paradise secure from death and other things. And in the reading of the chamber it means the plural.
34:38: 38 - And those who strive against Our verses the Qur’an to invalidate our verses by believing that we are unable to do so and that they will escape us those will be brought to the punishment
34:39: 39 - (Say: Verily, my Lord expands provision) He expands it for whomever He wills of His servants as a test and restricts it He restricts it for him after the expansion or for whomever He wills to test and whatever you spend in good (He will replace it. And He is the Best of providers) It is said that every person provides for his family, that is, with God’s provision.
34:40: 40 - And mention and the Day He will gather them all together meaning the polytheists then He will say to the angels, Was it these you by realizing the two hamzas and replacing the first with a ya’ and dropping it they used to worship
34:41: 41 - (They said: Glory be to You) in purity of You from having any partner You are our protector rather than them meaning there is no allegiance between us and them from our side Rather for the transition they used to worship the jinn the devils, meaning they obeyed them in their worship of us Most of them are believers in them believing in what they say to them
34:42: 42 - So today none of you will have power over others meaning some of the worshipped ones for some of the worshippers of benefit intercession or harm torment and We will say to those who did wrong who disbelieved Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny
34:43: 43 - And when Our verses are recited to them the Qur’an as clear proofs through the tongue of our Prophet Muhammad, may God bless him and grant him peace, (they say, This is not but a man who wants to avert you from what your fathers used to worship of idols. 41:11: 11 - Then He directed Himself He intended to the heaven while it was smoke rising steam and said to it and to the earth, Come both of you to what I want from you willingly or by compulsion in the place of the state, meaning willingly or by compulsion they said, We have come with those among us willingly in it is the predominance of the rational male or they descended to address them in his place
41:12: 12 - And He ordained them The pronoun refers to the heaven because it is in the sense of the collective that returns to Him, that is, He made them seven heavens in two days Thursday and Friday. He finished them in the last hour of it, and in them He created Adam. Therefore, He did not say equal here, and what is here is in agreement with the verses of the creation of the heavens and the earth in six days. And He inspired in each heaven its command with which He commanded those in it of obedience and worship. And We adorned the lowest heaven with lamps with stars. And as a safeguard in the accusative case with its implied verb, that is, We preserved it from the devils’ eavesdropping with meteors. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might in His kingdom. The Knowing of His creation.

They fought The plural is in view of the meaning, because each party is a group, and it was read that they fought. (Then make peace between them) The dual is in view of the wording. But if one of them transgresses transgresses against the other, then fight against the one that transgresses until it returns returns to the command of God the truth. (But if it returns, then make peace between them with justice) with fairness and act justly be just. For God loves those who act justly.
