Ridicule, contempt, and disdain a people meaning men from among you of a people who may be better than them in the sight of God nor women from among you (of women who may be better than them. And do not insult one another) do not find fault with one another and do not call one another by nicknames do not call one another by nicknames that they dislike, including O wicked one and O disbeliever. Evil is the name mentioned of ridicule, slander, and name-calling. Wickedness after faith is a substitute for the name, as it indicates that it is wickedness, as it is usually repeated. And whoever does not repent from that then those are the wrongdoers.
49:12: 12 - (O you who have believed, avoid much [negative] assumption. Indeed, some assumptions are sinful.) That is, they are sinful, and they are common, such as thinking ill of the good people among the believers, and they are common, unlike the wicked among them, so there is no sin in them in what appears from them. And do not spy. One of the two ta’s was omitted from it. Do not search for the faults and shortcomings of the Muslims by searching for them. And do not backbite one another. Do not mention something that he dislikes, even if it is in it. Would one of you like to eat the flesh of his brother when dead? with the emphasis and with the stress, that is, it is not good for him. You would detest it. That is, backbiting him during his life is like eating his flesh after his death. The second was presented to you, and you disliked it, so dislike the first. And fear God. His punishment for backbiting by repenting from it. Indeed, God is Forgiving. Accepts the repentance of the repentant. And Merciful. with them.

60:1: 1 - O you who have believed, do not take My enemies and your enemies meaning the disbelievers of Mecca as allies, receiving communicating to them the intention of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, to invade them, which he confided in you and concealed with Hunayn with affection between you and them. Hatib bin Abi Balta’ah wrote a letter to them regarding this because he had polytheistic children and relatives with them, so the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, took him back from those who had sent him with him, by God Almighty informing him of this and accepting Hatib’s excuse regarding it and they have disbelieved in what came to you of the truth meaning the religion of Islam and the Qur’an and they expel the Messenger and you from Mecca by making things difficult for you believe meaning because you believed in God, your Lord, if you have gone out for jihad for jihad in My cause and seeking My pleasure and the answer to the condition is indicated by what preceded it, meaning do not take them as allies (and secretly towards them with affection, while I know what you conceal and what you declare. And whoever among you does it) meaning secretly tells them about the Prophet has certainly strayed from the right path He strayed from the path of guidance and righteousness, and he went to the middle path.

He will judge) in the passive voice (between you and them, so you will be in Paradise and they will be among the disbelievers in Hell. (And God is Seeing of what you do.)
60:4: 4 - There has already been for you an excellent example with the hamza broken and damma in both places, as an example in Abraham that is, in him in word and deed and those with him of the believers when they said to their people, Indeed, we are disassociated plural of bari’ like dharif (of you and of what you worship other than God. We have rejected you) we have denied you and there has appeared between us and you animosity and hatred forever with the realization of the two hamzas and the replacement of the second with a waw until you believe in God alone, except for the saying of Abraham to his father, I will surely ask forgiveness for you is an exception from the example, so you are not to follow his example in that by asking forgiveness for the disbelievers. And his saying and I do not possess for you from God that is, from His punishment and reward of anything is a metaphor for the fact that he does not possess for him anything other than seeking forgiveness, so it is based on it, an exception in terms of what is intended from it, even though it is from its appearance something in which one can be imitated. Say, Then who possesses for you anything from God? And his seeking forgiveness for him before it becomes clear to him that he is an enemy of God, as He mentioned in Innocence Our Lord, in You we have put our trust, and to You we have turned, and to You is the final destination from the transmission of Al-Khalil and those with him, meaning they said:

Indeed, You are the Exalted in Might, the Wise) in Your kingdom and Your creation.

60:8: 8 - God does not forbid you from those who do not fight you of the infidels because of religion and do not expel you from your homes - from being righteous toward them a substitute for inclusion from those and acting justly you judge towards them with justice, that is, with fairness. This is how the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, made them swear (God knows best about their faith. But if you ascertain that they are believers, then do not return them to the disbelievers. They are not lawful for them, nor are they lawful for them. And give them) that is, give the disbelievers their husbands what they spent on them of dowries and there is no blame upon you if you marry them on the condition when you have given them their due compensation their dowries and do not hold on with emphasis and with emphasis to the marriage bonds of disbelieving women your wives, in order to sever your Islam to them on the condition, or those who join the polytheists as apostates, in order to sever your Islam to them on the condition. 60:11: 11 - And if any of your wives have passed away to you meaning one or more of them or any of their dowries due to them going to the disbelievers as apostates and you punish them so you raid and take spoils then give those whose wives have passed away from the spoils the equivalent of what they spent due to it being lost to them due to the disbelievers and fear God in whom you are believers and the believers did what they were commanded to do of giving to the disbelievers and the believers, then this ruling was lifted.

61:1: 1 - Glorify God, whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth meaning glorify Him, so the lam is an additional letter, and what is less than that is brought to make the majority stronger and He is the Exalted in Might in His kingdom the Wise in His creation.

61:13: 13 - And He will give you a blessing (and another which you love: victory from God and an imminent conquest. And give good tidings to the believers) of victory and conquest.

He said (Jesus, son of Mary, to the disciples: Who are my supporters to God?) That is, from the supporters who will be with me, heading to support God. (The disciples said: We are supporters of God.) The disciples are the chosen ones of Jesus and they were the first to believe in him and they were twelve men of the pure whiteness. It was said that they were short people who bleached clothes, that is, they whitened them. So a party of the Children of Israel believed in Jesus and said that he was the servant of God who was raised to the heavens. And a party disbelieved because they said that he was the son of God who was raised to Him. So the two parties fought. So We supported We strengthened those who believed from the two parties over their enemy the disbelieving party. And they became dominant victorious.

66:1: 1 - O Prophet, why do you forbid what God has made lawful to you from your female slave, Maria the Copt, when he had intercourse with her in the house of Hafsa and she was absent, then she came and it was difficult for her to do that in her house and on her bed, as you said, It is forbidden to me seek by forbidding her the pleasure of your wives meaning their pleasure and God is Forgiving and Merciful He forgave you this prohibition.

66:4: 4 - If you both repent meaning Hafsah and Aishah to God, your hearts have already inclined to forbid Maria, meaning that it pleased you both despite the Prophet’s (peace and blessings of God be upon him) disliking it, and that is a sin. He used the word hearts to mean two hearts, but did not express it because of the difficulty of combining two duals in what is like a single word. The damma was changed to a kasra to match the yaa.

He knows that) lightened from the heavy, meaning that there will be among you sick and others traveling throughout the land traveling seek[ing] from the bounty of God they seek from And others fight in the cause of God. And each of the three groups finds it difficult what was mentioned in the night prayer, so He made it easy for them by performing what is easy of it. Then that was abrogated by the five daily prayers. And recite what is easy of it, as mentioned above, and establish prayer, the obligatory ones, and give zakah and loan God, by spending what is not obligatory of money in the way of goodness, a good loan, with a good heart. And whatever good you send forth for yourselves - you will find it with God. It is better, than what you left behind, and it is an excess. And what comes after it, even if it is not a definite noun, resembles it because it is prevented from being definite, and greater in reward. And seek forgiveness of God. Indeed, God is Forgiving and Merciful, to the believers.

106:4: 4 - Who fed them against hunger that is, for his sake and made them safe from fear that is, for his sake. Hunger would afflict them due to the lack of crops in Mecca, and they feared the elephant army. 1 - Have you seen the one who denies the Judgment the recompense and the reckoning? That is, have you recognized him even if you do not know him? 2 - That is the report after the letter fa who repels the orphan that is, violently pushes him away from his right 3 - And he does not urge himself or others to feed the poor meaning to feed him. It was revealed about Al-Aas Ibn Wa’il or Al-Walid Ibn Al-Mughirah. 4 - Woe to the worshippers 5 - Those who are heedless of their prayers are negligent and delay them beyond their appointed time. 6 - Those who show off in prayer and other things 7 - And they prevent utensils such as needles, axes, pots, and bowls.
108:1: 1 - Indeed, We have given you O Muhammad Al-Kawthar is a river in Paradise, which is his basin, and his nation will come to it. Al-Kawthar is the abundant good of prophecy, the Qur’an, intercession, and the like.
108:2: 2 - Pray for your Lord the Eid al-Adha prayer and sacrifice your sacrifice
108:3: 3 - Indeed, your enemy meaning the one who hates you is the one cut off cut off from all good or the one whose lineage is cut off. It was revealed about Al-Aas bin Wa’il. The Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, called him cut off when his son Al-Qasim died.
109:2: 2 - I do not worship immediately what you worship of idols
109:3: 3 - Nor do you worship in the present what I worship which is God Almighty alone
109:4: 4 - Nor am I a worshipper in the reception what you worship
109:5: 5 - And you do not worship in the future what I worship God knows from them that they do not believe, and the application of what to God is in the form of a contrast
109:6: 6 - For you is your religion polytheism and for me is my religion Islam. This was before he was ordered to go to war. The seven reciters omitted the ya’ of addition in both pause and connection, and Ya’qub confirmed it in both cases.
110:1: 1 - When the victory of God comes to His Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, over his enemies and the conquest the conquest of Mecca
110:2: 2 - And you saw the people entering into the religion of God meaning Islam in droves groups after they had previously entered it one by one, and that was after the conquest of Mecca. The Arabs came to him from all corners of the earth, obediently.
110:3: 3 - So glorify the praises of your Lord meaning, be immersed in His praise (and seek His forgiveness. Indeed, He is the Accepter of repentance). And after the revelation of this surah, may God bless him and grant him peace, he would often say: Glory be to God and His praise, and I seek God’s forgiveness and I repent to Him. And he knew from it that his time had drawn near. The conquest of Mecca was in Ramadan of the year eight, and he died, may God bless him and grant him peace, in Rabi’ al-Awwal of the year ten.
111:1: 1 - And perish he lost the hands of Abu Lahab meaning his whole body. And he expressed it with the hands metaphorically because most actions are performed with them. And this sentence is a supplication. And perish he lost. And this is report like their saying God destroyed him, and he perished. And when the Prophet threatened him with punishment, he said, If what my nephew says is true, then I will ransom myself from him with my money and my children. He descended.
111:2: 2 - His wealth and what he earned will not avail him meaning his earnings, meaning his children, will not enrich him in the sense of enriches.
111:3: 3 - He will burn in a fire of flame meaning it will burn and ignite, and this is the meaning of his nickname, It will burn his face with radiance and redness.
111:4: 4 - And his wife is in apposition to the pronoun prays, and the separation is permitted by the object and its description, which is Umm Jameel carrier in the nominative and accusative firewood thorns and sa’dan, which she throws in the path of the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace.
111:5: 5 - Around her neck her neck is a rope of palm fibre, and this sentence is a state of the firewood carrier, which is an attribute of his wife or the predicate of an implied subject.
112:1: 1 - Say, He is God, the One God is the predicate of He, and One is a substitute for it or a second predicate.
112:2: 2 - God, the Eternal Refuge subject and predicate, meaning the one who is always sought in needs
112:3: 3 - He did not give birth because of the lack of similarity to Him and He was not born because of the lack of occurrence from Him
112:4: 4 - And there is none comparable to Him meaning equal or similar. And for is related to comparable and was presented before it because it is the focus of the negation. And one was delayed, and it is a noun that is different from its predicate, in order to take into account the comma.
113:1: 1 - (Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of the daybreak) Al-Fajr
113:2: 2 - From the evil of what He created of animals with and without obligations, and inanimate objects such as poison and others.
113:3: 3 - And from the evil of darkness when it comes meaning the night when it darkens or the moon when it sets
113:4: 4 - And from the evil of the blowers the witches who blow into the knots that they tie on the thread, blowing into them with something they say without saliva. Al-Zamakhshari said with him like the daughters of Labid mentioned above.
113:5: 5 - And from the evil of the envier when he envies He showed his envy and acted according to it, like the aforementioned Kalbaid from the Jews who envied the Prophet, may God bless him and grant him peace, and he mentioned the three that included what was created after him due to the severity of their evil.
114:1: 1 - (Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of mankind) their Creator and Owner. They were specifically mentioned to honor them and to seek refuge from the evil of the whisperer in their hearts.
114:3: 3 - God of mankind with two substitutes, two attributes, or two explanatory appositions, and the complement to them is made clearer for the sake of clarification
114:4: 4 - From the evil of the whisperer Satan is called the event because of his frequent association with it the withdrawing one because he withdraws and is late from the heart whenever God is mentioned.
114:5: 5 - Who whispers in the hearts of people their hearts if they forget the remembrance of God
